name: Zoom Workplace
pros_raw: Lo que más me gusta de Zoom es que me permite llevar adelante mi negocio de forma completa. En principio, es una herramienta fácil de usar. El fondo de mi habitación no es tan agradable, pero gracias a Zoom puedo colocar un fondo falso que me permite realizar entrevistas sin importar cómo luzca mi casa. Algo que me encanta es que me permite grabar, por lo que puedo llevar adelante conferencias, masterclass y entrevistas con mis clientes y luego transcribirlas gracias a su herramienta de transcripción inteligente. Lo uso con muchísima frecuencia, casi a diario.

Zoom has an intuitive interface, which does not limit the users' level of knowledge to start using it. In a work team that is not familiar with the use of video calls, Zoom allows for easy implementation. It is available for phones, tablets, computers with Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android operating systems. I can schedule meetings in advance and send invitations to participants' company or external emails. As a host, I have control over who can share their screen. If I want to have a conversation with only one participant, I can keep the others in the room. It allows me to share the full screen or just a specific application, which facilitates the presentation of ideas, data, or documents. With the help of the chat, additional communication is allowed during meetings. It allows me to record meetings to review them later or share them with participants who could not attend.

I love how easy it is to use! The integration with my calendar makes it so easy to schedule any meeting, and I use it at least twice a day, if not more.

Setting up the account took no time at all; it's been very easily implemented by our entire team.

I can send a Zoom link to people who don't have an account and they can still join the meeting. The recording feature has made it very easy to review my meetings, ensuring I don't miss anything.

It is a versatile and security enabled platform for seamless communication at work places. The  best part about this application is its availability on different devices, be it phone, tablet or computer. The application is very light and just takes a few minutes to load and all the devices get synchronized very easily

Overall Zoom is easy to use. I mainly use it for conducting video calls to onboard clients. I like the chat and share screen features. Those are my 2 most used features and I find them easy to use, even for non tech savvy users. I use zoom daily and use it for all of my meetings. I find the overall video and audio quality to be good. Have never had an issue!

As a frequent user of Zoom for workplace communication, I find it to be an invaluable tool for keeping teams connected, especially in a hybrid or remote work environment. The video conferencing feature is incredibly reliable, offering high-quality video and audio even with larger groups, which is essential for our team meetings and client presentations. I also appreciate the screen sharing and whiteboard functions, which make it easy to collaborate in real-time on design mockups and marketing strategies.
Making the decision to leave Zoom is my favorite thing about it.

I enjoy how easy it is to integrate Zoom into both our IDP, Email provider and multiple other applications. It makes it so easy to not only work in an easy flow but even better to automate the application for our team members

Zoom is reliable in all use situations. App on broadband at the home office. Check. App on mobile devices on mobile broadband. Check. App on Wi-Fi at a coffee shop. Check. The reliability, ease of use, Google Calendar plug in all are both easy to use and super efficient. Now with Granola, not only do I get the Zoom AI notes, but but I'm able to get my notes taken by Granola too, and then do more with them,

Easy to use and integrates seamlessly into daily workflows

Efficient communication through video calls, chat, and file sharing

Reliable with high-quality video and audio
Ideal for meetings, webinars, and virtual events

Ability to record meetings for later access adds value

1. Facilidade de Uso: A interface intuitiva torna o Zoom Workplace fácil de usar, mesmo para quem não tem muita experiência com tecnologia.


2. Integração: Ele se integra bem com outras ferramentas, como Slack, Google Workspace e Microsoft Office.


3. Qualidade de Chamadas: Áudio e vídeo de alta qualidade, mesmo em conexões de internet menos estáveis.


4. Recursos Avançados: Inclui funcionalidades como gravação de reuniões, transcrições automáticas e compartilhamento de tela interativo.


5. Colaboração em Equipe: Oferece ferramentas como quadros brancos digitais e salas de reunião em grupo para discussões privadas.

What I love most about Zoom is how it brings people closer, no matter how far apart they are. I appreciate how it makes communication so easy, whether it’s for work or connecting with family and friends. For me, Zoom sometimes feels like a small window to the world, where we can share ideas, learn new things, and build stronger relationships.

I have always found it very user-friendly. Zoom introduced me to my first online meeting and client coaching session. It was very easy to set up. I also like that I can easily acess all my meetings, past, current and future with the click of a button. Editing my meetings is also straight forward - and you can edit an entire recurring meetings at once. It is also easy to access recordings of my meetings. There are a lot of tools and capabilities that are available and make my meetings go smoothly. I also like that there is no limit to the number of times I use the platform. It is also easily integrated with other apps I work with - and I do not any IT specialist to help me with that. The customer support is always great.

I've used many different platforms for video calling, and Zoom has hands-down been the best option for me for weekly video calls. It's universal and easy for anyone to use, even if they do not have the Zoom app. Connections are always clear and seamless, and it's extremely easy to present from this platform. The interface is user-friendly and intiutive.

Zoom is great for sharing screens, recording, taking notes, AI summaries, and real-time chat. It also integrates easily with Google Calendar and my contacts. I use this multiple times a week without any issues. It requires occasional system updates, but those are completely within minutes. It's easy to install on devices. I've used Zoom regularly for 3 years no and haven't had to contact customer support for any reason so far. Great tool.
Its wide variety of features related to communication and collaboration tools on a single interface which is very easy to navigate and really super effective by its nature.

Zoom workplace is best in most of the things that is real time chat, employee engagement, communication, hybrid and in person meetings.
It also has virtual whiteboards for detailed explanations for meeting and screen sharing option is also best. Collaborative notes for teams is also one of the best feature.
It's easy to use, and allows multiple users to join and add their own meetings. I like that you can control users who join, and allow them to speak or not, or show their cameras or not.

The best aspects of Zoom Workplace often highlighted by users include its ease of use, high-quality video and audio clarity, and integration with popular tools like Slack and Outlook. Users love the Instant Meeting feature, which allows quick and seamless collaboration, making it highly efficient for remote work settings. Additionally, its real-time collaboration tools such as file sharing, video conferencing, and chat functions are praised for enhancing productivity

What I like most about Zoom Workplace is that it is easy to use and reliable during meetings. The screen sharing feature is very useful when reviewing AutoCAD, Civil 3D, and Revit drawings. This allows for real-time collaboration between everyone involved. The ability to annotate directly on shared screens further improves team communication, making it easier to discuss project details and provide feedback to participants.

I love using Zoom for calls. It lets us break into this smaller breakout rooms or sessions within an ongoing meeting that lets us have the private conversation in the middle of the meeting, and then re-group. It integrates well with the entire environment.

Undeniably effective for seamless joining of meetings and scheduling.
Spectacularly crucial app for smooth communication.
Splendidly ideal for team calls and webinars.
Dreadful with discussions and activities for small group by breakout rooms.
Versatile and robust with presentations and demonstrations.
Exceptional with virtual backgrounds for fun with meetings.

I really appreciate the fact that I do not have to worry about data integrity, and I love how easy it is to use. Writing better, easier documents is a direct result of great online collaboration. With Zoom Workplace, you can not only store files and documents in the cloud, but you can also have online video chats. The flexibility to create forms and surveys, set up a calendar for meetings, and most importantly, integrate with any platform or software are all features that I really like.

The stability of the programming makes Zoom the premiere online video conferencing programme. For educators, this especially is useful with the creation of breakout rooms and for hosting webinars. Zoom deserves all the credit for immediately removing the 40-minute time limit for teachers at the start of the Covid-19 Pandemic.

Zoom is a very powerful application with many features. Its great implementation team walked us through changing out our previous system. For the end user, it is very easy to use and can go from voice to video or chat. It can be multi-platform with Windows, Mac, Chromebook, Android, or iOS, so it is very versatile and fairly consistent across all platforms.

I like that with Zoom you can screen share, use breakout rooms, and chat, it helps when you are trying to assist someone on how something works. I like that you can do multiple things all in one call.

Zoom Workplace stands out for its seamless video conferencing, user-friendly interface, and reliable performance even with varying internet conditions. Its robust collaboration tools, such as screen sharing, breakout rooms, and chat integration, make teamwork effortless. The platform’s scalability and compatibility across devices ensure it meets the needs of both small teams and large organizations.

Best thing about Zoom are it's multiple features. Though is came out as an app for calls and video conferencing but the enhances features like task and project management , file sharing , third party integrations have made it one stop shop to use in everyday coporate life.

Zoom Workplace has several features that make it a popular choice for many users. The interface is user-friendly and intuitive, making it easy for people to navigate and use without much training. It integrates well with other tools and platforms, such as Slack, Microsoft Office, and Google Workspace, which helps streamline workflows. It offers robust security features, including end-to-end encryption, to ensure that meetings and data are protected. Whether you’re a small team or a large organization, Zoom can scale to meet your needs with various plans and features.

Zoom is user friendly and enables parties from all over the world to join, meet and discuss.  The ability to share your screen, present important demos and voice call with a large group is excellent.  In addition, I like that Zoom offers the most user friendly interface for the other attendees on the calls.  If one of the attendees needs to share their screen or face, Zoom works perfectly every time.  I have found other video conferencing apps struggle with this and the host is usually stuck trying to figure out how to allow for someone other than themselves to share their screen.

I like the UI, simple sweet and best. It is really easy to learn. It can be integrated with other tools like slack, teams, outlook, etc. I like the audio quality and microphone checks to ensure a smooth experience

What I like best about Zoom Workplace is its seamless integration of communication tools, which makes collaborating with my team effortless. The video conferencing quality is consistently reliable, even with large groups, and the addition of features like Zoom Whiteboard and Zoom Chat allows for real-time collaboration and idea-sharing. The platform is intuitive to use, whether for quick check-ins or larger virtual events, and its ability to integrate with other workplace tools streamlines our workflow, making it an indispensable part of our remote work setup.

I love the AI feature. It was always so irritating taking notes, but now with Zoom's AI feature - we zoom more and have AI do the work for our "meeting minutes." You can also with permissions, record phone calls, and is helpful for AI to listen in and take meetings while on the road.

Zoom does listen to their customers and modifies their service based on customer suggestions and complaints. They are wanting to continuously upgrade their product and it shows. We went with Zoom because they combined phone, video, email, team chat and scheduling all in one app that was significantly cheaper than what we were paying when we had those functions through seperate applications. The scheduling software has been life changing at our company. We suggested a modification that would be helpful for our company and they started working on it. Zoom has been user friendly and most of our team has picked it up very quickly despite not being technologically savvy. Our transition from Teams to Zoom was fairly easy as was our transition from DialPad to Zoom. If you already use a web based phone system, it should be really easy.
It works 99% of the time. I have no issues with Zoom per se, especially the new Zoom Workplace Business Plus with integrated Zoom phone.

The ease of use. Sending chats, starting groups, making phones calls, video meetings...it's all intuitive. Messages are clear, it's easy to do basic work tasks i.e. format, attach files, emphasize - and the management portal is helpful as well. Does not require  much training to use. The desktop app is updated often, it is easy to make organization-wide changes, and has a very friendly interface.

Brings all the communication services expected in a modern enterprise into one platform, and does an excellent job replacing desk-bound VOIP systems.

Video conferencing, meetings, and the like are top-notch as expected from such a popular platform that really found its footing during COVID. Beats the pants off of Teams, that's for sure.

Setting up the platform for your business isn't simple (no suprise there), but it is straightforward and easy to learn.

Zoom offers a ton of different features including video chatting, text chatting, live whiteboards, and much more. Its so easy to use we never have any issues to deal with coming from our end users. Its super easy to implement too as there are installers for every device. We use it every day and in the off chance we need support their customer support team is always quick to respond. It also easily integrates with any current system.

As a global company, staying connected across different time zones and locations is critical for us, and Zoom Workplace has been a game-changer. We rely on Zoom daily for meetings, and the platform makes it incredibly easy to keep everyone in the loop, no matter where they are. The integrated phone lines are a huge plus, allowing us to reach team members or clients directly without needing separate tools. The ability to host and manage meetings effortlessly has really helped us maintain strong communication and collaboration across our global teams.
Zoom provides a simple end-user experience that anyone can use from just about any electronic equipment: computer, laptop, handheld or phone.

I love that it is very user-friendly, options and settings are easy to find and it's as customizable as it gets, especially for meeting hosts. Most tools can be set up effortlessly using the app itself, and the advanced settings are also easily found on its web page.

Intuitive interface makes navigation simple, Offers diverse tools, including HD video conferencing and breakout rooms, seamlessly connects with applications like Google Workspace and Microsoft Office, Responsive support team and a comprehensive knowledge base and Enhanced collaboration through regular meetings and messaging.

Zoom Workplace, or Zoom's suite of tools for business communication, has a lot going for it. What stands out to me is its ability to seamlessly integrate video conferencing with team collaboration tools. The combination of video calls, chat, and file sharing in one platform makes it really convenient for teams to stay connected and organized. The ease of setting up meetings and the flexibility of its features, like breakout rooms and virtual backgrounds, also add a lot of value.

The most preferable and loved feature is Video calling and its stability in connections while on a call,  improving the feature to compete with the competitors which is the integration of AI-powered tools, easy to use, Very user friendly and even a low graduate can use.

we use this on a daily bases for team connects, Knowledge transfer and for conducting brwonbag sessions

My previous workplace used to use Google Meet, and compared to that, Zoom Workspace is a night and day difference. Firstly, the integrations and extensions store is beyond imagination. By properly using them, you can probably customize every aspect of the meeting. The new Zoom AI keeps track of the meeting events and even takes notes for you. Recently Zoom got a pretty huge update that massively improved the UI. The backgrounds, image filters, and customized noise cancellation make it a breeze to have productive meetings.

OMG, I totally love Zoom Workplace because it's like super easy to connect with my friends and work on group projects! The video calls are so fun, and we can even share screens and stuff. Plus, the chat feature is awesome for quick updates and memes. It makes everything way more organized, and I can totally keep up with schoolwork without feeling stressed. It’s like having a virtual hangout and study session all in one!

I highly recommend Zoom for any team or individual looking for a robust and user-friendly video conferencing solution.

It has been a game-changer for our team's remote meetings and collaborations. The platform is incredibly easy to use, with a simple and intuitive interface. The video and audio quality are consistently high, even with large groups. I appreciate the features like screen sharing, chat, and virtual backgrounds.

Zoom is among one of the most useful virtual communication tool for remote work, online learning, and Personal contacts . It is perfect since it lets individual connect effortlessly from anywhere around the globe.
There are so many upsides of using zoom
1.collaboration ease
2. Tool based integration
3. Up to date features
4. Worldwide connectivity
5.communications are secure

It is very user-friendly! The integration with my calendar simplifies the scheduling of meetings. I can send a Zoom link to participants without accounts, allowing them to join effortlessly. The recording feature has been extremely helpful for reviewing meetings, ensuring I don’t overlook any important details.The real-time translation tools that have been added lately help me a lot in making meetings with customers from different countries.
cons_raw: Creo que me gustaría que pudiesen añadir más espacio disponible para las grabaciones en el plan inicial. Que sean solo 5 GB me parece bastante poco.

The free version of Zoom has some restrictions, such as the maximum duration of meetings being 40 minutes, the number of participants, and the lack of some advanced features. This can be limiting when having large teams.
There isn't anything to dislike. So far, I haven't had any issues and it's been a helpful tool for our whole team.
The basic version provides only a limited resources however the paid one has features like larger participant groups, unlimited meeting time etc. The caveat is that the paid one is very costly
Honestly, there is nothing that comes to mind that I dislike about Zoom. I find it to be the best product out there that I've used for video conferencing, etc. Keep up the good work.

On the downside, Zoom can sometimes be resource-heavy, especially on older devices, which causes occasional lag or crashes. Additionally, the security features—though improved—require a bit of extra setup to ensure all meetings are fully protected. Overall, Zoom Workplace is an excellent solution for improving communication and collaboration, allowing us to stay connected and productive no matter where we are.

Ever since Zoom began updating Workplace, especially this year (2024), the experience has steadily declined. They've added so many bells and whistles, yet none of them actually improve our experience with hosting virtual meetings for clients and team members. I was much happier with Zoom when it was simpler. Even though it's a premium video conferencing service, I had no problem paying for annual subscriptions—until this year. My renewal in June left me feeling, "It's time to break up; this relationship is over."

Unfortunately, I had already paid for the year, and Zoom doesn't offer refunds—not even a prorated one. When I contacted support, they wasted a lot of my time just trying to verify my account. I was already logged in and could provide any information they needed, but they insisted on my account number. It wasn’t where they said it would be, even after I sent screenshots of where I was looking. I probably spent 20 minutes just trying to prove I was the account owner.

So, goodbye, Zoom. I'm so frustrated that I'm happy to let the rest of my subscription go to waste. Honestly, I'm baffled by all the positive reviews.

What I am not a fan of is the the response time of the support team or various issues with the billing department. We have had to contact them a few times regarding features that we are missing/paying for.
The update process could be handled better. Nothing is worse than being ready to join a call, and the "auto update" happens. Of course that makes you late for the call

1. Custo: As versões avançadas podem ser caras para pequenas empresas ou indivíduos.


2. Segurança e Privacidade: Apesar de melhorias, questões relacionadas à privacidade em anos anteriores ainda são uma preocupação para alguns.


3. Dependência de Internet: Funcionalidade altamente dependente de conexões estáveis, o que pode ser um problema em áreas com infraestrutura limitada.


4. Aprendizado Inicial: Algumas empresas relataram dificuldade em adaptar equipes maiores ao uso eficiente de todas as funcionalidades disponíveis.


5. Consumo de Recursos: Pode consumir mais banda larga e recursos do dispositivo em comparação a algumas ferramentas concorrentes.

What I dislike about Zoom is that it sometimes makes communication feel too formal, even in moments when we need more spontaneity. Additionally, the overload of virtual meetings can feel exhausting at times, as they lack the warmth of in-person interactions, where we can read body language and connect on a deeper level.

The 40 minute curb on meetings for the Free Plan - it was easier to get my clients to sign up on Zoom and encourage them to remain on the same platform. This was easier when they had that 1 hour free for their own meetings.
I really don't have anything bad to say. I have had nothing but a good experience with using Zoom for business.
When sharing, the toolbar can get in the way or get lost on my screen
Nothing! I love all of the features available.

The free version of Zoom gives a 40-minute limit on group meetings with three or more participants. This restriction can be limiting for longer discussions, presentations, or virtual events, requiring users to upgrade to a paid plan for extended sessions.

Sometimes, you are not able to see recurring meetings on the desktop, or if they are on the desktop version, they are not on the online version. Also, sometimes, we don't get sound recorded even though it says "recording in progress."
I would like a system that moves our camera system when someone is speaking to automatically show the speaker instead of having one of our team members do this manually.

Connectivity Issues: Users often experience audio or video disruptions when the internet connection is weak, which can hinder meeting quality [❞] [❞].

Annoying Notifications: Sounds when someone joins late can interrupt presentations, and this notification can't easily be turned off [❞].

Echo and Delays: Occasionally, there's an echo or delay in the audio during meetings, which can be frustrating and difficult to troubleshoot [❞].

Time-Sensitive Meetings: Changing meeting times for those with technical issues isn't always easy, causing participants to miss scheduled meetings [❞].

What I don't like about Zoom Workplace is that sometimes, if the files to be shared are very large, such as Revit or Civil 3D 3D models, I can find that communication slows down and part of the idea I want to express to the work team gets lost. The resolution may not be sufficient to review specific details.
There is not much I dislike about it as I almost use it everyday.
Versatile and robust with virtual backgrounds for fun with meetings.

Although I've had a great time using Zoom Workplace so far, one feature that would be great is the ability to access files even when I don't have an Internet connection - just like with Google products.

The cost of Zoom makes it prohibitive for a regular educator or small company to be able to purchase. For a non-profit, the pricing of Zoom is out of reach, putting an undue burden on organisations that are trying to improve this world. The removal of the 40-minute time limit is laudable, but everyone knew that it could not last. The lack of a change in pricing structure, after the expected economic downturn and loss of jobs due to Covid-19 will prevent many from being able to access the full scale of Zoom possibilities. There is a LOT of lag when doing a presentation that has videos built into them through Zoom, I was no impressed at all by this.

As an Administrator the dashboard shows everything but it can be a little intimidating and not that straightforward.  We started out using the Polycom phones and had a lot of issues with them.  The customer support with Zoom was good but they could not help with the phones and kept referring us to Polycom tech support, which is terrible.  They would not help support their own equipment.  Since then we have swapped over to Yealink phones and it has been much better.

One reason I don't like Zoom is it has a lot of lag. There are multiple times when I am in a meeting and go to talk and it freezes or when I try and share my screen there are lots of pop-ups that make it difficult to work around. At the same time if you don't have the chat box open you won't be notified that someone sent a chat.

While Zoom Workplace offers excellent features, some users may find its cost relatively high for smaller teams or organizations. Occasional issues with audio or video quality in low-bandwidth situations can also be frustrating. Additionally, navigating advanced settings can feel overwhelming for new users, and stronger security measures might be desired for sensitive meetings.

It requires full subscription to use it's all  features which becomes difficult in case of some organizations. Also , Zoom workspace may require a bit time to get used to due to some new users. Hence , it might not be easy to use for some people.

While Zoom Workplace has many strengths, there are a few areas where users might find room for improvement:

Bandwidth Requirements: High-quality video and audio can consume a lot of bandwidth, which might be challenging for users with slower internet connections.
Security Concerns: Despite improvements, some users still worry about privacy and security, especially with the rise of “Zoombombing” incidents in the past.
Cost: For larger organizations or those needing advanced features, the cost can add up quickly.

Zoom does have some issues with connectivity. I have frequent microphone issues and have dropped a few calls along the way.  I am not a fan of the new waiting room feature.  This feature was auto-enabled and caused some delays for my guests in joining calls and meetings.  Once i adjusted my settings, this was no longer an issue, but it did cause a few delays at first.
The video quality the rendering time of the video is sometimes slow with a lot of people inside the meeting room, rest some giphy support can be implemented

One thing I dislike about Zoom Workplace is that it can sometimes feel overwhelming with the sheer number of features available, especially for new users. It can take some time to navigate and fully understand all the options, which can lead to a bit of a learning curve. Additionally, occasional updates can temporarily disrupt workflows as users adjust to new interfaces or features. While the platform is generally reliable, there have been instances where video quality fluctuates or connectivity issues arise, which can be frustrating during important meetings.

I don't like that sometimes it makes your mandatory upgrade. Usually, it works fine, and then right before you are about to hop on a call/zoom it upgrades and it may take a few seconds. Irritating if it happens when you are running late to a meeting.

Zoom could have better support once you are a customer. The support during our trial period before we actually purchased Zoom was amazing. After purchasing, they were unable to help us as much. We do still receive support and they are still a great company to work with, it just is something they could improve on.

The AI companion is half-baked. Some of the other features that are now in Zoom Workplace are pretty obtrusive and get in the way of just wanting to conduct a meeting or being on the phone. Support with Zoom has become a nightmare to use. I used to be able to quickly change billing, add or subtract services all within the Zoom.us portal, but now I have to go through some sort of billing support to get anything done, and between the chatbots not knowing what to do, waiting onhold with with support, the support site being down a lot, and getting trasnferred form one department to the next, really makes me think if there is something else out there (i.e., Teams, Slack) that would work jsut as well.

Regardless of the validity, Zoom is marketed in a negative way to the extent many companies have blocked Zoom entirely - presenting challenges when it comes to meeting with external partners. Personally, many of my partners opt for Teams, and are unable or unfamiliar to use Zoom. There are also significant connectivity issues, specific to Zoom, depending on my location. Customer support is a hit or miss.

The biggest issue, which is minor in the bigger scheme of things, is that users cannot record their own name for the phone directory. This is a big problem for people with names from tonal languages (and many more), which is disrespectful to the individuals. Users can record their own voicemail greetings, so it should be easy enough, but Zoom has been sleeping at the wheel on this one.

Customer support could also be better - they're very slow to get back to issues regarding usage, but pretty quick to address configuration or implementation problems.

For integration purposes, for multiple offices, the directory setup isn't as user friendly as it could be.
There's really nothing I could list about Zoom that I don't like. over the years they have gotten better and better.

We occasionally experience minor video or audio issues, especially during high-traffic times. Additionally, some of the advanced features, like large webinars, can become costly. However, these issues are relatively small considering how much Zoom has helped us stay connected and productive as a global team.

Many options are not visible, nor is it obvious how to find them. Different options are visible on different types of machines. Since the end-user experiences differs from machine to machine, an end-user on one type of machine cannot necessarily help an end-user on a different type of machine. Also, for break-out sessions, the invitation that is sent to the designated people is very short-lived and if a person doesn't move to a room in time, there is no way for the Host to get them into their break-out room so the participant really needs to quit the meeting then re-enter. Plus we would have to purchase an expensive phone number if we wanted everyone to have a dial-in option at no cost to them. And we are a small non-profit so this isn't a viable solution.
My only complaint would be the time it takes to convert and download recordings. I've used other similar programs, such as MS Teams and it seems to take a little less time there.

Initial setup can be challenging for larger organizations, Time restrictions on group meetings hinder effectiveness, Occasional Technical Issues, Connectivity problems can disrupt meetings, Some advanced tools require additional training.

A potential weakness of Zoom Workplace is its user interface, which at times can be crowded or daunting, particularly when loaded with too many features and integrations. It might take some time for users to get comfortable navigating everything well. Other times, internet speed may be slow leading to poor connectivity and performance in certain users’ systems. There are also security issues that arise as a result of having so much data shared on the platform.

Limited free version and limited features on free version,
Bandwidth issue

Meetings are capped at 40 minutes on the free version. The paid version is quite expensive even if you opt for the yearly payment discount. Although zoom workplace keeps track of all chats from recurring meetings, we have had instances where we lost those chats.

Ugh, honestly, one thing I really dislike about Zoom Workplace is when the connection is super laggy. It’s the worst when you’re trying to talk and everyone’s frozen! 😩 Also, sometimes the notifications get super overwhelming, and I feel like I'm missing important messages. Plus, it can be a little awkward when no one knows who should talk first. I wish there were more fun backgrounds too, like the ones we see on regular Zoom calls! Overall, it's cool, but it could definitely use some tweaks.

I've experienced some minor issues:

- Occasional connectivity drops or delays.
- Limited customization options for virtual backgrounds.
- Some friends have reported difficulties with calendar integration.
Security issues and technical issues are the main reasons overall everything is good
Nothing, I have very good experience with Zoom.

name: Speexx
pros_raw:
Speex offers amazing lessons, the Speak trainnings and the way the grammar is applied into the exercises.
The interface is well designed and easy to use, also the reminders help us to keep track and better evaluate our learning path.

Speexx has truly revolutionized the way organizations approach language training. As a corporate language learning platform, it combines advanced technology with a personalized approach that caters to individual learning needs.

One of the standout features of Speexx is its user-friendly interface. The platform is intuitive, making navigation seamless for users of all skill levels. The blend of live virtual classes, on-demand content, and interactive exercises keeps learners engaged and motivated.

What I particularly appreciate is the adaptive learning technology, which tailors the curriculum based on the learner's progress and preferences. This personalization ensures that each user receives the support they need to succeed, making the learning experience not only efficient but also enjoyable.

Moreover, the availability of experienced instructors adds significant value. The live sessions foster real-time interaction, allowing learners to practice speaking and receive immediate feedback, which is essential for language acquisition.

Overall, Speexx stands out as a comprehensive solution for language learning in a corporate environment. Its combination of flexibility, personalized content, and expert guidance makes it an excellent choice for organizations looking to enhance their employees' language skills. I highly recommend Speexx for anyone serious about effective language training.

The articles and videos are plenty if you want to get away from the exercises or theory for a while, and there is a good variety of subjects.

What I like most is the ability to access lessons anytime and from any device. The individual and group lessons cover a wide range of interesting topics, available at all hours. Additionally, the opportunity to record speaking and writing exercises, which are then corrected by the assigned tutor, is extremely helpful for improving skills.

The coaches are all very professional and have the ability to put me at ease: I was often "afraid" of making a mistake when speaking another language, this does not happen with Speexx. The platform is also user friendly and allows you to practice offline by monitoring the course, both for "at home" exercises and for sessions with the coach.
In addition to the above, the solution allows me to learn English without affecting my work thanks to the flexibility of the coaches.
The platform is used also for the final exam and the related certification.

While Speexx offers a range of valuable features for language learning, there are several areas where it could enhance the user experience.

One notable concern is the platform’s navigation. Although the interface is generally user-friendly, some users find certain sections a bit confusing. It can take time to locate specific resources or courses, which may disrupt the learning flow.

Another area for improvement is the availability of instructors. While there are experienced teachers, at times, scheduling conflicts can make it difficult to find live sessions that fit into a busy work schedule. This could lead to missed opportunities for real-time interaction and feedback.

Additionally, while the content is diverse, some learners feel that the materials can become repetitive. Introducing more varied and engaging activities could help maintain motivation and keep the learning experience fresh.

Lastly, the pricing structure might be a barrier for some organizations. While Speexx provides quality training, it may not be the most budget-friendly option for all companies.

In summary, while Speexx has a solid foundation for language learning, addressing these areas could significantly enhance the overall user experience and make it an even more compelling choice.

name: Echo360
pros_raw: Echo360 makes it extremely easy to create videos with and without the installable application. With the introduction of their Browser Based Capture, end users can easily use the same functionality of the downloaded application without needing to download anything. The best part of these recordings is that you have a super clear side by side recording. One full window of your screen share, and one full window of your camera capture. This is very useful for faculty and students who are sharing presentations. Echo360 also includes a very basic editor that is great for quick editing funcitons.

I'm using Echo 360in almost all training sessions. It allows me to keep the audience engaged, check their emotional response, verify the acquisition of knowledge and stimulate their problem-solving skills. It is easy to use (especially with the AI question-generator), completely and smoothly inegrated into the presentation, very intuitive to use and apply. Ask the audience to scan a QR code on the screen or monitor and your'e in business, live or online. Other features allow me to create groups for competitions, contests, etc. Reporting is simple, detailed and easy to analyze. Pefect for synchronous and asynchronous training.

I like that it is integrated into PowerPoint for seamless polling through a software application I'm using everyday anyway.  Once students have done there part the usage is very stable.  I also like that I can upload quizzes right after class in about 2 minutes.  One unexpected advantage of daily quizzes has been the ability to see who is struggling and have a pattern of performance (attendance, scores, etc.) when engaging in correction or intervention.  I use a few of the advanced features at times, but even just sticking with the basics has become a strength of the class.  The energy rises when the bar graph shows 50/50 on a true/false.  Sometimes I reveal a winner, sometimes I will quickly repoll and say "convince someone your right!" and more often than not the correct answer comes in at a higher percentage.

Customer support has generally been strong over my time using the program.  New student videos for how to register have come in timely manner.  In addition, I've literally sent problem session files and had experts help me out.  I've used it for many years and have never felt like I've been left without a solution.

I use PointSolutions for most all my class sessions. The ability to gather student feedback in real time is crucial to ensuring student understanding while fostering active engagement. I love how it integrates with Canvas allowing for quick grading. As someone who uses Peer Instruction for most of my sessions, Anywhere Polling is my product of choice after using many different options.

Echo360 is our chosen solution for lecture capture at our institution. It offers active learning features and analytics that the faculty find extremely helpful for delivery of asynchronous content, and synchronous classes equally use it for polling and review options. From an institutional perspective, Echo offers us an affordable cloud-based solution for video storage, which would otherwise not be feasible in our LMS. Additionally, we have had an excellent experience with support needs, as their support team has consistently addressed needs quickly, including feature requests.

Echo360 has been steadily improving their platform, devices, and offerings. This has lead to it being one of our primary media tools for the campus. The interface is fairly easy to use, and easy to integrate into LMS courses.

Although I am sure most people will talk about Echos many features here or their ease of use, but the thing that I like the most about echo is their customer support.  They are happy and eager to provide live and/or online training when needed.  And if there are any problems, I can always receive immediate help and support.
Echo360 is easy to use. Once the system is installed and implemented, there are relatively few issues. If an issues does arise, the support team is responsive and extremely helpful.
The UX is very seamless. Use it everytime to revise my lectures. Very easily integrated with Canvas LMS
Embedded Polls, Unlimited space, and Ease of use
Reliability of lecture capture and customer service.
Recording and storing the videos. Also, the editing with various features is one of the cool things. Sharing and integrating ability makes this software more usable.

There are many great things that are in echo 360. The way it allows to find files, volume, speed option and picture in picture options. If you want to listen at slow speed you can just reduce the speed of the video. If you want to go at high speed then you can just increase the speed to your desired speed.
The product works great for audience response and student engagement. Anyone can use the ppt. plugin to create questions ahead of time without a license. Their customer service has been great.

We like that we can use one program for all of the current programs. We like the versatility and the easy usage. We liked the technical team and the customer service that we are able to use all of the time. We love having feed back and input on future development.
Ease of use and customization of product

This easily tied into Powerpoint and offers multiple ways for the voters to provide their vote.
I like the automatic nature of the platform in that it's tethered to the start and end time of a class. Nothing needed for turning on or off.
Teachers use it to administer tests and quizzes. It helps prevent us students from cheating or cheating and makes data collection and analysis much smoother.
Ease of use for all ages. flexibility to participate by computer or phone. Flexibility in build options.
It has nice integration with Powerpoint, it can seem native given the way the toolbar works.

Well-thought out SaaS platform for higher ed.  Designed for higher ed.  Use of sprints with updates, and very receptive to product features.  Excellent support services.  Very stable platform.  Recording appliances are very stable.  Administered at the institution level, but flexible with sub-admins for colleges, departments, or units.  Good student app as well as integration with major LMSs.
It allows for professors to record lectures for students to watch later
Improves the engagement with the students or others, and helps provide instant feedback as to the instructor.
This app is super helpful to use when my colleagues and I are deciding on something and we need to poll.
