I love how customizable questions are, and I especially like the fact that it makes big university lecture halls feel more tight-knit. Students could learn from each other, and it helped me understand what areas were misunderstood across the class (i.e. when a large number of students missed the same questions.). Love that you can see real-time results immediately following a poll.
It does provide data analytics (tracking answers from clickers) which can be very helpful in course and individual performance

I did like the simplicity and ease of access that TurningPoint offers - my university uses TurningPoint quite often, so most students have the app already downloaded, and the platform is easily accessible.
Echo automates the recording and delivery process. The dashboards are easy to use and their LTIs work well.
I liked that it picked up sound clearly.

Echo360 maintain an aggressive roadmap, and for the most part deliver the goods. While their design decisions haven't pleased everyone (which is impossible to do) they have an impressive track record in getting it right, which gives me confidence.
The ease of use and ability to implement into a wide variety of circumstances.
A different way to interact with the staff attending an educational program
The ability to have your audience know that they can honestly provide anonymous feedback during a live presentation

I enjoyed using this technology in the classroom because it kept the students more engaged with the topic. They could respond easily to the questions in front of them and I felt it made them more confident and comfortable to answer questions.
Easy use. Turning Point is a great piece of technology that allows instructors to quickly assess student knowledge in large classes. Very easy software to use!
I like that the clickers are no longer mandatory and can be substituted with a phone.
It's very easy to make polling slides on PC or Mac.  My clients like the keypads vs attendees using their phones.
The live analytics for presentations and participant engagement and record maintenance.

We used Turning Point for years, but recently switched to iClicker (formerly called Reef Polling).  I liked the straightforward usability of Turning Point. Before smartphones became ubiquitous, their physical clickers were reliable and cheap. I liked the ability to integrate with our learning management system automatically.
I like that the clickers can be used in meetings without having to identify participants.
cons_raw: There is not much I have found to dislike with Echo360. My main issue would most likely be the licensing structure. Four our industry, it goes by number of active users, which includes users who have only logged in one time the entire year. It is a cumulative number that grows the first time each user logs in.
Nothing essential, but I'd like to keep animations created on PPT. I mjst say that ECHO 360 team has been very attentive and responsive to my suggestions.

I'm sure everyone has there wish list of additional features.  My current top three are to be able to set a session value instead of just a question value so I could consistently say that each daily quiz is 15 points.  Currently it depends on how many questions I ask or I need to manually adjust each question. Secondly, I wish it could scatterplot but that may be a PowerPoint limitation.  Finally, I wish it would tell me who is answering in the room vs. remote.  I do want sick students to stay home, but I'd like to be able to see any patters of concern.

I like that the desktop applications get an upgrade on a regular basis but this does mean that I need to coordinate the update with our tech team for our classroom tech carts. This can occasionally cause an issue if I don't plan ahead.

While it is beneficial to have more than one integration option for Echo360, some of them can be technically clunky, requiring multiple clicks and back end work, particularly when trying to use video across multiple semesters and having to clone sections. It can prove to be a steep learning curve for many faculty. The B2 to LTI needs have been somewhat slow, also.

Echo360 has gone through some hiccups with branding and roadmaps, making big (and ultimately positive) interface changes at inopportune times, or mentioning new features and tools to be launched with no real development timelines. This can make it a challenge to keep documentation up to date. Because Echo360 can do so many things, there are a lot of ways to accomplish the same task which can be confusing to convey to users. And the company has had several shifts in support/contact representatives in recent years which have caused minor issues with support times and getting timely information.
I do wish that echo would offer a feature that would allow both instructor and students to be live on cam for a more face-to-face classroom environment.
Sometimes, the CSV files that are uploaded have errors which can take some time to troubleshoot.
The video speed should exceed more than 2x.
Slow response time from support and technical glitches with new features.
Sometimes, trying to do too much. Less bells and whistles and more core functionality. Primary focus should be lecture capture and polling.
I like most of the features but still, I feel I do not know how to use a few features. I would like to see more friendly navigation.
It doesn't easily allow the user to download videos. The forward and backward are somewhat not accurate. You need to click on screen then move arrows to go to desired time.

The off-line login is cumbersome. There is a feature to remember your credentials for off-line logging in but it does not work. This is problematic when there is not access to WiFi to validate license credentials. The focus of the product offerings are on with higher education customers where some of cost is differed to the student and they can take advantage of on-line features and app. The only way to take advantage of most features is to require attendees or participants to download an app which works for internal employees and students but not so much with corporate customers, adult education, conferences, and tradeshow applications. We use the response cards which limits the features and options available.  Not a fan of using G2 to solicit feedback. There are other, less invasive feedback tools.
We did not like not having the ability to see as many screens of attendees, nor waiting for turning point and exam view integration but are promised that it is coming
Ability to use live video in presentation
There have been times where the software didn't properly save feedback.
I wish I was using the tool years ago.  It is a game changer.

We couldn't find an easy way to tie it into a totally virtual vote that met the parameters of our population.  This was really more our issue because our parameters are very complex.  It meets them for a live vote, just not the virtual vote.
It's very hard to edit the material carefully once the recording has been generated, without removing large portions of it-- in a fast / easy manner.

I think that the only problem I have found in the use of the software is the price. Many of us students have to pay the monthly paid version, but there are others who cannot afford it and that is a limitation.
Still a little clunky with starting/resetting for each question instead of resetting all questions at once.

The way the user is required to have a Turning account can be very onerous, licence management within an institution is a nightmare as the user is required to work through the company. I find some of the features to be pretty unstable.

Somewhat expensive, but given how we were managing servers with the on-premise solution, the SaaS is definitely better.  They expanded into audience response which was OK, but we use a different solution for Echo360 for that.
It doesn't cancel out background noise, so you can hear students rustling around
No live reporting on the current usage, and overall reporting.
I don't like how there are lots of bugs here and there, probably because it is lesser known.

The old ESS was allowed to get v buggy before the SaaS solution was released & the software’s accessibility features are unnecessarily expensive, so automated captions which are 80% accurate would cost my institution £30k alone.
The quality of the online content can depend on the IT setup that is installed and managed, but the software platform itself is fantastic.

The app often doesn’t stay logged in to the session if closed out and doesn’t remember user ID.
App can crash, freeze, or otherwise have extremely slow update speeds for new questions.
Sometimes the questions don’t appear on the user platform, which can make it difficult depending on setting of use.
Turning point does not show how much time is left to answer the question on the users screen

There is additional licensing, at a cost, that is required to use TurningPoint Cloud if you want to view/export participant results. Years ago that was not the case. Negative change for sure from the user end!!
You can use it without licensing to get aggregate results, but you are not able to export results or run any participant-based reports without either your Turning Account being licensed or every participant's Turning Account being licensed/registered.
There is no way to stop students from passing on clickers to other students to answer. There were sometimes technical issues with the software gathering results.
The process is somewhat clunky but we are using an old version of the software.  Upgrading to the current cloud version may resolve the interface and setup.

I really am not a fan of TurningPoint - while I liked how easy it is to access the platform, I found that it was fairly ineffective as a interactive platform, since there are far too few question types and not much room for creativity.  I also found that it was very difficult to use TurningPoint for attendance, since there's no way to link attendance outcomes in TurningPoint to the attendance grade system we use.

The first problem is that lecture recording does not have a way to alert the speaker if their microphone is not being picked up. Without audio the recording is useless. Once a decent recording is loaded there are very minimal editing options.
Nothing, everything seemed to work very well on my end.
The baseline product is a little expensive. The UI could be a little more mobile friendly.
Sometimes the software is faulty and does not work on every computer.
There are other more innovative ways to interact with attendees now (mobile phone vs handheld clicker)
There is nothing that I currently dislike
The set-up and training process took much longer than I would've liked.
Cost. To be able to use this, these do come at a pretty hefty cost. I am not sure to the actual numbers, but hopefully nobody is actually buying these outright, but their workplace will consider it.
Interface didn’t always update properly when students were trying to answer questions.

I found that TP doesn't always function correctly depending on the type of PowerPoint update.    TP will work great, but after a small Microsoft update to PowerPoint, TP will not display graphs correctly, or the clock animation doesn't work.  I use rental laptops, so I don't have control over the version of PowerPoint updates.   MOST of the time, the system works.
The number of steps required for set up is tedious but for integrations purposes its good.

Like many companies Turning Point has moved away from sales and toward leasing a license for their product. The cost for this license became too high for our students and we were forced to change over to Reef Polling, now called iClicker.
With the newest version, the clickers each need to be licensed, and the license only lasts a year. Otherwise, you cannot collect and analyze the responses in certain ways (i.e. per user).

name: iSpring Suite
pros_raw: Designing, creating e-learning courses usually evokes bad feelings when the name SCORM is dropped. And that's where the iSpring package comes to the rescue. A package, because it's ultimately more than one piece of software that allows you to turn a training idea into a professionally built course. It's worth mentioning that existing licenses allow institutions such as institutes and universities to make it available in a server version so as to optimize purchasing and its use. At the Military Medical Institute -State Research Institute, since iSpring began to be used, the creation of the course can be carried out by the Content Author, which makes the course itself as faithful as possible to the author's ideas.

iSpring Suite has made it so easy to create engaging quizzes and interactive learning sessions.  The software integrates with PowerPoint, so the learning curve was short and easy.  I don't think there's an easier product to learn when it comes to creating interactive lessons and quizzes.  Plus, the support from the company has been phenomenal.

iSpring Suite is easy to use right out of PowerPoint. I can add slides, quizes, videos, etc and have it imbedded in my presentation. There are a lot of characters that you can chose from to add a professional touch or a whimsical touch.  The premade slide formats are helpful and really do make it easy to follow a desired pathway to a presentation or creation of a course that will be added to iSpring Learn. I like also that they are continue to update. There are new slides and features that reflect a wide varieity of businesses.

The iSpring Suite is compatible with MS PowerPoint, which mean you can open PPT and use the Suite's many techniques to enhance trainings from right there. For example, I create asynchronous trainings for adults.  I want to both engage them in the learning process AND make sure that they have picked up the key learning points by the end of the training. For enagement, I often use iSpring's fun, ungraded quiz and survey features as well as the customizable storyboards for learners to practice various "what would you do" scenarios. To test their knowledge, I use iSpring's graded quiz feature to generaate various questions and let learners know if they have passed the training by the end.  The quiz results can also be emailed directly to me, so I am alerted when anyone passes or fails. This has been a game-changer for our trainings, which were previously just voiced-over slideshows with no interactions.

I’ve found iSpring Suite to be an incredibly useful tool, especially since it integrates directly with PowerPoint. This makes creating engaging training content so much easier because I don’t have to switch between different platforms. I love how it lets me add quizzes and interactive elements, plus the built-in library of stock characters and objects is a great bonus. Uploading courses to my LMS is straightforward, and the customization options make it simple to tailor the training exactly to my needs. It’s amazing how much functionality is packed into one tool—it really opens up so many possibilities for designing creative, interactive courses.

I love the ease of use that comes with iSpring Suite.  I am a training and development manager, and prior to ispring, all of our training was done completely live.  I purchased ispring suite to start builing e-learnings for my organization and it has worked great.  I really appreciate it being imbedded into Power Point, a tool I am already familiar with.  My biggest fear is that everything would look like a boring old power point presentation, but the tool is powerful and provides a lot of options for variety!

As the Training Director for a large aerospace facility, I only use iSpring Suite for all of our FAA required training. It allows me to create and send out training modules that are site-specific and gives me the ability to track all of the results. The software is easy to dowload, easy to use and has a number of features to help create detailed training courses. I've only really had one technical issue in my years of using this software and received amazing and prompt customer support that quickly got me past it. I can't recommend iSpring Suite highly enough.

iSpring is a fantastic tool for creating e-learning programs quickly and easily. It's super user-friendly and perfect for anyone, even if you don’t have advanced technical skills. You can upload content, customize it, and turn it into engaging courses in no time, thanks to its intuitive interface that doesn’t require a steep learning curve.

Multimedia Features:
Videos, images, audio, and interactive elements? No problem! iSpring integrates all kinds of media to help you create dynamic and interesting courses. Plus, if you want to add quizzes or tests, it has all the tools you need for that too.

Versatility:
The best part is that iSpring exports everything in formats like SCORM, xAPI, or HTML5 – perfect for use on Learning Management Systems. This means you can easily share your courses across different platforms.

E-Learning Features:
For creating interactive content, iSpring also offers tools for dialogue simulations and screen recordings. This is especially useful for software training or step-by-step guides.

Conclusion:
iSpring is a powerful, yet simple-to-use tool that helps you create professional e-learning programs quickly and efficiently. If you're looking for an easy solution to develop interactive learning content, iSpring is definitely worth checking out.

It's easy and fun to use, easy to navigate, if you don't know how to use a automation, interaction, quiz etc, the ''how to'' videos are extremely helpful.

Whenever I have contacted my account manager or customer service, they have been very responsive and solved any issues I've had.

iSpring has been a great addition to our learning & design toolkit! The learning curve is small and the customer service is great whenever I have questions. I also enjoy the frequent webinars and take advantage of attending to learn new things whenever possible.

It is easy to use and understand and the support team is always available to help you. I have used Ispring to make roleplays to educate users on how to understand different situations from different points of view.  This is my organisation's third year of using it. They have courses if you want to learn more. It is easy to use and learn intuitively if you know how to use Powerpoint.

I think the text to voice feature is a great way to quickly add richness to to the existing content. We were able to take a previously designed powerpoint file, then add in graphics, interactive elements, and voiceover in a very short time. The system us easy to use, and quick to learn.

iSpring Suite is ideally suited for quickly creating very attractive eLearning modules. Either as a short video module or as an interactive course. You can easily integrate avatars, nice backgrounds and voices for a great design and best experience. The personal design options for customizing the avatars are particularly practical.

I've been developing learning platforms for my classes with iSpring for 6 years and I love the features they offer. ISpring had come a long way in 6 years, and every upgrade to the service brings new and great changes/updates. If I ever have an issue with the product or a question, the iSpring help agents are extremely helpful. They are real people who actually care about the product they are selling. It is very apparent that the people who work at iSpring believe their product is the best, and that's because it probably is. If you are looking to launch an online school or online learning platform, iSpring is a must.

We've been in the process of converting many of our instructor-lead trainings to SCORM packages. In order to do that, we had to convert mayne powerpoints, some of which had 200 slides, into a whole new format. Ispirng being based on PowerPoint really is a deal breaker. Even though there are many different additionnal tools with Ispring suite max, most of them are PowerPoint based, so there really isn't that big of a learning curve. The other tools provided are fairly simple, the interactions are great too! Even though it seemed like just an add-on for PowerPoint, i do feel that i provides eveything we need in order for our leaners to have a great experience.

The tools within iSpring Suite are easy to use, and make it possible for a lot of interactivity within courses. The learning curve for the software was relatively short, and there are a lot of resources and help docs available to find what you are looking for. I really like that you can create custom characters, and the text to speech feature. Customer service has been quick and very helpful as I learned the software and navigating our LMS integration.

I like that you can use a client's PowerPoint presentation and add interactive features such as quizzes or drag-and-drop questions to reinforce the material. Using PowerPoint as the foundation also reduces the learning curve. Having SCORM support allows us to utilize our existing LMS system and upgrade the courses. I also like having the full-screen option in our SCORM packages, which assists those with visual issues with small text.

Customer support helped resolve issues I have encountered.

We were struggling with out eLearning platform to upload PPTs and PDFs. After a thorugh research we ended up using iSpring Suite. It has streamlined our SCORM content creation through MS PowerPoint, which is very easy to use. Now our end users enjoy the training material created with iSpring and also the Quiz creation is a smooth process.

There are many features of iSpring that are helpful. Of the most notable would be the closed captions feature in addition to the user friendly options to make training/learning material relevant and engaging for learners.

As long as you get the "Max" subscription, this product has everything you need to create training content for online format. You don't need to be an expert, as long as you know Powerpoint you can create what I call "glorified PowerPoints" which are essentially slides-based courses but you can add video, audio narration of any kind including text-to-speech, quizzes, interactions, and role plays. The thing I like best is that iSpring is constantly coming out with new features/improvements to make your life easier, such as adding to their content library (e.g. new illustrated characters and backgrounds). They PERSONALLY emailed me to ask what new features I  needed/wanted. I told them I needed audio attachable to each quiz question and answer choice (for language courses). And they DID IT. Now everyone can enjoy this new feature, and they never stop improving. Suddenly there is closed captioning (for videos) available; Suddenly the course player is available in Arabic; Suddenly there are beautiful new options for designing a Page; etc. etc. Customer service is fantastic and there is also a team dedicated to putting on professional webinars to develop your skills.

I love that I can use it for engaging content for students, technical content for teachers and compliance content for all staff.

It is also great that I can use the content in our LMS or one our intranet to deliver the content.

I love that it is continually evolving, listening to customer feedback. It's intuitive to use and has an ever growing suite of tools. If there's been a glitch, for example recently had trouble embedding a YouTube video, their customer support is exceptional. Worked with me to solve the problem, reviewing my file, sending link to download .... problem solved!

Video and Audio: iSpring Suite allows the incorporation of various multimedia elements, including video and audio, enhancing the interactivity and engagement of the courses.
Quizzes and Assessments: The tool supports the creation of diverse quiz types, from multiple-choice to drag-and-drop, enabling effective knowledge assessment.
PowerPoint Integration: Users familiar with PowerPoint will find iSpring Suite intuitive and easy to use.

As a trainer, I pay special attention to the preparation of e-learning, which begins with selecting the appropriate tools. Of course, there are free programs that allow you to create e-learning training, but they have much fewer functions than those offered by iSpring. Continuing, for the training to be interesting for its participants, it must constitute a logical whole and its content must be diverse and interesting. The use of graphics, multimedia and interactive elements not only positively affects the engagement of recipients, but also increases the acquisition of knowledge, and such solutions are found in the iSpring program.

One of the standout features of iSpring Suite is its deep integration with Microsoft PowerPoint. This allows users to convert existing PowerPoint presentations into interactive e-learning courses easily. This feature is especially appreciated because it leverages a familiar tool.  iSpring Suite’s integration with PowerPoint is one of its major strengths in terms of ease of implementation. The software is designed to be intuitive and user-friendly. iSpring Suite produces content that is SCORM-compliant, ensuring easy integration with most Learning Management Systems (LMS). The presence of an active user community and forums allows users to exchange tips, solve problems collaboratively, and share best practices.

I like the Text to speech feature as it is very easy to use. It cut down a lot on user to record their own voice. Besides, it is easy to implement where user just need to ensure their content of the slide is sufficient. We use the iSpring every day to kick start wit our eLearning module. iSpring can easily integrate with our Oracle system to create a platform for staff eLearning. The vendor we deal with is very supportive and able to answer our questions and needs.
The fact that it is smoothly added to PowerPoint is of great use. Besides, the level of customisation one gets with characters, backgrounds, and roleplay is the reason why I acquired the package.
I like that the software is easy to customize. We have several different projects that require either elearning content, quizzes, surveys, or long and more intense exams.

The most helpful aspects of iSpring Suite include its user-friendly interface, seamless conversion of PowerPoint to interactive HTML, and excellent customer support. Its upsides are its ease of use, robust features for creating engaging e-learning content, and reliable technical assistance.

The  tool is impressively user-friendly, making it accessible even for beginners. Its intuitive design ensures that users can quickly grasp its functionalities without requiring extensive training. The seamless integration with PowerPoint is a standout feature, allowing users to import and enhance their existing presentations with ease. Additionally, the text-to-speech function is highly effective, offering clear and natural-sounding voice options that can enhance the learning experience. The tool also boasts a wide array of well-designed templates, which can significantly streamline the course creation process and provide a professional look to the final product. Overall, it’s a robust and versatile tool that caters to a wide range of course creation needs.

Many educators, including teachers, lecturers, and trainers, often lack the ICT technical skills needed to create their own e-learning resources. Despite creating valuable and effective PowerPoint presentations for their students, these educators frequently find that students do not engage well with the resources they have created for them.

In my role as an educational technologist at a UK university, and with my previous background as an ICT and Computing teacher in a UK Secondary school setting, I have observed this issue extensively. During my Master's project, I discovered the iSpring Suite application, which revolutionised how I created resources for my students.

With iSpring Suite, I can effortlessly convert PowerPoint presentations into interactive e-learning formats without needing advanced technical skills. I have assisted many lecturers and teachers in transforming their standard PowerPoint presentations into engaging, interactive e-learning resources. The lecturers and teachers that I have helped, have all been impressed and the students love the resources to, especially those narrated presentations that have got embed media such as videos.

The interactive design format of iSpring published resources allows students to interact with the resources, a key feature that students like.

In my opinion, iSpring Suite is an essential tool for anyone responsible for creating educational or training resources. The investment is worthwhile in the long run. I recommend starting with a trial of the application; the results will speak for themselves. Additionally, iSpring offers excellent training resources that will guide you and if you need more ideas of what you can use iSpring Suite application for.
It's easy to use for me. It contains a lot of useful tools to create interactive content.
Easy to integrate the quizies to powerpoint presentation.

iSpring doesn't require you to learn an entirely new program. It builds on what is great about MS PowerPoint to create asthethic, interactive, and engaging learning modules. As a professor, I use iSpring to create asynchonus courses with audio and video. The ease of integration allows me to leverage existing slides and to quickly turn them into training modules that can be accessesed by students on most platforms. The latest version of iSpring has added many new features (such as greater design flexibility) that I'm looking forward to implementing into new modules.
It is easy to install and export the modules. Customer support was good when it was needed.

Its flexibility and simplicity when creating PowerPoint-based courses. Powerful and simple tools that make for a very agile learning curve compared to other authoring tools
Its conversation simulations are the most exciting and dynamic. The simplicity of incorporating computerized voice, the dynamic interactives that save many hours of work due to their creative potential.

I just started using iSpring for the creation of training materials. Previously we used PowerPoint to create presentations that played automatically. Transitioning to iSpring was exceptionally easy with its integration into PowerPoint. I was able to easily transform a PowerPoint into an effective training product with a learning check and final quiz. The platform is very intuitive for anyone familiar with using PowerPoint.

Customer support was very quick to respond when I ran into trouble. They asked pointed questions based on my request to understand not just what happened but also how I was using and saving my work to better ascertain what could be wrong and answer my inquiry without a constant back and forth between emails. After I answered support's questions, they only needed to respond once, and I had everything I needed to continue and create training material.
The iSpring Suite helps us to create consistant e-learning modules in combination with a ton of usefull features. The way it is integrated within PowerPoint is very accessable.
We use this to create learning to add to our LMS, and it's exactly what we need!

I am profficient with Office products and iSpring integrates well with these. Since this is the case it is very easy to use and to convert Powerpoint for example into Scorm for moodle. I have had a few issues and customer support has been on hand. The webinars are greats to help you with the ease of implementation.  I use it for every presentation for apprentices and it adds additional fuctionality.

The interactive features, such as role-play, have been a great asset to our training. Bringing our agents into interactive training modules has increased the retention of our material. The material is very easy to add to an LMS system. We have our own LMS, and the integration was smooth. It was also straight forward to start the creation process of our new training updates.

Easy navigation when using it. it encourages creativivty
its easy to implement

One of the things I like the most is the versatility of use, as it allows creating a video course as well as a conversation simulator, turning a simple PDF into a magazine, or creating a quiz.

The integration with PowerPoint (a good part of the material for in-person training is already in that format) also helps a lot to reduce time and work.

In addition, the easy installation of the tool on a computer makes it suitable for people without technical training.

One can practically start creating decent content after a few hours of use, its learning curve is not steep.

Two features that save time, money, and effort are the Text-to-Speech tool and the character creator, the first converting text to voice very simply and the second allowing the creation of a highly personalized character in various poses.

Of all the tools, the one we like a lot is QuizMaker, as it is very simple both in the creation of quizzes and exams and in their maintenance.

Finally, it is very reassuring to have their 24/7 technical support service, to address any doubts we might have with a function or simply with the use of the license on a device.
That would be a toss up between being easy to use and the people of iSpring.
Easy to use whether creating content from scratch or using existing content
That I can use voiceovers from the suite saving me time from recording my own voice.

The iSpring Suite as a PowerPoint plugin was easy in implementation and integration, and makes it quick and easy to create e-learning courses, even in collaboration with colleagues. We use the tools since installation easily and on a daily base. The standalone tools such as Quizmaker, Visuals, Talkmaster and CamPro as well as the online features such as Sites and Online Quiz have been convincing us for over a year. Best investment in L&D projects in recent years! We are fully satisfied with the customer support. There are simple online tutorials and a team of competent support colleagues.

The seamless integration with PowerPoint is user-friendly and versatile, allowing you to transform standard presentations into dynamic, interactive courses. The quizzing tool makes it easy to create diverse and engaging quizzes to test learners’ understanding.

It integrates with most PowerPoint options. It is easy to use. High-quality courses can be created in a short investment of time. It has a good image bank, allowing for the creation of most resources needed in e-learning. I have been using it for over 5 years and I love it. It allows for creating and editing videos, courses can be created in normal format and blog format, audios, animations, and transitions can be added, I use it many times a month, customer service is very fast and efficient, when publishing the courses they work, they are implemented and integrated into various platforms.
There are many helpful features that iSpring has. I love the quizzes and interactions. It's easy to learn and fun to use.
cons_raw: Difficult to point out, it might be worth adding templates for courses, diplomas so as to shorten the path of learning tools.

I've been using iSpring Suite for many months now, and I haven't seen any downsides to the software.  If I had to come up with an issue, I guess it would be nice to have more downloadable examples of different things you can do with the software.  But they already provide quite a few.

It took me many calls to the IT help desk to figure out how I can move material from my original personal computer to my work computer. The presentations would move over just fine, but the voice overs would not. The IT help desk showed me what I needed to do and we were able to resolve the issue. But it took me months before I was able to get it up and running because of that delay.  I think there are many videos or guides that would be needed for some of us to figure it out on our own.

Customer service is responsive in terms of quick replies to email, but I've found that the representatives tend to prefer email, even when you request a videoconference for tech support. I am guessing they are trying to reserve these tech support sessions for the most-needed, but I personally find it frustrating to have to email back and forth if I have a tech question.

One challenge I faced when I first started using iSpring Suite was the learning curve. With so many features and settings, it took a bit of time to figure out how to optimize everything for my LMS. Another hurdle was that some settings are spread across different sections, which meant a bit of extra navigation to tweak everything just right. However, after five years of working with it, those quirks have become second nature. Honestly, the benefits and flexibility it offers far outweigh the initial hurdles—it’s well worth the effort to learn.

My biggest complaint with ispring suite, is it can be a little redundant when trying to test the completed courses prior to publishing them.  It takes a minute or two for a preview to generate, if I find a small mistake that I want to correct immediatley, I have to close the preview to make the change, then the preview has to load all over again.  I wish at least minor edits could be made without closing the preview.  While its annoying, it would not deter me from making the purchase again.
I can't think of a single thing I don't like about iSpring Suite. It's a fantastic product that works perfectlly for me.
iSpring is heavily integrated with PowerPoint, so you need to be comfortable using PowerPoint to make the most of it. If you're not familiar with PowerPoint, this could be a barrier.

Not that I dislike it, however, I have found using the search function for example, looking for a certain facial expression, is not always helpful.  I find scrolling through the images I get a better selection, because what I end up choosing hasn't necessarily been tagged with the word used for searching.  I also find this in the icons and objects.
The biggest downside is that the features open in new windows and the existing PowerPoint windows are not able to be accessed when the iSpring feature windows are open.

I wish it could integrate with TutorLMS, as far as I checked it wasn't possible. We are an NGO, so it is a bit expensive to use the Ispring LMS. I wish it was easier to see the results other than getting an email.

Sometimes the integration of ISpring can be a challenge, especially if you are editing graphics, text to voice, or other elements in a pop up window, you cannot switch back to the minimized display for the original powerpoint. If you are using multiple monitors, it can be frustrating to find wich window you are trying to work in.
the voice quality of the german speakers did not satisfy us

There's nothing I dislike. Any issues I have with iSpring I can just ask for help from the developers and they will help me solve the problem. The only feature I never use are the "characters" and people they have interested because I personally feel they are too "fake." However, if you wanted the look of icon people for your product there are hundreds to pick from. I personally don't use these features and unload my own images- which iSpring has no trouble doing!
Though the text-to-speech is great in english, we're a french company, and the french text-to-speech could be improved.

We rely on using illustrated custom characters to keep our materials evergreen, but they only have front views available. It would be handy to have a back view or sideways view like the photographed images have evenutally. Occasionally, a word will be mispronounced from text to speech.

With such a fantastic option to include text-to-speech, they have made it extremely time-consuming to implement into a course, with numerous steps adding up to hundreds of clicks, especially if you want it to be an optional feature.
You cannot copy the text from the PowerPoint screen into the text-to-speech box, so I must copy the file into a PDF or copy the text into an outside document before it can be added to each screen's content. There has got to be a better design for this great option. With over 50 courses, adding the much-needed voice option considerably increases the redesign cost.

There is a slight learning curve in the beginning. But if you are good with PPT the iSpring suite has all sorts of guides to help you create your learning module. We are still exploring all the features to fully utilize it.
When we first started using iSpring (about 2 years ago) the closed caption feature was not as easy to locate and ensure that it is included in the lesson.

I really dislike that I cannot collaborate with other users of iSpring because we are technically on different "plans"/"subscriptions". In real life we are part of the same organization because I work with them as a freelancer, yet we cannot collaborate on any projects (housed in iSpring Cloud) because we purchased subscriptions separately. As an independent freelancer, I will ALWAYS have this problem for the rest of my career unless I can convince an organization to pay for my "seat". But it is hard enough as it is to get the client in the first place. I cannot ask them to also pay for an extra seat for me.
I wish the account could be location based, rather than user-based. We would benefit much more if a few people could take advantage of the software.
Probably the thing to be improved is keeping the help documents up to date with current versions.

The iSpringCloud Editor is very limited when compared to iSpring Suite, with no narration tool and very limited options for interactive objects.
iSpring Suite should provide a tool to export interactive objects to PDF files.
iSpring Suite and iSpringCloud Editor should be compatible; it would be very helpful to import and export objects between both tools.
I don't have any idea hoh can i halp with this.

While iSpring Suite is great for rapid content creation with its straightforward tools, it may not offer the depth of customization and advanced interactivity that some other e-learning authoring tools provide, such as Articulate Storyline or Adobe Captivate.
If you are using the laptop with lower spec, the loading to preview will take some time.
It is not something I dislike per se, it is just that to take the most out of the features, there is a learning curve before one can include them effectively within the presentation.
I like the features that are available. Very customizable and relatively easy to use.

While iSpring Suite is generally very good, there are a few issues that need fixing. For example, it occasionally experiences glitches with morph transitions, and there's a noticeable dropdown effect in wipe animations for text.

Has notable limitations when it comes to creating more dynamic and engaging courses. Making the courses lively and adding movement is quite challenging. The characters and elements are static, lacking the ability to animate or interact in ways that could make the content more engaging. As a result, the courses can feel rather flat and uninspiring. the capabilities for this are largely constrained by the features available in PowerPoint.

•	It will be good to include the Text to speech in the Lifetime Ispring Suite license.
•	It will be good to have a means of adding captions to the narrated published resources.

A great application, I have not really found any major problems when using it.
Need to add the option to save course progress on the server. Local cookies and cache is not very convenient for students. Some students experience getting stuck on certain slides.
If i need to modify just one quiz, i have to reload all of the quizies, i waste a lot of time with it.
It would be great to allow better integration of videos along with the text content displayed in slides.
Navigation between the various sections of the application can get confusing or cumbersome.
It takes a long time to migrate the features of the suite that operates on PowerPoint to its cloud version iSpring Space
I may have missed the warning, as I tend to hit the ground running, but a warning that saving projects to shared folder/cloud drive is not recommended would be very helpful.

There is little about iSpring I dislike. If there is one thing that would make it better, it is the way the custom templates (like colours and text) are handled in the different apps like quizmaker, talkmaster and visuals. It seems that you cannot make your own templates the default template that is used when you start a new. Each time I must select it by hand.
The only thing that I dislike, is the time it takes to convert to a SCORM file.
Would like to see some more different way of questions in assessments
You could add more training videos or have an isping academy.
in all honesty, i was disappointed when i couldnot publish my course. it simply showed i cant publish HTML

The official support in Spanish is something that would help a lot, although one can write to them in Spanish and the staff makes an effort to respond also in Spanish, the official response is in English.

The Interactions (or Visuals) tool used to create graphic organizers falls a bit short in terms of graphic customization and creating different behaviors, although this is something sacrificed in favor of ease of use and the tool's low learning curve.
I hate that they aren't as well know, yet, as some of the other tools.
The default animation is set to slides and not clicks
It doesnt work well with things like word art or altering images with backgrounds.

There are no major difficulties, apart from the fact that the SCORM file export is sometimes complicated if you have integrated special features in e-learning (branches with quizzes, repetition elearnings, etc.). TalkMaster could also be simpler, perhaps with AI support (by AI-prompting a GPT model instead of manual dialogue capture).
While it’s great for general eLearning needs, it may not be suitable for highly specialized or technical training. Additionally, the advanced features might seem somewhat limited for power users.

For now, the only thing I feel is missing is the ability to integrate with 3D images and their various animations, including the transformation with these images, and that the forward buttons could be enabled at the moment a specific animation finishes or occurs.

There are some glitches. If it can't find a file suddently you have no audio, etc. There are some things I'd like to see, like being able to save audio files separately or add captioning. Why isn't that a thing?

name: Kahoot!
pros_raw: Easy to put up Q/A for a zoom call with the team.
The readiness and ease of use, Pre loaded with questions
I love the way Kahoot opens up the interactivity of online meetings and learnings.
The best feature for me is the quiz game. It activates the learners competitive spirit and fast response.

Kahoot has a great interface for users- it's really easy to use and beginner-friendly. I have actively used it in classes both as a participant as well as an implementer. Kahoot is also great at advertising educational quizzes, and the best part is that its free!

I love that my students love it so much! I love that they are able to practice their skills and their vocabulary for Spanish in a fun and easy way. They enjoy the friendly competition among their peers as well.
It is easy to use, it makes it easy for me to break the ice at the beginning of meetings or a workshop, also to check if the topics that are being developed are being understood
It's very easy to use, meaning it's intuitive, the UI/UX is great and overall, it creates a great atmosphere whenever I present using Kahoot's quiz option, also it's very interactive

I love having the possibility to create fun activities for my students in a simple way. Additionally, it offers a thousand possibilities to gamify the activities so they see it as an entertaining challenge rather than an academic activity.

Kahoot is an engaging tool to use in the classroom to assess students or review for a test or quiz informally. Students love the game-like format and earning points! What I like best is that it can used as a study tool. However students think it is a game.
Discover feature with kahoots and we can modify and use them to the level of our students ability
Kahoot is so great for team games, meetings, training and so much more! It allows the creator to select from preexisting templates, or create their own! It is easy to use and very straightforward.

What we mainly like about the Kahoot platform is how we have gotten students more interested in learning through its tools. It has a straightforward interface allowing any person to adapt to it quite quickly; it creates online quizzes that provide more dynamic and effective communication with the audience. We are happy that nowadays, thanks to our tools with Kahoot, we can allow students to take some time for themselves and play through Kahoot. That way, through their phones, they can compete in a friendly manner while reviewing and reinforcing the topics already discussed in previous days.
Kahgoot we use for games and collab calls very interactive and easy to use
The User interface is very engaging and ensures users are easily able to get started with the quiz. Also, we integrated this with Airmeet and it has made process of quiz creation and launch very easy
Kahoot has made online quiz taking a really great experience for users. The user experience is great and the process of creating and managing quiz is very smooth.

Kahoot! offers several features that facilitate data collection and suitable knowledge bases; my company uses it to design and administer a wide variety of surveys to its staff. My organization has benefited greatly from using Kahoot! as I can now drill down on specific topics and advance them to an intermediate level in more routine survey activities. The incredible ease of use, good online training resources, and helpful customer service are just some features that make this software stand out.
It's a fun game based learning platform. Love to play engaging Q&A games.

I like that it is engaging and offers a multiplayer experience where friends and colleagues can join for whatever activity is planned and learn. I also like browsing to find other content that suits me for whatever I may need.
Kahoot is undoubtedly one of our favourite employee engagement tools. It's an incredible tool for bringing people together while having fun.
Kahoot makes learning fun.  Kids ask to play it.
I like the user friendly interface and how easy it is the access the quizzes with a large group
I love that I can use Kahoot with my work colleagues to help break the ice when starting a meeting. We can have fun while answering trivia questions I created related to our job duties.

I like Kahoot because it is fun. It helps to get people's attention and it also helps to learn. The good thing about Kahoot is that there are different types of interactions with the audience. What I used the most are polls and quizzes. I used them for educational purposes as well as to interact with the audience at my live or online events.
I like how simple it is to use and the data that it collects for myself and the students/staff I am using it with
Kahoot allows students to take a break from the regular "classroom" and break out their phones for a friendly competition while reinforcing topics discussed.

The most helpful thing about Kahoot is that my students actually look forward to playing. Before I started using Kahoot, reviewing with my students was like pulling teeth. Now, they look forward to logging on to kahoot.it, putting in the game code, and reviewing various topics. While it is so easy to create a Kahoot, there are also many premade Kahoots that I can use with my students. This saves me time in planning, which is major for a teacher.

My favorite thing about Kahoot! is the ability to create interactive quizzes that you can share with colleagues! In this way, we are able to help each other and make work lighter! My kids really love this software and ultimately leave knowing they have learned something!

My favorite aspect of Kahoot is because it is a fun approach to assessing my pupils' conceptual understanding. They are enthusiastic about playing Kahoot, but it's an exam showing which concepts they understand and need to be improved.

Kahoot is the method of reviewing and learning that students are consistently excited about. There's just something about making it a head-to-head game that motivates them in a new way and makes everyone sit up straight in their chairs as they plug the game code in to their phones! The platform is intuitive to set up questions, and the variety of question formats that also allows for information-gathering is beneficial in harnessing a captive audience for purposes beyond their review questions.

During the pandemic, Kahoot has been an innovative and game-changing platform for all corporates and educational institutes. We can make our quizzes and assignments at our own pace and convenience and set times and categories according to the audience. It provides individual rankings and scoreboards and also a feature for the audience to improve their scores further. The platform interface and its theme is perfect and appealing to all kind of audience.

Kahoot! is a wonderful tool to get students motivated and enthusiastic about learning. It is easy to use and the students love it. It is clear and quick. There are different games and you can download the reports at the end of the game. The students also love the podium.

Kahoot has proven to be a game changer for educational institutions and workplaces to conduct trivia, and engage audiences, especially during covid times. It allows one to create their own quizzes or assessments for more audience engagement. It also improves to perform better owing to the scoreboard feature, which really amazes the participants.

The most favourable feature of Kahoot is that the user interface of this platform both for the teachers and students is robust, user-friendly and intuitive which makes creating quizzes fun and interesting. Another thing that I like about this platform is that Kahoot constantly updates the platform and adds advanced features with each release. Recently, Kahoot added the feature of displaying the questions and answers on the users' devices which has proved to be very helpful for me as any meeting disturbance will not affect the test. Lastly, all the participants can see their ranks which person is ahead of them resulting in competition among students.
The design and the response time and the ease to use the site are too good.

Kahoot is very useful for keeping training and other meeting-type settings fresh and interactive. Instead of a "show of hands" or asking for participation, users/trainees are able to interact with questions and knowledge checks in a non-intrusive manner using their computers or smart devices.

My students and I love Kahoot!  I love the ability to create my own games which we can use to review content or even to pre-assess new content.  The fact that the mobile option was added during Covid-19 made the site even sweeter.  Teachers can now assign a game and put a start and end time.  After that, students who can see who won.  This really allows everyone to stay connected even when they are not together.

As a fifth grade teacher, I also love the availability of games that were not made by me but are still applicable.  If we have extra time, I can load a game on a relevant topic and we are ready to play within only a couple of minutes.

Many students thrive on competitiveness and this allows them to give their all.
Easy operation, user-friendly. The office loves to see the Kahoot game code, it's something they all look forward to. Keeps them involved with safety without reading policies and procedures.
I like the interface of the software.  It is very simple to use and you can prepare the questions quickly. Also, many people can join at the same time using with the code generated by the program.
The way we can answer multiple choice in a meeting
Students get excited to use this, and that's the best thing about it. Students actually request we use it in class when reviewing content and vocabulary. It's easy to use as a teacher.

When in terms of online games ,i really prefer this online app, it's easy to search, easy to learn, easy to understand the game mechanics, it gives adrenaline to the participants, another source of bonding with colleagues, another source of fun and games. I
This can play with so many players.

Simple to use format makes it easy for me to quickly and efficiently create engaging quizzes for my class. The students are able to play the quizzes as if it's a game and I find that material is retained better.

I like the online quiz functionality. My colleagues and I have been forced to work remotely the last few months due to lockdowns. We use Kahoot weekly to play fun games together in company wide meetings for prizes. It's a great way to bond with coworkers when working remotely. The quizes are fun and the way they calculate the score is based off if your answer is correct and the time it took you to answer. These games can get very competitive when prizes are on the line!

The interface is very user friendly that anyone could figure it out instantly. The way to connect to a game online is also very simple. I like everything about Kahoot!

My favorite thing about Kahoot is that my students think it is a game and not an assessment or learning tool. Any time I use a Kahoot as a formative assessment at the end of a lesson, my students are excited to play, even at times that they may not have enjoyed the lesson content. In addition, Kahoot offers tools to review your students answers post-game and to break down which questions may need to be covered again before a quiz or test.

I love that Kahoot! has an app to help facilitate participation in live quizzes and games. We're all on our phones anyway, so it's a great way to feel like you are playing a game. I also love that there is the option to create your own quizzes, or use premade quizzes (a holiday quiz, for example).

Kahoot is a great way to bring up the energy level in a classroom or even corporate setting. The platform is based on selecting multiple choice answers from a given question, and the faster the correct answer is chosen, the more points that are give to the participant. After each question, a leaderboard is shown. Kahoot quizzes are made by many contributors, many of which are public and can be played and assigned by anyone, or can be duplicated and edited. Even the "waiting room" music is fun. Personally, the smooth and professional design is what seals the deal in my book. It makes it look intriguing and not at all clunky for students.

Kahoot! 360 Presenter Plan has the most attractive features so far. It allows me do conduct polls, brainstorming activities, word clouds and quizzes for my training programs and even during our events.

Kahoot provides a very interactive and engaging platform where people can learn through games. Schools, universities, and many other organizations use it to conduct online quizzes and many other fun activities through which students and employees can learn and understand new things. Kahoot is vey easy to use and even a  first tim user can easilynavigate through the website.

The engagement factor and how it is adaptable inside the classroom. Students seem to love this program. Great for educational use as learning becomes a game, and friendly competition among students pushes them to understand what the teacher is teaching. I think the ability to add images and Youtube videos to the Kahoots adds even more to the interactivity of the platform. And I love having students create their own games and share those with the class.
cons_raw:
No customer service, hostage renewal processes and and refusal to "make right" when renewal charges disputed.

There is no service line. Your renewal charge may or may not be visible in your account dashboard. They will gladly "cancel" your service once disputed but will not refund a charge. I paid $240 to use this service 1x. I asked to have my renewal charge refunded when the paid features were NOT available to me after renewal. They refused and "cancelled" my account. Writing this review and hopefully saving others from this experience is my only recourse.
It will take only 50 users in free package

I never got to use my subscription. I decided against it. But I was outside of the 14-day period. So I'm charged for the full year. No option for cancellation. Not even at a prorated rate. After explaining the situation, still nothing. Rules are rules, I guess. But it feels a bit like a money grab and just generally like an unfair practice.
For the platform itself, I would say there is none.
While it's great at teaching topics, there are only so many questions that one can practice and it feels limited and restricted

I do not like that after a while you will have to pay for a subscription to Kahoot! I also don't like that there aren't other game formats available. I do not like that the students are able to grab pre made questions from the internet like I am

Initially the free version had multiple options, then they changed and the free version is practically no longer useful, so it necessarily forces you to pay a cost to use it, and it is not one of the cheapest options, there are other similar ones that offer more in their free version advantages, that is why today I practically do not use it

There is nothing in particular that I dislike; it gets the job done for me and it always creates a fun environment, the only thing I would say is that sometimes when the players are logged in and waiting for the game to start, it would crash out and not let them play the game

Some of my students feel a little overwhelmed by Kahoot!, among other things due to the time limit, the music, etc. I would also like it to offer the possibility of integrating it with the WordPress ecosystem in some way.

Honestly, there is nothing I dislike about Kahoot or the website. Their website is very user-friendly and easy to navigate. When hosting a game, many options are available that make differentiating the level of complexity for students easy.
It has been great but some features are not available in free version

My issue with Kahoot! is that I cannot justify the subscription price for our small company. It also doesn't allow you to sign up for a one time event which I feel like is a huge miss on their end because I would use it often if it was reasonably priced like some of its competitors

The Kahoot platform provides a lot of support for our organization. However, we can not fail to mention the biggest dislike we have, which is related to the cost of your license; we consider that they are very rigid on this issue since they do not offer subscription packages, perhaps with fewer tools but more economical, or at least a subscription per day to take advantage of the platform and address issues within the organization momentarily while it manages to grow more and has enough money for the payment of the license.
Could be better if Kahoot can incorporate with other chat or cloud service for video calls
I feel everything is great, but the user interface should be more customizable. Custom theme options can be very helpful to cover all the case. Rest all is good.
The User experience on Safari browser can be better. I sometimes felt the application was laggin in Safari browser. Apart from this rest is good. Video based quiz can help.
It would be wonderful to see survey results in real-time, and I would also like to create more customized reports.
UI UX of kahoot can be much more better.
It is not so easy to use, especially for newcomers and also unable to go back and change previous answers submitted before the timer runs out. Other than those, I have no complaints
I feel that it's a little pricey, I really want them to focus on providing more affordable services. Yes, there is a free plan but most of the features are absent in the free version.
About the only downside with Kahoot is that it gets the students wound up and they can be difficult to get settled after depending on the class.
Sometimes the the turnaround time for technical support can be a hindrance
The downside is when I want to use someone else's quiz I may forget to check all the details and sometimes there's not enough time to answer the question which leaves players feeling frustrated.

It is too complicated. There are too many options to choose from, it's hard to find what I need. That's why I stopped using them. The free version is very limited and I just couldn't try all the options and see if it is worth paying for.
I think that sometimes the main interface is hard to navigate and often there are glitches during games.
When using Kahoot live in the classroom, it doesn't perform well on wifi. We must have the host on a wired connection.
There are no downsides to Kahoot that I can think of. It is a great platform for reviewing and even quizzing my students. There is nothing I would improve with Kahoot.
I would love for Kahoot to continue diversifying their games to prevent boredom. Providing different music options would be more fun for the kids also while they are playing the game.
I am a vast Kahoot fan. I utilize Kahoot in the classroom, and so do my kids. Kahoot is so simple to use and enjoyable to play that I can't think of a single thing I dislike about that now.

I haven't gotten a really good grip on any of the features of Kahoot beyond the ability to create quizzes. The initial page after logging in comes off a bit overwhelming to me, and it's not the most intuitive to me until I get into the feature of quiz-building.

There are no such downsides to Kahoot, just the fact that it requires strong internet connectivity for the participants to use it. Even with the increase in the number of participants the platform starts to lag a bit which might get a bit irritating for the participants. There should be an improvement in the maximum number of participants to play the quiz.

Some of the features are not free. Sometimes it is annoying because I just want to use that feature. There is nothing else to dislike. Kahoot added pictures and made the text bigger. We also use folders now.

There is nothing much that I dislike about Kahoot, whereas changes in the user interface can definitely make it more user-friendly, for eg. the network usage, requires good internet connectivity which at times can be frustrating for participants.

Various bot hacks and auto-answer hacks have been built for Kahoot Sometimes, my quizzes are flooded with bots which is very annoying. I have to end the Kahoot and restart it to remove the bots. People can join Kahoots with their nicknames so I can't be sure that a person is the real person.
There is nothing I dislike about Kahoot.

Kahoot is gamification, but it also can come across a little "clunky" in user interface. It would be nice if you had the option of changing the overall "theme" of the user interface for either more professional settings, or for more low-key/casual audience groups.
Can be difficult to track down what student goes with what nickname.  Students say that sometimes it glitches and chooses an answer for them.
There is nothing at this time for me to dislike about Kahoot.
You don't have many options in answering, only, selection from four or selection from two question-answer types you can choose.
It is at times slow, causing delays and inconvince

The one downside is when a student performs poorly, it can be difficult for them to catch up with points. Low-performing students can get frustrated when they realize they numerically can't win and will shut down. It'd be helpful if there were a mechanism in place to confront this issue.
That you need fast internet connection to be able to compete. This game need internet to answers the questions, if your internet is slow, you might lose the game.
The subscription for a kahoot over 10 players is somewhat expensive. Most classrooms have more than ten students. I ended up purchasing a subscription but I wish it cost a little less.
Nothing comes to mind! The base functionality works great. I wish Kahoot would branch out more into other online games. I am sure that is on their future roadmap.

The only thing I dislike about Kahoot is that when you're making a custom quiz, images are no longer available from the free version. You can still use GIFs, which work just as well, but I don't know why the developers would remove the images.
There isn't much that I dislike about this software. It's a great way to encourage live engagement in meetings.

Once Kahoot came out with more than the free, basic platform, it was apparent that they had very much improved the features (audio questions, typed answers). This is very nice and could be useful for remote learning, but unfortunately it is not free. The only complaint is that the text does not allow for math typeface, but it is fairly simple to insert a jpeg image with any math problem shown in the question area.

I also don't enjoy that the platform sometimes causes students some anxiety since the questions are speed-based and timed. However, the administrator of the Kahoot has the ability to adjust the time limits.
I love everything about Kahoot! The price may seem a bit pricy. Can they maybe give discounts to long-time clients?

There is nothing to dislike about the software. The only thing Kahoot can do is maybe improve their server size as sometimes the page gets refresh on its own or the options are not visible during quizzes.

Not much honestly. I think Kahoot does everything they set out to do. They have enhanced student data tracking to see past results and statistics. It has changed quite a lot since its beginning, so the look and feel is totally different and will require relearning of the site.

name: Mentimeter
pros_raw: The program's pre-designed templates streamline the data entry process and provide ideas for engaging the audience, e.g., for icebreakers. Other notable benefits include a user-friendly interface, the capacity to create presentations in a timely manner, a concise overview of the outcomes, the option to import alternative presentation formats, and even the complimentary use of animated GIFs.
Its flexible and rather easy to use.  Especially the voting features etc.  I can be used in many different settings.

I use Mentimeter to create knowledge check quizzes that are timed and competitive in my nursing class. It only takes me about an hour to create a ten-question quiz with multiple choice and brief written answer questions, and the platform adds fun for students who tell me they like the graphics. The results provided after the quiz allow me to see which areas students are having the most trouble with. It's very win-win.

Even as a first time user of the tool, it was very clear what I needed to do to set up my first poll. This went down really well during our webinar and helped us gain some insight into who we had on the call. It was visually appealing, and as we got the majority of delegates participating, I can assume that they found it easy to use too.

I teach online remote classes, and some students don't speak up in the discussions. The reasons vary from a fear of being incorrect in front of classmates to a lack of confidence in English.

Mentimeter is a bridge that students are willing to cross without fear. The anonymity makes it easier for them to contribute. The variety of options, ranging from Wordcloud to open-ended questions, increases my options to engage students. The ready-to-use templates are a valuable addition.

Interactive and editing options also come in handy to add more flavours to the mix.

Amazing way to interact with the audience and engage with students, especially in the Nordic context. It is very hard to encourage the students to talk during class/express their feelings. Mentimeter facilitates that. I am especially enjoying the anonymous Q&A feature: students feel safe and confident to ask questions this way. Polls are useful. LaTeX code support should be an industry standard and Mentimeter has it - well done!
I am not really working much with response analysis after the presentations but those interested will appreciate easy access and provided data.

I use Mentimeter on our monthly team calls.  It's a great tool to warm the group up and put everyone in a place to contribute to the remainder of the meeting.

Some months I leverage the templates and pre-built slides provided by Menti.  Other months I build a quick deck with custom or team-specific questions.  Both methods are super straightforward and require minimal effort (but create maximum fun).

For us, it leads in to fun conversation and chatter during what would otherwise be dead time.  I've leverated multiple choice, word cloud and open ended questions- all with great results.

I often export the results and drop them in team slack- it's fun to have!

I love that participants don't need to set up a login---or even click on a link---to access. My users also appreciate that their contributions are anonymous. It's much more phone-friendly than other interactive tools I've tried, and the learning curve is fast. I'm using it to replace Jamboard, and it's much simpler to implement and navigate than other replacements I'm experimenting with (like Figjam, which has a lot of features). I also like that I can reuse the access code to return to the board for 14 days. I can also see how it would be easy to merge a Mentimeter deck with my existing informational slides, and I like that I can implement different types of interactions  (answer, poll, wordcloud)  from the same slidedeck

I love using Mentimeter to receive feedback about my presentations, but also to get a bit of background info on our Trainees. I can ask creative questions, not just "Rate this on a scale from 1-5" but also let them brainstorm and write a bit about themselves. The tools offer a lot of options, no matter if you are looking for answers from just one person at a time or a big crowd. The new AI tools also seem very impressive and make reviewing your feedback easy and exciting.

What I liked the best about Mentimeter is that I can reach younger students who are tech oriented. The results, gifs, graphics, and embedded videos are incorporating a learning style that fits what they grew up with. As an educator, it is important to modify our teaching styles to fit students. The timet er nails it in that regard. I also appreciate that introverted students are able to participate in class allowing them to overcome their social anxiety by showing them privately that they know the material and should feel confident in their answers.

Menti really helps keep my presentations engaging. It is beneficial with larger groups where it is difficult to get them up and talking and sharing ideas with each other, as I can ask open-response questions through Menti and let them share that way.

It is also great in smaller groups, as I often plan times for them to get up and physically engage with each other. However, there are people who are not comfortable speaking up in group work and discussions like that, so Menti provides a safe place for them to share ideas and ask questions without anyone knowing it is them.

I have also enjoyed that with Menti, I can go back and create great documents capturing ideas and suggestions and responding to questions, which can be sent out as follow-up to presentations.

Greetings for the day to mentimeter team,
First while making the ppt using this software was a pleasure before class time.
