Mentimeter is easy to use with my college students, who are attached to their phones or laptops. The QR code scan or accessing with the code at the top of the screen are easy to use and work well as long as you have internet access.

I've used it in smaller settings to get a sense of audience opinions or needs, I like the word cloud feature for revealing shared language use and understanding, the comment function for people to share in their own words (and I just used the "identify themes" feature so I didn't have to group responses on the fly - a little glitchy with similar comments appearing for different themes, but overall a big help).

As a department, we've used menti in faculty meetings to mix things up and anonymously gauge ideas in the room - it's great for non-judgemental brainstorming sessions!
In classes, I've used it as "content check-ins", where students anonymously reply and I can get a sense of what assigned materials they understood or not. It also helps collect their thoughts on where to focus our time, or types of examples I can bring in to lecture.

Overall, this has been an easy and affordable technology for me to adopt, it's been easy to integrate and implement in class, adding an interactive element in a space that's difficult to move around. I like that I was able to test the slides/questions with my teaching team before using it in class, and then simply reset so that the results weren't scewed with the earlier use.

Overall, a worthwhile addition to my college classroom!

I like being able to tell people they DO need to use their cellphones for this (because they're programmed to be told to put those things away and then surreptitiously keep them open for texting and other distractions). I like their phones being co-opted with the screen on their phone. It's easy to use and develop for me as a facilitator, as well as participants. I've never had a single complaint after having hundreds now participate. It's easy to upload a previously created slidedeck, and then being able to give the results of their responses back to the team is a value-add. I use it with groups of 10 and upwards of 100.

I love that it gives participants options. Some participants are not willing to share because they are shy or fear their comments or questions will be harshly judged. It gives them the ability to share their ideas and see the ideas and questions from their colleagues. They realize it is a safe place to share and the interactive component allows for sharing of ideas. I also love the ability to control the presentation from my phone when going from slide to slide and the notes feature at the palm of my hand.

I like being able to easily create interactive presentations for my team that engage the audience and that I can collect the results about after the presentation.  I've used these presentations many times as quizzes for my team to not only increase engagement but also identify knowledge gaps to help inform what training the team would benefit from.
My team's favourite part is watching where they and others fall in the leadership board after each question.

I love the way mentimeter shows the results in realtime, it helps to show instantly what people think, and takes away the having to chase up for feedback or getting poor feedback after events if left to people to send feedback. If you run presentation mode and gather that feedback in the moment, you get the pure unfiltered truth which is hat you want when it comes to feedback

It is easy to use and easy to implement and no time at all to create an account an instantly start to build your surveys or feedback forms.

You ahve a history of activity and can download the results to have a mechanism to show growth in the feedback and where you can improve, so you can implement those changes.

I like the interactive features and the clean layout.  It simplifies the process and makes it funj for students.  I found my students are more engaged when the presentation has interactive features that are on their computer.

- I was able to reuse the created Mentis multiple times and results are available in excel for each instances.
- Integration with MS Teams is an amazing feature
- Corporate accont integration
- Quick and self learnable

It allowed a way to include equally both in person and online participants in training, and kept participants more engaged and awake than usual!  I had many team members comment on how much they liked this too.  I love the report out at the end, so I have record of the team's feedback and answers.  This can be a great tool for internal research!

I feel that the best thing about Mentimeter is that it encourages all students to participate without fear of failure in front of their peers.  When designing the interactivity, the online help is extremely useful and almost immediate.  This gives me great confidence that I will be able to make the technology work.

I like using Mentimeter because of its simplicity and ease of use. The ability to choose the slide type (multichoice, poll, open ended, and so on) for each type of question or for your own use. The live interactivity is great and my colleagues love it. I usually use Mentimeter presentations when I do live webinars to over 100 people, it is a great way to engage them all at once with a simple qr code.

Working with teenagers can sometimes be lonely, especially in group settings as they are less likey to answer questions or engage in debates verbally due to peer embarressment. This can sometimes leave a presenation/ meeting with the speaker in a wall of silence.  However, with Menimeter you can ask questions and few responses live on screen. This allows interaction to occur in a virtual sense which then seems to build the confidence needed to see live on screen that their opinions do count and they do have peers with similar feelings. Before you kn ow it everyone is talking and adding to the discussions. A great aid allowing us adults to form a conncetion with teenagers in their preferred setting, vitually.
Various question types and data visualisation of the results available on the platform allowing for on the spot data presentation.

I work with a group of about 700, and it can be hard to make things interactive with that many college students in a lecture hall. Mentimeter allows me to create something that still keepts them engaged with the material, but also helps to feel like they're connecting with their peers. We have used this 4 times in the last few weeks and it has been great! I would also say its easy to use.

I like that mentimeter is easy to set up and begin use even if you don't have prior experience with the program.  You are able to add polls, questions, and many other features within your slide presentation. This ability is helpful in the education sector to increase student engagement.  If you have a paid version of the program then you can import Google Slides or PowerPoint into your presentation.  The free version allows you to have 50 people utilize your presentation per month whereas as you pay for versions of the site you have unlimited users within your presentation which would allow you to utilize Mentimeter more frequently within a given month.

The interactive nature of Mentimeter and the wide array of question types make pulling together presentations to collect feedback from client and stakeholder groups fun and easy. Our clients enjoy engaging with the process of design through Mentimeter and seeing their feedback spring up live. Also, documenting design decisions made is a BIG part of the architectural process - so being able to export client feedback collected during presentations to send back to client groups has also been valuable.

Mentimeter is a platform that literally has it all. Using mentimeter allows for my teaching to be diverse and engaging. Students are brought to a new level of technoogical learning that enables them to share their ideas in ways never thought before and, of course, in a way that is highly innovative and interactive. Mentimeter demonstrates the power of learning through technology and how the connection between the two serves a greater purpose: integration. The way that menitmeter opens the avenues of co-constructive learning is next to none; with the ever-so-interactive approach and tactics applied within its walls, mentimeter provides its users with endless opportunities to share knowledge across all platforms and the versatility of its components make for the perfect learning toolkit. This particular teaching and learning instrument contains a large variety of functions/settings which allow its users to utilize this on different occasions and as often as they prefer: its settings are also user-friendly which is an added bonus when it comes to 'navigation' potential challenges. The support received while being an active user of this tool is a state-of-the-art one: you will always have the guidance and responses you need to implement the best version of your teaching. Mentimeter 's integration into the lesson is one that takes place in the most innovative and seamless as possible: studetns always comment on its creative aspect and its ability to engage everyone in topics that they perhaps would otherwise not take part in. It is a tool that carries out respect, equality and principles of integration through its plethora of applications which are geared towards (quite literally) everyone's interests and current topics.

The variety of interactive questions and features are worth appreciating. The ease of adding images, videos and text is also quite useful and makes the usage more effective and engaging. The ease of launching the quizzes is also very high. The results that are received are easily downloaded and can be used to analyze further. I have been using Mnetimeter quite frequently in my work and has made me interact with a vast variety (diversity) of audience/participants, that are primarily teachers and students at HEIs. Many teachers have been trained by me to use and apply menti in their classes and beyond.

The support we receive from Mentimeter is by far one of the best things about Mentimeter. Our CSM is so excited and willing to present Mentimeter to our faculty. One semester, we had monthly sessions for our faculty to attend in which different topics and use case scenarios were covered. Now, we're embarking on a new professional development opprtunity for our faculty that will prove to be very exciting and innovative. A lot of other technology vendors don't give this kind of support or attention and thus has created a great working relationship.

I have been using Mentimeter for short quizzes as a part of employee orientation program for new joiners, and it has been a fantastic experience so far! The interactive quizzes keep everyone engaged and energized, making the onboarding process both fun and informative. The real-time feedback allows us to gauge understanding and encourage participation. Overall, Mentimeter is an excellent tool for employee engagement. Highly recommend it for any training session!

I love mentimeter. It allows me to make really profesional presentations and surveys in no time. The interactive options keep your audience listening to you. I use it for making better presentations, getting valuable insights from my pupils and even make quick test about what I am talking about.
It's super easy to use. It gives you few design options. This can be a downside but I find it very useful in order to work faster.
And I love AI features! I teach arts and design and I make new presentations almost everyday.

The interactive elements. My employees loves the surveys, the quizzes that I applied on my presentations with them, and this is something that is impossible with other corporate tools (MS Powerpoint, Google Presentations...). Also, the template use of the presentation is good and accelerate the presentation creation to me. It's easy to use, and is good even for experienced users, or for new ones.

I like the fact that there are many formats to ask a question and that the slide is editable with titles and images.
The real-time results make everything very interactive.
I also really like the fact that you can put one answer right and the others wrong, this makes it all very fun.

What I love most about Mentimeter is how easy it makes engaging with an audience. The real-time polls and quizzes are super fun and keep everyone involved. Plus, the platform is really simple to use, and the live feedback helps me see what people are thinking right away. It’s a great tool for making presentations way more interactive and interesting.
Easy to use and create, userfriendly tool. I like it
It is a fantastic tool that offers many possibilities for promoting interaction with the audience in a quick and simple way.

I use Mentimeter typically several times per week as a teacher/lecturer. I also enjoy sharing presentations and surveys with our faculty. Easy to use: even without reading tutorials, one can create a nice interactive presentation. But Mentimeter has created nice short video tutorials that allow delving into catchy presentations. The preview mode allows one to see how the presentation looks both for the presenter and for the audience's smartphone.

Variety of Question Types
Although the most engaging and funny type of question is the Quiz, there are many other useful types of slides. Multiple choice questions, Pin on the picture, Prioritization, Word-clouds, and others. Importantly, Throughout the presentation, the audience may ask questions, Vote for others questions etc.
Recent developments lead to embedding AI Tools within Open-Ended Questions. This works best for larger audiences and allows instant grasping of the main trends in the audience or revealing the most wanted gaps that the presenter might focus on.

Constant Support
Mentimeter has an excellent team that supports instantly. On several occasions our team required it and the solution was always sufficient and timely.

The Mentimeter team did great with the integration of PowerPoint. Nevertheless, the MentiPresentation can be self-sufficient many times. Another useful feature is that the results can be converted into pdf and analyzed later.

Students love it!

Easy acsess to gathering feedbak from students, good way of creating student activity in class and outside class, great tool for making reflection on different topics, nice way of cheking the students progress and understanding, to use as guidance for me in planning to make sure the teaching is at the rigth level.
Great follow-up by the Mentimeter team
Easy to use, doesn´t need a lot of training besides the introductions on Mentimeters webpage/blog
Great inspirational blog and academy

The most helpful part of Mentimeter is the intuitive and flexible interface, which allows users to design and run engaging activities before, during, and after meetings or events anonymously. I love the variety of questions and content types available to me as a user. It adds an extra layer to a meeting or event and saves time documenting responses and ideas. I've never had to contact customer support, but I know where to find it quickly should I need it in the future.

I've tried other websites to create word clouds. By comparison, Mentimeter is the easiest to use. The sign-in  process for both students and facilitators is quick and effortless. I also enjoy how responsive it is. I get results from the survey instantaneously, which improves engagement.  Creating a QR code is a breeze-especially for the less tech savy user. In addition, the font size and color scheme is not distracting. Overall, it's a great tool, and I look forward to exploring other capabilities.

It is an interactive way to share ideas and collaborate on different topics. You can include different types of questions (multiple choice, ranking, etc.) and either upload your own images or choose from a large database. It is easy to set up and control. There is a great library of themes and templates to choose from and get inspiration from. At the end of the presentation you can easily download the results. You can easily integrate it into different types of meetings on either a frequent or infrequent basis. I have not had to use customer support yet as the tool is really intuitive.

Mentimeter is easy to use and makes sessions highly interactive. Integrating my Google Slides was seamless and added to the experience. It allows the audience to get involved and provide input effortlessly. The ability to export results for future engagement is a great feature. Overall, I'm very happy with the product.

It is extremely easy to set up and use. It provides a clear output and it is very easy to share with participants. It increased interactivity and engagement on a long training session that would have otherwise been a monologue. It was very simple to incorporate into my existing training presentation. On top of that, at the end of the presentation, it allowed me to download the results easily and in a format perfect for sharing with others.

It is great to be able to immediately visualise audience comments to fellow audience members. It is great to get discussions started without spending so long on individual brainstorming, since one idea might spark other ideas easily in others.

My college students were fully engaging in the Mentimeter activity that we used to review for our first quiz of the semester. They loved the competitive aspect, animated characters from which to chose,  and the instant feedback. We are already planning to add more questions for our next review day and they are exercising their clicker fingers! :)

I recently started lecturing using Menti without PowerPoint. I particularly enjoyed how it enhanced audience engagement through interactive slides. The use of open-ended questions, polls, and ratings allowed for dynamic and participatory sessions. Incorporating quizzes with a leadership board also effectively gamified the class, enriching the student experience and making learning more enjoyable and interactive.

We used Mentimeter both during and at the end of our last two presentations. It allowed us to quickly gather information from the participants and display it immediately in a cleanly interpretable visual display.

It's fun and interactive. It gives EVERYONE a level playing field (whether they're in the room/in-person or whether they're virtual).  I started using Menti during pandemic years because of lots of these hybrid situations and have kept using it ever since, for both virtual and in-person team gatherings.

One of the best things is the fact that you can easily download the slides/data afterwards and share this with participants - they love it (esp. the word clouds)

The tool is very easy to use and helped me to create an engaging interaction with a young audience. The interaction was part of an internal training program in my firm and the AI beta feature helped me to figure out what would be the best configuration for each specific subject I was presenting.  The audience had no problem to participate in the interactions using the QR code, even though there were both presencial and online participants.
cons_raw: The software’s ready-made templates simplify the input process and offer suggestions for engaging the audience, including icebreakers. Its greatest advantage lies in its user-friendly interface, which facilitates quick presentation creation and succinct results summaries.

However I woiuld like to have mer possibilities to format the text in the slide especially in for exemple the 2x2 grid i.e. format the items and also the position on the slide.
In short - better formatting functions

The second imporvement is how to extract data - the data oupurt is some what cumbersome to analyse in different program.  I would lika to be able to have possiblities to adjust how th output should look like.

I would also like to have a live preview - that I can do adjustment and se the resluts live.

I would like to have it incorporated in a powerpoint presentation.  Te solutions today is not good enough - i you import the powerpoint presentation you are lacking the animation done in powerpoinit and the use of the webbased poweerpoint lack some features in poerpoint and not always accessible depend of your companies license.

There's no real downside, but I do wish there were more than two methods for writing these types of quizzes, for instance, pick three statements that are correct from these four (you can't toggle more than one right answer in the current version).

I struggled with the integration with PowerPoint, as I wanted the poll to appear within my slides. That might be because of how our systems are set up, and probably not a Mentimeter problem.

I set up a survey that asked to feedback on the session, and I wanted to include a tickbox to confirm whether or not the comments from the respondent could be used for promotion of future events, but couldn't find a way of doing this.

There isn't anything to dislike, but there is always a scope for improvement, and that is what I would like to share.

Presenting to  MS Teams option is helpful, but it would be great if other remote classroom tools, such as Zoom, etc. could be included. In the future, having a direct link and sync option with the LMS, such as D2L Brightspace, will also be extremely helpful.

Sadly, sometimes the software feels a bit "raw" and unpolished. It would be fine for a beta version distributed for testing but not for an end product. Here is my list of negatives:
- Fields within bullet slides are limited to 140 symbols. Why? The text scales well and at 140 symbols you will have 1 word on the second line but the rest of the line cannot be used. I found a workaround to bypass the character limit but won't share it so it can't be patched.
- Same applies to "Quote" slide symbol limit. Why??? I want to use lengthy quotes sometimes.
- No table integration. If you are solving a Finance exercise and want to include a table with calculations, well, tough luck. Best you can do is paste screenshots like it's 2002. Seems like a major oversight.
- Export to PDF is ridiculous, it just converts everything to images! Selectable text, links in the presentation - everything is lost on export. I like posting the slides for my students to be able to check, but with Menti export it doesn't work! They just get a PDF with slide images, even search doesn't work!
- Some scaling issues: instead of a dynamic scale there is a discrete cutoff. At 4 bullets the text will be huge, at 5 bullets it is tiny with nothing in between.
- Very poor image handling! You can't add multiple images to a slide like in beamer-LaTeX or in (pardon me) Microsoft PowerPoint. And even when you add a single image, you can't adjust positioning or scaling, there are several presets which are all rather poor.
- Building on the previous point, there is no slide constructor. There are some templates but in general you can't have, say, a text box or a quote and then some bullet points. You can't combine different slide types and techniques.
- Multiple other minor bugs and inconveniences not worth mentioning in detail - if devs are reading please reach out to me.
All these negatives don't cancel the fact that Mentimeter is a useful tool. I just wish it was possible to fix those issues and add some QoL features to make it actually pleasant to use.

The support team is nice but I don't feel like the developer team actively focuses on feedback. Some of the features I mentioned (like proper PDF export) have been requested for years with no real changes. If Mentimeter wants to become an industry leader, they really need to listen to the users and accelerate development to not fall behind compared to competitors. As a frequent user of Mentimeter (at a very serious level), it pains me that most of the feedback feels ignored. Hopefully, this review is a step in the right direction. Again, I am personally very open to talk with developers at any time, please don't hesitate to contact me.

I really don't have anything negative to say about Menti.  It's easy to use from both Admin and end user perspective.

If I HAD to complain- I don't love switching between a Google Slides Deck and the Menti Presentation.  I do know there's an integration option- I haven't explored this, believe it's part of paid plan.
Sometimes I like to have small groups working on different slides simultaneously, and I'm not Mentimeter has this capability, at least not within the same slidedeck.

I wish I had more options on the designs of my slides. Maybe that will come with the Premium version as I am just using the free one at the moment, but I do find the available options a bit repetetive after a while.

It was less intuitive to figure out how to download the slides. I wish I could have an option to download everything as a pdf AND ppt. I also think it would be interesting if the AI was able to automatically translate my text on the student's devices. So those who are contending with language barriers can understand the material.
I have not found any challenges yet. It has provided everything that I am looking for.

In free service their might be limitation of participants may be up to 100 twice or thrice in a month.

In this view, it may help me as an academician to inculculate creativity and innovation skills to students from day 1 of the college.

Also, it helps us to update in our areas of interest, its a tool for exploration for academician.

Apart from the restriction of participants in a month in free account, there is no absolutely bugs in the software.

Once again thank the mentimeter team to help me and my students to indulge in creativity and brain storming.

Thus far I've only found small issues, using it with my college lecture of 68 students. FYI, for more than 50 students, you need to subscribe, so my department covers it as part of my teaching tools.

On the sign-up slide, I want the sign-in code to be BIGGER. It's difficult to see from across the room.

Rather then 12 people writing the same answer, I'd like them to up-vote the first time a person wrote it rather than crowding the screen with the same answer (BUT, i just used the "identify themes" feature with helped with that - a little glitchy with similar comments appearing for different themes, but overall a big help)

If adding the QR code to later slides (and not only the sign-in slide), it covers slide content.

I can't figure out what the "thumbs up" is doing - students are using it, but it doesn't seem attached to particular comments, only the slide itself

I can't determine when the word cloud function will count a phrase "process of communication"  as ONE word, or THREE. It seems to vary

Like I said, small issues and ones I can work around!
I really enjoy it. It's the best at this interactive presentation software I've used. Like anything there's always a learning curve when you try something new.
I don't see a downside. It has been well received and allows for a lot of participation.

There are not many things I don't like about Mentimeter, the only downside is maybe the cost of having more participants, though I have never run out for my department meetings (once per month), I would like to use it for my weekly meetings as well but I would exceed the number of free participants so I use another app for the weekly meetings and save Menti for bigger ones.
Dislike is a strong word, I would say with any tool there could be refinements, As soem of the menu structures seem to be in places that would not be obvious. Adding in some AI elements would be good

It is not as flexible as powerpoint or google slides, so I can't create slides with all the designs or images I want.  However, I am still learning Mentimeter, so I may find better ways to do this in the future.  I know there is compatability with PowerPoint, so I will try to look more into it.

- Felt very limited with the free account, but eventually once I enabled paid version on my corporate account then was able to unlleash the full benefits.
- While the MS Teams integration feature is great, but people mentioned they cannot see the barcore or the menti code on the screen, hence had to put the code manually in the chat everytime.

With the free version, it's a little awkward to toggle between power point and menti (we did not build our entire presentation in Menti).  As with anything, there is a bit of a learning curve.  I wanted to compare our results in a power point presentation side by side to state wide and nationwide results, but was unable to do so (I think this is possible wiht a paid subscription)

The only downside that I can think of is that I always create my slides in PowerPoint and then import them.  The reason for this is that there is not enough flexibility in size and positioning of typing.  Also it is hard to manipulate photos within a screen.
Nothing. For the use I make of it, it works perfectly.
I cannot find any negatives to mentimeter, i would highly recommend.
On the free version where anyone can drop in to the session, participants are not asked to enter their names, so all their responses are anonymous.

I will always wish things like this are free. Especially in the educator sector, finding the funding for our programs can be very difficult, and we had to pay for this due to the size of our group.
Aside from cost, I would love a QR code students could scan to log in rather than trying to get a code- with our lecture hall being so large, it can be hard trying to squint and see the number.

I wish that mentimeter had more themes / backgrounds to choose from and that it had a few more options when inserting photos into the slides in terms of the formatting of the side with text and picture.  However for the free version of the product that I am currently using it is very intuitive and easy to integrate into lessons and presentations.
We have not experienced downsides aside from user errors and some confusion when giving presentations for those who are new to the Mentimeter platform.
I wish I would have discovered this tool sooner in my career! But it is here now, so I am grateful and hopeful!
Since past few months, the restriction in number of participants (only 50/month) is not welcoming. Earlier it was quite good.

It would be cool if Mentimeter could be created directly within the LMS so that faculty didn't have to work outside in a different platform. Due to this fact is why I think less faculty use it or take advantage of their license.

One drawback of using Mentimeter for our induction quiz sessions was the limited number of participants allowed in the free version. This restriction made it challenging to engage everyone in larger groups, leading to some missed opportunities for interaction. While the tool is effective for smaller sessions, the participant cap can hinder its potential for broader engagement. Upgrading to accommodate more users would definitely enhance the experience!

When this get fixed it will be suuuper powerful: you can work on better questions inside chatgpt and deliver instructions to mentimeter to get your perfect survey in seconds.

Also, I'd love if it could be easier to custom and change all the slides theme in an easier way.

I miss more variety on some templates. Sample: create a slide with 2 images, side by side. This kind of simple thing isn't possible, and sometimes requires an external image editor to do. Perhaps, for each type of template/mode of content, having more options would be greatul.
Otherwise, I have problems with sharing with some other people that are working with me on the presentation building. On Google Presentations and MS Powerpoint is easy to share with someone else, and get some help in some slides. Nowadays, this is taking some time for me, I ask to the other person to generate this slides on other programs, export as PNG and import as image, and this is a little bit clunky.
In addition to that, could be very helpful if we could import a powerpoint presentation and select some slides to import to the presentation.

I'm sorry that the free version only has 50 participants, working in a large company I only had the chance to try it once because I had many participants. I would try it at least three times before buying it but I can't.
Furthermore, it is a bit complicated for the creator to understand how to send the code or QR code, the "share" modal is a bit confusing
For what I'm using, I don't have anything bad to say.

Sometimes too many notifications receiving in emails about the pproducst so it is har to clarify what
is pure commercial and what is important. You should divide articles in some sectons like promoting new features, or explaining how something is wokring for new users.... but not sending commercials one after another...
The functions available from non-premium options have decreased over time.

Not much...  Until recently we suffered from not enough space While Mentimeter provides a range of powerful tools and features, it requires careful planning to use them efficiently. To create effective presentations and interactive sessions, users need to invest time in designing their content and setting up the various elements. Without proper planning, sessions can become disorganized or run over time, diminishing their effectiveness.
The integration with powerpoint is a bit tricky to set up and we have experienced that it doesn´t always work
I wouldn't say that I dislike anything in particular. Some previously appreciated features have been removed.
Of course, I will there were more free options.
When setting up and presenting via Mentimeter, I have not experienced anything that I did not like.

There aren't too many negative points, but switching between Google Slides and Mentimeter wasn't seamless. I couldn't continue with the clicker and had to use the computer mouse to manually click the next arrow going from the Google Slides to the Mentimeter (the other way around it worked fine). Not sure why that was, but it was a little annoying in terms of presentation flow.
I do not see any downsides at this time.

The PowerPoint integration can be reasonably flawed at times. I use it often, so it would be great if it worked closer to 90% of the time as opposed to less. Admittedly though I did not contact customer support about this, who may be able to easily implement a fix to this.
There was a learning curve in moving the slideshow forward. Sometimes it worked to advance the slide and other times I had to press enter. I'm sure that is just operator error though.
It could benefit from a more flexible slide design, multiple answer options in quizzes, and easier image positioning.
I'm not sure yet of the best way to link the results directly into my presentation, but I'm still new to using it.

The layout of the slides when editing - I can't seem to set up different layouts (e.g. grid, list etc.)
When it's updated, buttons/clicks usually change position and this is a bit frustrating when you've finally worked out the magic '3 clicks' you need to do to, say, download slides as PDF...and then it's all gone topsy turvey (equivalent to a supermarket changing all the position of its products on shelves. lol!)
I would like to have aditional interaction possibilities as list matching or sorting, as examples.

name: Dyknow Classroom
pros_raw: Since my students aren't immediately with me in the classroom, this has been a great tool for redirecting students that tend to go off on a Google/YouTube-induced tangent. I use the option to send individual messages more than anything, especially when I notice a student who is supposed to be working with me playing games and video chatting with their friends. I sometimes wish I could see the look on their face when their game gets paused and the link to my virtual class automatically opens
I can keep my students on track and focused on the lesson and not playing games or communicating with others.
I love actively monitoring student technology use in real-time instead of helplessly hearing that familiar, frenzied click-click-click of closing tabs as I approach.

The product is very intuitive, and our teachers do not need a lot of training to understand all of its features and how they can use them in a classroom setting.  It can be set up to always be on so the teacher does not have to remember to launch the software at the beginning of a class.  It allows the teacher to limit specific websites from being launched.  It can direct message students so a teacher can send a link in a chat message to all students or select students to access that site with a click.

I like that I can check the student's work as they complete their assignments.  It helps stop and fix mistakes before the students submit their work.  It also allows me not to stand over the students checking their work during Covid.
I can redirect students back to where they need to be.

I like that I can see what my students are working on.  Especially since most of my kiddos are virtual, if they are struggling with something, I can walk them through step by step and see what they are clicking or typing when helping them navigate their computer.
I also like that I can block websites that students should not be using during class time.
I love being able to check in, send students personal or group messages to keep them engaged. I love that I can seew what they are doing and send them individual message to keep them on task.
I love the blocking plans because it allows me to decide what the student can and can't be on.

I love the ability to monitor what my students are working on even if I am not in the classroom.  When I am out of the building I can log into see what they are working on with the Substitute and redirect students if needed.
The freedom to pull a small group of students and still easily manage the online behaviors of the rest of the students working independently.
The easy of using tis platform is awesome.
The best feature is being able to see what your students are doing in real time and the ability to communicate with them privately for redirection.
I love the ability to not only view students' screens but to be able to open a resource automatically for students.
The ability to monitor students' screens to make sure they are on task.
I love seeing what students are doing and block them if need be or send students a private message.
Gives ability to monitor students and keep them on task without wasting valuable instruction time.
Um, I like to help students at middle school. I'm a teacher, and they want me as their teacher, and they don't want me to leave, so that is what I do for fun, I hope you.
Being able to watch student's screens to make sure they are in the right place while we are distance learning has been extremely helpful.
I like being able to see what my students are looking at on their device.  If they happen to not be on the site I directed them to be on, I can address that quickly and easily.

I love to be able to see what each student is doing when they are at school and online.  I like to be able to add myself to teachers' classes on the fly and watch student during observations as well. Sending messages to kids is so powerful!

The ability to quickly see what applications and sites are in use and redirect scholars through direct messages to their screens. This lets me redirect scholars into positive activities, see when scholars are stuck and then engage with them to help them gain a better understanding of the material.

I really enjoy the ease of use and  how easy it is to navigate. I was able to log in and start using it immediately without a tutorial. I like the fact that I was able to combine my class with ease. Remote learning has made this an important factor because single classes were separated. I love the fact that I am able to send my students messages directly to their screen and know if they receive it.  Also being able to block student from certain websites really assist with making students remain focused. I also like that I am able to "take" my current students from another teacher if they have forgotten to release them without having to contact another teacher. I also enjoy being able to talk  a student through a process because I can see exactly what they are doing on their screen and what is on the screen.  Dyknow has really made it so much more manageable to teach during such trying times.

What I like best about DyKnow is the ability it gives you to monitor students during class. It also gives a transcript of everything they went on and the time spent once you close the monitoring session.

I like being able to see all screens at once! The Real-Time Activity Tracker is great to see how many kids are on certain sites and pick out a student or two who are on sites they shouldn't be. I also like being able to send a private message to a student who is off task. Freezing a screen is also extremely helpful. I also liked that I was able to combine some classes and rename them.

I love that I can set up "Blocking Plans" that can allow me to permanently block certain sites, like YouTube. I can also set up plans that only allow my students to be on a singular site at one time. This is great when students need to be focused or testing!
It can help me monitor students while I'm teaching small groups.
The ability to monitor remotely and assist students to remain on educational sights.

The ease of use is impressive. I've had no problems setting up groups and everything is right there for me to see. When first starting to use DyKnow, I referenced the help videos several times and they were easy to follow and informative. I haven't had a problem with the software yet and it gives me confidence that my students are on task without a running verbal dialogue from me to keep them there. I can privately let them know I'm with them and use this information to verify who is working toward mastery and who may need a little more help.

Dyknow is an easy to use tool for monitoring students. It allows the user to see the screen of students, send them messages to refocus, create blocking plans for certain times, allows co-teachers, and is effective at restricting materials. It is also a great way to allow a student to only be on the LMS of a school so there is no cheating. You can also print reports of computer activity.

I LOVE segmented blocking. It helps so much for an interventionist who sees multiple groups throughout the day. I also love that I can keep my students focused on the task at hand by blocking distracting sites and messaging them reminders.

Now that we are all still virtual, I really like the fact that I can do a quick check up and see exactly what the kids are doing.  It is also nice to be able to get their attention by temporarily locking  their screen with a personalized message.

I love having the ability to manage my classes when teaching virtually.  Testing virtually obviously comes with its own challenges, but at least blocking the ability to open other browsers while testing is a really helpful option.  I also love that I can see which programs my students are using in class.  Sometimes we work on grammar and research simultaneously, so it helps me differentiate my lessons and allow students to move freely from one program to the next.

The thing I like best is that I can see the students work as they are doing it. This helps me know if the understand the task or if they need guidance as they are doing it. I would have to wait until the students turned in the assignment to see the work teaching virtually without Dyknow . This would be a problem when teaching students with disabilities because some students need assistance through each step of a task. In short, this allows me to share my screen for the students to see during a meet while I use Dyknow to make sure they are doing step by step instructions.
I like that I can see what my students are doing in the virtual setting

I LOVE BEING ABLE TO BLOCK STUDENTS THAT ARE NOT ON TASK!  IF TESTING, I CAN MAKE SURE THAT THEY ARE NOT ON THE INCORRECT SITE.  WITH E-LEARNING, I LOVE HAVING AN EXTRA SET OF EYES ON MY STUDENTS EVEN WHILE THEY ARE IN THE COMFORT OF THEIR OWN HOMES.

I like the ability to be able to see student's screens when I physically cannot see their screens ALL the time. It also helps with telling parents what their students may be doing if they are not always on task as they should be.

I like that our teachers can see students' screens and redirect students' in real time, giving them the ability to have some control over what students are seeing and how they are spending their time, even when the teacher and student are not in the same room.

I get to turn on segmented blocking, so it is really helpful for my students that only come to me 2 days a week rather than 4-5. I have 2 groups of students (A day and B day) and DyKnow helps me to keep my A day students on track in class while leaving my B day students computers' alone since they're not in class on those days.

I love that I can monitor my students during virtual learning, I also loved it during in person learning as well. It helps me pace my lessons so I can see that everyone is on the right screen. It also helps me redirect students that are becoming off-task.

I like that Dyknow allows me to monitor my student's screens while they work.  This allows me to make sure that they are on-task during class time.  I can also view their screens and help them via messaging to help them find something.  It is also EXTREMELY helpful that I can allow only certain websites if needed.

What I like best about using DyKnow is being able to see student work live. There are many upsides to using this tool, all which are focused on student achievement. I love that I can see what students are doing on school devices at any time. If we are working on an assignment online I can easily see if they have a part they are getting wrong and help them fix it. I also love that I can send them links directly and it will automatically pull up the link to the page I want students to be on! I also love that I can block students from websites that may be distracting or inappropriate and guide them back to the appropriate work task. DyKnow is an essential tool for any modern educator!

Dyknow keeps track of the sites students frequent so that a blocking plan can be generated based on the data. I especially like the immediate blocking plan features so that students who do get distracted by going to other sites on the internet can be blocked.  One feature I wish they had a feature that allowed me to select specific students from any of my classes because sometimes classes are mixed up and I can't effectively adjust their access for the students that I have.  A new feature that has been very helpful is allowing the teacher to push out polls and questions to check for understanding.
I love that I can assign work on Google classroom or other platform and instantly KNOW if students are on task

Dyknow allows teachers to make students accountable when using technology. I can block distracting websites, send personal and group messages; and because the students know I am watching, they are less likely to do anything bad on their devices.

I love that I can see all my students computers, but also communicate directly with them about an issue. My students know that I am actively watching their screens, and that keeps them focused. I also like that I can block certain websites straight from the application, for instance, Youtube. We sometimes use Youtube in class for activities, so I do not need it blocked all the time.

Dyknow lets my students know their computer use is being monitored so they are less likely to be off task. In addition, since COVID , I have moved to a paperless math classroom and Dyknow has given me the opportunity to share students' screens so they can share strategies and ask questions in a meaningful way.
You can send private messages to students.
It is amazing being able to limit what students are able to access on their devices so that they are more likely to stay focused on their learning.
Being able to monitor student progress while they are remote
cons_raw: Of course, students being students, have found some ways to circumvent Dyknow. Ultimately though, I've decided if they are that dedicated to ignoring me, I'll just leave them to it. Some students have said they feel as if their teachers are just spying on them and being the Chromebook police.
The cost? Our school district paid for ours, but I know work at a small private school.

I don't particularly appreciate that only one teacher at a time can monitor a group of students. It is quite inconvenient when a previous teacher forgets to stop tracking a group of students when I need to begin. A solution to this is allowing multiple teachers to monitor actively simultaneously.
I really don't have anything negative to share.  We have used this product for years and our teachers really like it.

I don't like that its not a live version like apple classroom.  I hate that it takes time to update.  During class this isn't as much a problem but during assessments or when I am trying to make sure students don't cheat then the refresh rate takes forever.

IT is slow. I can't open two classes at once, without having to permanently combine the class. It takes a long time to open the student's screens. I can't block a page just for that student. IF a student logs out of their Chromebook they are no longer logged in and they have to click on log back into Dyknow. I can't schedule the classes to start and stop when needed. It doesn't save messages between teacher and student. That's all I can think about at the moment. Students are learning how to go around Dyknow by opening other programs.
That there are times, when Dyknow locks up and I have to reload the site.
Some of my students are asynchronous, and due to the way our classes are setup, I cannot monitor them simultanously with my in person students.
I dislike the fact that I often have to refresh the page in order to get away from one class to get to the next.

I wish that there were a way to set a list of blocked websites that would always remained blocked.  I currently have to begin my session and then click the list of sites that I want to block.  Also I wish that session would last longer than 2 hours.
Sometimes one of the students will not show up on the monitoring page- then it says to reach out to a higher level person.
It all works well and am very pleased with its functions.
Dislike that there are teachers who will add my kids to their watchlist while my class is still going on.
I wish DyKnow would create a way to be able to close a website or link.
I wished it was easier to choose my blocked sites list or turn off/on the list.
I would like to be able to control a kids desktop
Students can use other browsers to get around Dyknow.
um i don't like doing like scray hights or
Being able to remote in would be nice or to look at more than one tab.
Nothing.  Everything works like I would like it to.

I would like to be able to pull up an individual student by name and see what they are doing at that moment.  It would help me in parent conferences or when we are in a virtual mode. I'd also like for kids to be able to send me a message back.

Inability to quickly scroll through larger snapshots of student computers. I would like to be able to group and rearrange thumbnails with drag and drop. I would like to be able to update blocking with a popup instead of a separate page.
The only thing I would say is the lag time.  I would love to be able to see the student's activity in real time.

Everything is great about Dyknow except it only allows you to monitor students that are in your PowerSchool roster. I also do tutoring and intervention time. Since this is not an actual class, I cannot monitor or help those students because they are not listed with me. Another issue is that it is not live monitoring. It refreshes every 15 seconds.

I wish my teaching partner and I could be monitoring students at the same time. I also wish that even my student's specials teachers were able to have access to these as well.

Also, there should be more "how to" videos. There are aspects of this that I had to just figure out over time and it would have been more helpful to have some videos to explain and elaborate on different features of Dyknow.
Sometimes there seem to be glitches in the system.  The program doesn't always refresh and update as quickly as I would hope.
The lack of individual reports for activity use.

I worry that things are in such a minimalist userface, that the temptation might be to add on and expand what the software can do and its uses. I think that is what makes for a stress-free environment for learning how to use DyKnow.

The only downside one might experience is that it can take a moment to load student screens and update them. This can be a site issue or an internet issue.You are also limited in the custom messages you can send to a student length wise. Sometimes, it would be beneficial for a longer message option.
I do wish the screens for multiple students were larger so I could see more of what they are doing at a glance. It takes time to click on each one to enlarge it.

It seems like sometimes when I lock the screen to send a message, and I unlock it, they are already on something else.  I also wish there was a way to monitor when the kids open something in a different tab (they have gotten quick about changing tabs).

I don't like that my students can hook up another monitor to their school computer because Dyknow doesn't have the capability for me to see what students are doing on the other screen.  I also sometimes have a hard time blocking certain websites for students.  It seems to work on some school computers but not others, which becomes problematic.

I teach grades 5-8  and each class is coded differently. What I dislike about Dyknow is that I have to keep changing from class to class in order to see what each student in my class is doing which takes a little time.
There is nothing that I dislike.  Dyknow is a great resource.

IT IS SOMETIMES HARD TO KNOW IF I SHOULD INCLUDE OR EXCLUDE WITH BLOCKING... I HAVE KICKED MY KIDS OUT MANY TIMES DUE TO INCLUDING GOOGLE.  IT IS HARD TO KNOW IF I AM GOING TO KICK THEM OUT OF OUR GOOGLE CLASSROOM IF I BLOCK SOMETHING THAT THEY MAY NEED FOR CLASS, OR WILL IT JUST BLOCK THEM FROM EVERYTHING, INCLUDING OUT CLASS ON GOOGLE MEET?  I HAVE DONE THIS MANY TIMES!

I wish teachers had the ability to make a small group with any combination of students enrolled in any of their PowerSchool classes - rather having some one at the admin level make a class/group for them.
After using Dyknow for several years, I cannot think of something that I dislike with the program.  The Admin customer support is always so fast to respond and helpful, I truly appreciate that!

I only dislike that I can't see their screens in real time as they move it. The screens update after 30s or 60s most of the time and I know that they can switch tabs really quickly.... so I would like to be able to see what they are seeing at that exact moment.

I wish there was a way to see ALL of my students, maybe if they log into their Google Account and it comes with an extension. Because not all of my students are using a school-issued chromebook, I still have a handful who I cannot see.

Sometimes the app moves  a little slow.  A 15 second refresh time when I'm monitoring an individual student's screen is a little slow for my preferences.  Also would be helpful when looking at the whole-class thumbnails to have the screens refreshed more often than every 60 seconds.

The only downside I see with DyKnow is that students can't immediately message back. It would be helpful if there was like a "DyKnow" chat that students could respond to me after I say "get back in browser" etc. Sometimes their links aren't working or they're struggling and they need a way to communicate that to me.

I do not dislike any of the features of DyKnow. I just wish the select students option was available. That way when classes have been combined, I am able to set a blocking plan if needed. I would recommend having a 'quick clip' screenshot button within the program and uploading the parent's email addresses from PowerSchool so that teachers can send parents screenshots of what their child is/is not working on.  I also recently found out that an app is not available. I was devastated, but I will manage.  An app would be very beneficial for teachers.
Nothing. I like everything about it so far.

When I end monitoring for one class, I wish it would automatically go to the next class instead of to the analytics screen. I would also like to see an option to add a sticky note to student screens with the link or instructions for the day (if the non-lock screen message could just stay on the screen until I turn it off).
I wish that the screens would be a constant live view, instead of a timed update.
The only "dislike" would be the inability for students to share in real-time - while I can refesh and see new work or changes, there is a lag.
Too many screens to click back and forth.

Like all technology, DyKnow sometimes has issues that need resolved.  One of the biggest things students has figured out is that if they open a lot of tabs, they are able to get around DyKnow's filtering.
Ability for students to avoid monitoring through logging off

name: ClassDojo
pros_raw: I love many things about Class Dojo, but what I like best is the customizability it offers to those on all levels of its platform, whether you're the teacher, child, or guardian. Everyone is able to custom fit their experience to what they seek to get out of it.

ClassDojo is a very powerfull classroom management platform that allows teachers, students and parents to communicate, share information and track progress in a very easy and attractive interface. Its very easy to integrate with Google Classroom.

ClassDojo is most helpful for communication with parents! It is like a Facebook for the classroom. I can post messages and pictures for everyone to see or send individual parent messages. The other tools on ClassDojo are also quite helpful. I use the group maker often because the students can see that the groups are "fair" due to their being chosen randomly. The points tracker is a fantastic behavior management tool. We do not use it as much in 5th grade, but when I taught 1st grade, the students worked hard to receive points. There is also an option to take away points. However, I do not use that feature. Lastly, parents can access a behavior report based on the issues you give and take away.
I love that I can message my daughters teacher any time dirtying the school day and get a response.
It allows me to track student behavior througout the day, and keep parents updated as our day is happening!

The great thing about this particular app was the ability to connect with/message the parents quickly through text in the app! Also, parents were able to view when their student was rewarded or reprimanded for their behavior using a point system within the app. When students did well in class, the teacher could choose what they were being rewarded for and when students needed redirection, the reason was indicated and points were taken accordingly. In addition, you could display student points on the projector so the students could see and even hear when they received points or heard of points being taken away. It made rewarding and giving consequences very interactive! I genuinely believe that this app helped my students perform better in class!

ClassDojo was a great way for teachers to monitor behavior and for students to track their behavior over the course of the day. It also allowed parents to see how their children were doing and to connect with teachers if they had questions. It also had other tools like a randomizer and group maker that I used for lessons.

Classdojo makes it easy to provide access to parents and students to your classroom. Reaching out to them is easy as all messages go to their phones and it also allows parents to see how their children are doing behaviorally and academically. That accountability piece is important in making sure students stay engaged.
I live that I can see how my child is sing during the day as well as communicate with teachers.  It allows multiple family members to be added to view and stay up to date

My favorite thing is the simplicity of sharing weekly newsletters and photos of my class.  I basically use it as a bragging board and give my top winners small rewards every Monday.  I see them striving for the joyous sounds, and they try to let me catch them doing something exceptionally kind to others.  I also have the negatives available when needed, and my experience with 4th graders, it still works.

My favorite thing is the simplicity of sharing weekly newsletters and photos of my class.  I basically use it as a bragging board and give my top winners small rewards every Monday.  I see them striving for the joyous sounds, and they try to let me catch them doing something exceptionally kind to others.  I also have the negatives available when needed, and my experience with 4th graders, it still works.

ClassDojo is a multi-faceted edtech tool that is a behavior management and recording tool, a parent messenger system, and a classroom flattener/Instagram all rolled into one. It has so many essential features for teaching it may as well be called the Swiss Army Knife of the teacher tech toolbox.

What I love most is that it is FREE!!!

- I like how classDojo makes is teacher-parent interaction easier.
- i love the videos it provides that teach social and emotional skills.
- I like the point system ClassDojo uses which helps motivate students.
- I love how you can use it on Tablets, phones, laptops, etc...
- I like how fun, easy and customizable it is.

Helps me shape positive behaviors, while giving consequences too for undesired behaviors.
I make everything worth money.
I download student's pictures to replace the childish cartoons.

My students are crazy about ClassDojo. I use the app to reward my students for being awesome. When they are not on task or doing the right thing, they know that they will lose a Dojo if they do not correct their behavior. I use ClassDojo to keep track of student behavior, rewarding them with certain prizes for getting specific amounts of Dojo points.

ClassDojo is a multimedia portfolio platform that teachers can use completely free of cost. They can track behavior data using target "do's" and "don't" that are customizable; data is available for multiple timeframes to see short-term and long-term progress.

Parent communication is easy-- parents can be added and will see when students add and lose points. "Texting" parents through the platform is a capability that allows for easy and informal communication.

ClassDojo also uses cartoon monsters (Mojo and friends) in videos to teach students social-emotional skills with pre-written discussion questions designed for class and home
I like that you can reward points, contact families directly, and post student work instantly.
It translates messages within the app for my non English speaking families.
Being a school librarian, I love to hear when a teacher uses ClassDojo.  I know I can award good behavior.  And that not only will the teacher see this, but also the parents.

My favorite part about ClassDojo is that the characters are very engaging and cute. My students love to select their icons, and it keeps them highly motivated. I also enjoy that I can communicate with parents through the app.
I love how I can use it to contact parents in regards to their child's progress in the classroom in addition to rewarding points to the students that behave well
This is a great way to promote positive behaviors in my kindergarten classroom and give feedback to parents. My clsss can also work towards prizes or rewards through it.
I love how easy and multipurpose this is in the classroom. I also love that the kids can choose their own character.

I enjoy the multiple uses of this software. I can post updates to families about the class I am teaching, send private messages individually or by grouping (where parents see this group message only in their private inbox), provide points to students for their efforts, and post pictures and videos which show what students have learned/experienced in class.
The way you're able to connect your students and parents & even admin if you'd like so everyone is on board and all the stakeholders are involved
I appreciate the opportunity to recognize good behavior and share with families.
Communication with parents and the character education videos! These videos present lessons about empathy and many others on a kid level. My students love them!
The ability to communicate with my students and parents in a safe and effective manner.

I totally love ClassDojo, it makes me feel organized a little more own to earth when it comes to keeping track to reward, attendance and even communicating with my student's parents.
Since I've found classdojo I've been able to take roll without wasting class time, I can also keep record of my students participation and download the records at the end of the week. It's just incredible!
The translation feature is very helpful.
I love how instant the messages can be accessed by everyone on the company site and I also love how you can translate the messages for families who speak different languages.
I love ClassDojo! It is a great way to keep in touch with my child's teacher. I also love how the teacher can send me pictures throughout the day of activites.
It's easy to give points to students quickly
It was great giving students instant feedback.

Easy to navigate around and to see latest notifications or posts. You can see notices to you as the parent and then switch to see it from your child(ren)'s point of view where it lists their work and outstanding activities.  Like most things, this is only as useful as the school that uses it makes it and it depends on how they wish to utilise it.

It makes it so you can award students for their good behavior. This makes them motivated to behave well and finish their work on time as they get awarded with virtual points. Another thing that is amazing about ClassDojo is their impressive amount of analytics. I can easily see the students progress and see what activities he/she is excelling at. It also serves as a way for me to communicate with parents about their student's performance. They themselves can also track how their child is doing and improve on what he/she is lacking in.
I like the customizable behaviors with positive and negative points. The communication and the class stream.
Quick access to families through two way text

I like the students should personalise their own Dojo avatars. It's nice to be able to monitor activities and show progress and areas for improvement to the children and parents. Like the bidirectional contact built in. I like the customisation of the language of appreciation and reporting to suit the needs of the classroom. Both clear messages to related parents. You may display interactions between the parents and public messages (Class Story).

Classdojo helps me to communicate very effectively with teachers, especially when I cannot go frequently, to school or school meetings if I am very busy, what I like most about classdojo is that it is like a social network in which we can be I can even send messages to the teachers that my son is ill or that he will be late to school or even the teachers notify me of my son's compartment or his student performance even the teachers can leave messages on the board and I can see where they are working or what they are doing at any time also its ease of use is great ew very intuitive anyone can use it even without prior knowledge

My favorite feature is ease of messaging to students or parents in a safe and contextualized environment. At it's best, it's an excellent communication tool that allows sharing of pictures and other progress. The group maker is a really handy quick tool for creating small groups. I'll say more about the awarding of points built into the program in a moment, but if used carefully there are helpful ways a teacher can show parents and students where their strengths are and when they are having a good day.

This is a software that enables you to give students immediate feedback on their actions, participation and work in real time. For each term you teach, you can build separate classes. There are different character pictures that your students can choose from or pick. My students liked their character to pick. You may also personalize when and why students receive points. You can project the screen to see if students win or lose points. I appreciate your outstanding sharing in classrooms, in message communities, and in post-student events in your unique portfolio. I love the easy way parents can cope with school activities and recall them.

Adjust statement and linguistic documentation to meet classroom requirements. All clear linked parent communications. Public media perceptions of parents can be used. As a key means of communication for text and acts, I like ClassDojo. This is also nice if you can access an application or the internet. I do this in order to support socially skilled students with bravery, friendliness, kindness and not just positive and negative actions. I appreciate your sharing of examples of what you do with your unique portfolio in schools, message families and after-student work. I love the simplicity with which parents can keep up with school events and recall them.
I like that Class Dojo is set up to be a communication tool between parents for both text communication and behavior communications. It's also good that it's accessible via an app or the web.

I do that to help students with social skills do courage, kindness, empathy and not just positive and negative behavior tracking. In classrooms, message families and post-student work, I appreciate that you can share examples about what you do with your individual portfolio. I love the simplicity with which parents can keep up and recall school activities. Invitations, questions and photographs are useful for students.

ClassDojo has been an essential part of my classroom management and way to communicate with parents. I am able to keep in touch with my parents on a daily and sometimes hourly bases on behavior, classroom procedures, what we are working on and even classroom lesson feedback. My parents get notified of behavior points and even get informed when there are areas of concern that the student needs to work on for behavior. I have enjoyed how ClassDojo allows me to keep my students points and encouragements on the board all day. So I can use my smartboard in the classroom to add points, watch short videos and do a quick brain break or even use my cell phone to give out some positive points and even get a motivational short movie going on. I am able to launch messages to the board for my students to read. For example, on days I am out I can send a message to the smartboard using ClassDojo to inform my class of the expectations for the day and to remind them that I will be checking in. This platform allows more than just  behavior management and parent communication. I have been able to use this as a means for distance learning. Since the parents at our school are use to working with this app we have been able to keep up with communication, videos and even pictures on the chat feed. I know many of the teachers I work with were so pleased to be able to keep up with ClassDojo even not being in the classroom or seeing them at school. One of the features that we use often on my fourth grade team is the group collaboration.  There are multiple ways to place students in groups and allow a quick group formation. The group maker and the think pair share feature is fantastic in helping managing students and get students to work cooperatively.  The tools in ClassDojo help to run a smooth, transitional classroom. The noise meter, random student selector and timer are just some of the amazing tools in this app and system.

I like how it keeps a record of feedback for students. This comes in handy when we are analyzing data to look at class trends along with student trends. For example, I can see that during a specific time frame, a certain student is consistently struggling with staying on task (i.e. after lunch)> Because this is happening consistently, I can look into why. I noticed that he is wound up after recess, so I know that I need to do a calming down activity to get him ready to learn. I also noticed that my whole class needed a wake up activity between reading and writing. I also like how you can easily share information with parents. When I first started using it, I had a student who was particularly struggling with behavior. I was able to share a daily communication with his mom about his behavior, both positive behaviors and the negative behaviors. This made it easy for me to communicate daily about his needs so that she could reinforce behaviors at home, and we could pinpoint his biggest areas of struggle.

The classroom management feature and communication features for parents and teachers. The students respond better to hearing the teacher give + or - for their behavior. Also the teacher can assign the reason for the updates to the students' behavior. The app is very user friendly and easy to add parents. I'm a parent and a teacher and I'm able to toggle between both accounts with ease.

Class Dojo is easy to use and is much more than just individual point tracking. You have the ability to group students and give positive or negative points. This helps reinforce the behavior you want in your classroom. The program offers a super simple user interface. The program makes it easy for teachers, parents, and students to use without confusion.
cons_raw: It is truly difficult to think of any negative feedback for the platform. I would like to see more monsters added, but only because I think they're adorable and the minds behind Class Dojo have spectacular creativity!
I have no complaints about classdojo features or any other aspect

One thing I do not like about ClassDojo is that parents can not post on the main feed. It would be beneficial if I could give my room parents privileges to post information about parties and events because they do a lot of planning and organizing for me.
The pay to use feature. It should all be accessible if schools are using it.
I have no complaints with ClassDojo at this time!
The only issue that I had with using Class Dojo was that the school district eventually told staff that we could no longer use it since it was not district-approved.
I don't remember many downsides, to be honest. I *think* it had a message translator, but I don't remember. (We last used ClassDojo three years ago, and we've had some things happen since then) ;)

I wish there were a way to link Classdojo to my grading tool. This way, parents can be updated on how their children are doing.

Also, I wish there was a feature that allowed us to video conference through Classdojo. This would be great for parent/teacher nights, and Zoom isn't always reliable.
Only negitive I have is that teachers don't utilize it as effectively as they could. I also don't like that you have to get a paid version to do points at home.
Gosh, this is a tricky question.  I would have to say that I have several parents who feel uncertain if it is just a convo between them and myself, or if everyone can see it.
Gosh, this is a tricky question.  I would have to say that I have several parents who feel uncertain if it is just a convo between them and myself, or if everyone can see it.

The only thing I dislike is that people have a problem with this company, it is truly a gift to teaching, and my students and I could not live without it. There is nothing I can complain about when it comes to ClassDojo. This company has been responsive to all of my needs as a teacher, and if I ever find something I want with ClassDojo, I email them, and they do it for me, no questions asked.
I don't have any negative point to adress, except maybe for the fact that you cannot use it with adult learners.

Not geared towards high school.
I'd lik to see better avatars.
Absolutely nothing! ClassDojo is necessary for keeping track of student behavior as well as keeping in touch with parents. I have used ClassDojo to keep track of student attendance as well.
There's not much I do not like. I do wish I were able to upload more class photos at once.
The downsides are that it is difficult to translate with non english speakers and for class dojo to be effective, there needs to be buy in from all staff.
I wish it had some of the features of iMessage ( likes and dislikes).
There is nothing I dislike about ClassDojo.

Something I dislike about ClassDojo is that the process of getting parents to connect to the ClassDojo account can be difficult. I often have to step by step guide my families to connecting to the app.
I don't think there is anything to dislike about the app and website. I love how I can messsge parents without giving them my numbers in addition to rewarding points
At times my students can become too focused on it and the points. It can be all consuming to some which I don't like (as far as how many points they're accumulating etc ).
The only thing I dislike has nothing to do with the app but more so to do with the parents who don't even look at it.
I have noticed that sometimes the app does not notify me when I receive a message outside of the quiet hours.
An easier way to invite parents to the site
Negative behavior tracking is too public. I do not choose this feature with Kindergarten.
I cannot get all parents on board and sign up! Some Parents do not like to download another app to their phones in order to connect with me.
There is nothing that I dislike about this program.
Nothing, I totally love ClassDojo and I wouldn't change a thing!
It can be difficult for parents who are not proficient in technology.
I dislike the notifications, I wish you were able to disable notifications from a particular person as opposed to receiving all messages from those in the same company.
I have not found anything I dislike about using ClassDojo.
There's not an option for more than 5 points
Not being able to use it as an approved app in CPS. : {
It's really not easy to edit documents on a mobile device but it's pretty simple on a PC.  There's also no way to know if a message sent has been seen.

I dislike how it is somewhat hard to use ClassDojo. Sometimes, the features may malfunction and the analytics may show false information. The points on the dashboard can glitch out and causes the whole thing to fall apart. The mobile app is poorly made as even scrolling becomes a hassle.
I'd like an option for something to make it slightly more geared to my older sixth grade students.
Inconsistency in program - have had to reinstall several times to stay logged in, or messages would not come through as a notification

I wish I could give you several feedback points in just one go. I 'd love to see videos that answer commonly asked end-user questions and let teachers see what the students / parents are doing. I don't like any students being more likely to get negative behaviour. I 'd rather be central to behaviours.
