I have not had any problems with the application but I would love for the application to have more functions such as maybe a didactic game or puzzles or puzzles that children can use and be distracted depending on that not everything is tasks but rather that they learn having fun too and not be so tedious for them

Although this is a major feature, the points component does worry me. There is a strong behaviorist approach apparent in assigning 'points' for students doing well or poorly, and there's a good deal of research on the negative impacts this can have early on. It's like a digital version of the behavior clip up/clip down board, and i personally deeply disagree with this behavioral management approach. That said, teachers don't have to use the app in this way, but i do still list it as a dislike since the app structure may encourage such use.

Emails that aren't received in real time I don't like. Sometimes she has notifications too late and parents are irritated not to react. There is the ability to link to parents but I can only use this in a basic form without real student information and parent accounts because of the password and the log-on limitations in my school district. I would also like a minimized version to still be displayed while presenting a power point or other activity.

I want to provide multiple input sources at the same time. In order to find out what students / parents are looking at, I want to see the videos that address end-user questions. I don't like e-mails which are not always received in real time. She sometimes got too late notifications and parents were annoyed not to respond. I want the app to provide additional functionalities such as teaching games, puzzles or challenges that kids can use and relax depending on how fun and not so boring they are.

I dislike that communications do not always come through in real-time. Sometimes I've received communications that were time sensitive too late and had parents upset I did not respond.  Also, as a behavior recorder, I feel you are stuck either making broad categories and having to explain every interaction or make each interaction so specific that it takes forever to find it.

I have no problem with the application but I'd like the software to provide additional features, such as teaching games, puzzles or challenges that kids can use and relax from depending on how it's not just activities, but how they're fun too and not so boring to them. The attitudes are rather disgraceful. I think connecting with parents is crucial, but understanding and sharing what happens in the classroom.

I haven't run into an issue of dislike with the program. The ability to upload pictures and files has really allowed access and communication to be shared between students, parents and teachers. There is always the consideration of student and family privacy. Some students are not motivated by a point system and turn to anger and frustration when they see others that are receiving points and they aren't. Since all students are displayed it would be helpful to have points hidden so that there isn't a negative feeling towards others or causes a separation in students who are on task and not. It would be helpful to have a set goals feature. This would help the students who don't earn points often, but see a goal they are trying to attain and receive high fives for that.

There are some times I do not have tech with me (although I do like the app) and it is difficult for us to use with Guest Teachers. They do not have access to technology, so they cannot give them points, so it will not be immediate. I also wish we had some different options for characters that would were a little bit more appealing to older kids (they do love the characters, but would like something a bit more mature looking.

I used it for high school based on a suggestion but it's too juvenile to use with high school as a form of classroom management. Although I did have a slight increase in classroom participation, the effectiveness of the feature wore off.

There is not much I dislike about this program. I always like to suggest more options for the character selection since some of them can be "elementary". However, they have added custom options that help eliminate this negative. I implemented this program in both high school (grades 9-12) and middle school (grades 6-8), and it was a hit with both groups of students. However, i should note that the middle school students latched on much easier and quicker than the older students. Many of the older students thought it was t0o "elementary" for them.

name: LanSchool
pros_raw: When it works, it is pretty good at managing students from surfing to websites when restricting the sites.
Most helpful is monitoring student use of technology, sending students private messages, and overall control over computers.
it can be used in real time and students can see what you see
its a good program and all but please review what I dislike about the program

I like many aspects of this software - being able to privately message a student when you can see that they are not on the website or assignment they are supposed to be on is a favorite, but there are many features that I found beneficial.
Being able to monitor student computers.

LanSchool has been an absolute game-changer in my classroom. During virtual education and the pandemic, they became used to gaming or otherwise tuning out during class, then Googling all the answers once it came time to complete their assignments. This behavior carried over into the classroom this semester, and I have had to resort to LanSchool to manage it. Using LanSchool, I can block specific websites and applications during class, check in on students to ensure they are on task, and blank student screens when they need to focus on the front of the classroom.
I liked the ease of logging on to see kids versus the program we have now.
Able to make mentors make blended learning easier any time.

I find it a good feature that I can accurately keep an eye on all of the things that are being projected on the screens of the students in my class and block them as I see fit. It makes sure that everyone is trying their best and putting their full attention to the work at hand. If they are having trouble on their computer screen, I am able to remotely connect to their computer and control it to fix the problem that they are having. Another good feature is that I can screen share my screen to the screens of my students. By doing so, they can better see and understand the topic I am teaching.
Classroom management, ability to share screens, blocking websites, confidential chatting with students.

It is nice to centrally track, restrict the exposure and sharing of programs and pages on other screens. It helps to differentiate a lesson greatly as everyone actively engages in the subject. Students will look at my screen and follow my pattern in a small window. You don't have the problem that because of lighting issues or their classroom location, a student can't look at the monitor in advance.

I like that this software allows me complete control over the screens in my classroom. I don't have to worry about getting up and walking around the classroom as much to monitor screens because I have all of the tools necessary to do that from the front of the room at my desk. I also really like the fact that I am able to control screens and devices from my computer, this gives me more control over what is going on in the room. My favorite feature would be that I am able to set a list of blocked websites that students are not able to visit on their computers. I am also able to put everyone on the same website or page, or open programs with the click of a button. It is very nice software that allows me more control and to worry less about little things.

The ability to web limit is a tool that works very well from us. Obviously the ability to monitor all student devices within a given classroom is the main function. It does serve that purpose very well; however it seems to be sort of laggy in most cases. Building class lists is a good way to ensure that you have all students "locked" into your Lanschool. In the elementary, building class lists are very easy as you don't usually have any reason to  change the class. In the high school this is a little more difficult and with students changing classes, it's really up to the teach to keep on top of their rosters per class. Otherwise we do have situations where people bump into each other when trying to manage the class.

My teachers love the ability of LanSchool Air to integrate with Google Classroom making class management a piece of cake! The ability to 'curtain' the chromebook and also push out websites is great for the younger grades.

I really like the LIMIT WEB feature that allows teachers the control to set the sites they want their students to go to.  They can set it and forget it, and be confident that LAN School will only allow the students to go to the sites the teacher has pre approved.  The teacher is safe to move around the room and work in small groups knowing the other students are not accessing sites that are not allowed during their class.

LanSchool helps teachers to minimize distractions and encourage students to stay on task when completing assignments. I love that teachers are able to communicate one to one with a student without drawing unwanted attention. LanSchool increases student engagement as well as provide differentiated learning in the classrooms.
What I like best about LanSchool Air is the digital features such as monitoring a student's computer screen, viewing them all at once, and being able to easily send private messages to each student.
Able to keep fidelity of educational practices.

I love being able to see all screens at once. I am able to push out website to all students at once. Sending private messages directly to students allows me to communicate with my students without disrupting the learning of others. In setting up LanSchool Air, I love the ability to sync with Google Classroom.

I like being able to blank screens, send messages to students and somewhere is the ability to limit and assign websites and bring students into lanschool if they haven't accpeted except I really hadn't had the time this year to figure that out.  I know that the capacity to do so is there I jut have not yet figured it out.  I like being able to view and monitor screens and make sure that  they are on task.

Absolutely able to monitor (most) students’ computers and interact with them via instant message. For me, this is a life saver. Instead of calling a kid out in front of the entire class, I can message them directly and screenshot their screens, etc.

Interacting with a student via instant message/chat often deescalates the situation, and that’s a win in my book.

As a Tech I love using Lanschool to manage our 1-1 classroom carts of 20-30 each Lenovo Yoga laptops. I ask students to leave yogas out of the carts and powered up when the go to Activity. All I have to do is walk into the classroom, sit at the teacher's desk, login to all 30 yoga laptops via Lanschool and hit run a program, open a website, or test an application.
Uses include: windows updates, restarts, Lenovo system updates for drivers, intel driver checks, test software programs by controlling all, sound tests, etc etc. Very fast, very simple. I logoff all or restart all and move on to the next classroom. Students take care of yogas when they return from Activity.
Teachers use it to monitor students, teach them how to do something, send encouraging notes, prompt them to get back on task, let the whole class know that they have 10 minutes left, blank the screens to get their attention, mute the sound, or share the work that another has done on their individual computer. Teachers simply LOVE Lanschool.
Students use lanschool to monitor each other, send questions to the teacher, display their work, and assist themselves with their own personal digital citizenship.

I most like being able to look at and control the students' computers. I often don't tell them the full capacity of what I'm able to do but they usually find out when I freeze their screens with a message that gets their attention. Using a student's computer as a demo for the other students is also quite handy.

We just started testing Lanschool in our District for 2 schools in the last school year.  I have to say I love it.  As an IT Specialist, having Lanschool installed on our managed devices makes my life so much easier for installing software, viewing issues, detecting problems, etc.  My teachers are also having great results.  They truly appreciate the private connections they can make with students that are off task, have a question, or need help with projects.  I highly recommend this product to any school district that is looking for a remote assistance, and teacher monitor product for their classrooms.
I am recommending that we purchase this product for our other high schools in our district.

I like being able to see what the kids are on and bring up their screen. I also like being able to freeze their screens. I like being able to limit what websites their own because they always try to get on gaming site or sites that are inappropriate.

Monitoring students with LanSchool is easy and even fun, why should admins not have fun too? Preventing behaviour is much better than punishing and with LanSchool I get to be the lifeguard of the sea of digital information. Quick installs of other software is a breeze and communication in the classroom is enhanced greatly. Screenshots are extremely valuable in discipline situations and are irrefutable. Being able to see every desktop in the Tech Console is magical.

LanSchool gives me the control I need to make my classroom work while being engaged and focused on assignments, it has highly improved my students work and scores.
Also, the fact that I can screenshot my student's activity was a great tool for discipline and parent's conference. It helped a lot when small children from primary classes came to practice their own programs like I-Station, because LanSchool has helped me to log students at once, that saved a lot of time for teachers and students as well.

I love that I am able to limit the sites students can use on different projects. It helps students focus, and it helps remind them to be honest about what we are trying to do in the classroom. To me as a teacher, they should be focused on the task at hand.

What's great about this is that students are accountable for the work they do on computers. I didn't even have to check most of the time, but if a student was off-task, I could send them a silent message instead of calling them out in front of the class. There are other great features, such as quizzes and being able to blank screens when you need everyone's attention.

I use Lanschool to give my students fast survey's and quizzes. It is fast, easy and the results are immediate, they provide feedback to myself and my students in a manageable format that we can grasp and use ASAP. As well, I use Lanschool to monitor my students progress on class projects while they are using technology within the classroom. Lanschool has so many different aspects and tools, test writing too. I like the testing format because it will automatically grade the assessments for you and allows for different types of response from your kids.

One of the best things about LanSchool is that it helps teachers ensure students working on computers are actually on task.  The program lets you monitor what the student is doing as well as the web content used by students. It allows teachers to turn off unauthorized games and or websites.  Teachers can type questions or comments to students as they work to help guide the student's learning.   Another great feature is that students can communicate with you if they have questions on an online task.  This program is a must have for any school that is using computers one to one with each student.  My school gives all students computers for online tasks.  In today's technological world teaching students to work on the computer in multiple digital media is imperative to ensuring a well balanced education that allows students to be college and career ready.

Our teachers who have mobile or desktop labs love being able to push links to each computer shortening the time it takes to make digital instruction effective.  They also like being able to use the thumbnail view of each computer and show that over a projector so that students police themselves.  LanSchool helps lessen students not on task.

LOVE that this gives me control over my classroom. When I have 30+ students on laptops, it can be nearly impossible to monitor everyone. I often project all of my students screens on my board so that I can roam around my classroom and monitor at the same time. I also love that I can block websites and limit website use for my class. This makes my job so much easier.

I like LanSchools ability to show my screen to student screens so as we practice a new skill for the first time they can see my screen in a small window on theirs and follow my model. No more do you run into the issue of a student not being able to see the screen up front due to lighting issues or their position in the classroom. It's also a great tool to allow me to showcase a student's screen to the board or everyone elses screens so they can name specifically what is done well or help that student get better by pushing their own understandings further!

I find LanSchool to be invaluable in my lab. I teach grades 9 & 10 and it is great to help keep them on task. Sometimes I need to use the Blank Screen feature to guarantee they are not distracted by the computers.
I like 100% of the ideas behind LANSchool.  Monitoring a lab, executing tasks, remote controlling, key monitoring....a dozen other things...The overarching idea is terrific.

LanSchool has been a lifesaver for me since I started teaching computer skills classes. Yes, I can see what my kids are doing, and make sure that they're on task; I can roll out questions and files and such too. But the real beauty for me is the ability to demo projects and skills and have everyone's attention. Oh, and to be able to remote in to a students machine to help them out when they're stuck is a plus, too!
Lanschool is great to monitor students and share documents, screens and files with students.

Once Lanschool is set up (more on that  later) it is very helpful in the class.  You can do the usual monitoring of students and taking control of computers, but it has many useful teaching purposes too.  You can show all students one computer as an example.  You can push out specific web sites for students to get to quickly. You can talk to all or one student in headphones (they freak out the first time!). You can freeze all computers for lecture/discussion when they are not needed.
The provision of dashboard, multiscreen overview of my students currently using it making me look like a super server.

We have been surprised at the ease of learning the new system with our staff members. Teachers are able to connect right away with the system and begin using the monitor features with little training. Then we are able to go over other features as they become comfortable with the system and want to expand their use. Many teachers are asking within a week... what else can it do.

For the requirements of our k-12 district, by far we find Student Lab management to be the most useful feature of LANschool. The ability to simultaneously log in any number of machines at once is frankly invaluable. "Log on selected machines" is easily the most used feature for me.
I love having the ability to see all screens, to share a screen to all, to white list or blacklist sites for a specific class, I can't even tell you all the great features.  More details below!

The best part is not needing any extra hardware to control the workstations. The software has many features that give almost full control of the student workstations to the faculty members. They are able to see what the students are doing as well as specifically limit them from doing certain activities such as opening programs or visiting websites.

I liked that I could view each student's work, block them or send them messages whenever it deemed necessary. They responded when I froze their screen and asked them to return to their desks for transition. I also liked the feature in which you can push websites.
I like that I can view student search history and send messages to individual students.

Teachers can view students' screens and control their attention better using this software. Works on any platform (Mac, Windows, ChromeOS). Love how Lenovo is including a license with their Chromebooks.

What I love about LanSchool is that you are able to see classes directly related to your class and students that you are monitoring their time on task. Letting students know that you can see what they are doing, will indirectly force them to complete their daily task.
cons_raw: Customer service is not very helpful.  Students do not consistently show up on the monitor when they are logged in.  There is a lag and students are able to jump fromone page to another without the teacher seeing it. Lanschool also does not show window tabs. If a student has 5 tabs in a window, it only shows the one they are on. Lanschool also won't show when students are using computer products like the calculator. When math teachers are giving tests and students are not supposed to use calculator, the teacher needs to see the students using those products. New students will sometimes show up as expected,  while others will never load on lanschool. The product is only available on more recent software.

The lag time between what is on the student screen and what shows up in LanSchool is the least helpful.  That lag time is a critical flaw that impedes my ability to keep students on task.

The program has frozen a few times, not allowing me to dismiss a class or start a new one. It seems to be happening more lately.
can't see students who use their own device
I hate that they sell/use your info without people's permission I'm thinking of possibly suing them for doing that

I never learned how to update all of the remote computers from my main computer when updates were issued to the software, so that required time and effort duplicating the installs to every student computer.

Lanschool Air give a lot less control to the teacher than the past version I had. I can no longer control a student device, or look up internet history. I do not like the "upgrade" to Lanschool Air at all.
LanSchool is one of the few programs that I cannot really think of a downside to using.
LanSchool always went down.  It lagged often.  We checked to see if it was our system but it was not.  It was the program
The affordability of the gadget to be used.

I dislike how hard it is to get used to the software. It is not in the slightest "user friendly". Unfortunately, I'm not much of a tech geek so it took a lot of time to get used to and I had to ask some of my collogues how are also using it to give me a detailed explanation of LanSchool which only took away from the time I could be using it. Another problem is that remotely connecting to a computer takes a lot of time and it's responding time is the slowest I have ever seen.
Unable to remote control Chromebook screens. Unable to pull keystrokes from Chromebooks.

It could be a little difficult to schedule lessons with many different teachers. Scripting is too technical to configure. A simple Quick Launch Transmission tool should be given to allow me to claim that you have these next computers in this room rather than open outdated configuration files.

The interface is not amazing and you have to go into menus sometimes to get features. Also when it freezes or gets buggy you have to completely close and reopen the program which can take some time to do.

The crashing and unfriendly user interface. Mostly the fact that it doesn't open as a normal program and is hidden in the bottom right of Windows hurts. Our staff that is computer illiterate has a tough time understanding this. We try to have days where we try to show off more uses for Lanschool. Unfortunately, some people "lose" themselves in the menus even though it doesn't seem that difficult for typical computer savvy users.
Still waiting for the ability to share teacher/student screens with the class.

Ther are some hiccups with the Chromebooks.  Students can restart the Chromebooks (which only takes a few seconds) and when they come back online the teacher no longer sees the device in their console so the student is free from the teacher control.

I would not consider this a dislike, However, it would be great for students to be able to return their assignment to teachers after they have been completed. Also, it would be great to have more accessibility options for students. For instance, like immersive reader, and incentive for students who do a great job! (I.E stickers. emojies...etc)
I dislike that I am not able to view student history.
It is sometimes difficult to monitor large groups.
I wish I had the ability to view students' history or click/exit off different tabs that my students may have open.

I went from the regular version to the air and it actually seems like the air is harder to use. It's not really intuitive it's like I need to have someone sit down and show me how to do things instead of just clicking on a student screen and getting options of what I want to do that I can instantly apply. For example I can't just click on a student's screen and send a message I have to click somewhere else select the student from a menu and them send a message...  I also used to just be able to have the window with the students screens open all the time or minimized in the background, but now if I am not using it for a while it needs to be refresed or I have to log back in.  It also seems like a lot of the time I have quiet a few students whose screens are not showing on my monitor like it is easy for them to get around the monitoring software somehow.

At my previous school, we had the full version of LanSchool. I’m the reason we researched and got this program. LanSchool Air is like seeing a really great profile on a dating website, connecting with the person via text/phone calls, and then meeting in person and realizing they were using their better looking sibling’s information. It lacks the ability to follow keystrokes, see previous sites visited, and control a student’s screen remotely. Also, the add/block/restrict function is not as useful in Air.

We have had some difficulties getting Lanschool Tech Console to work consistently in our environment. We have also been unsuccessful with the Lanschool reporting application to alert us to inappropriate student use of search engines.

Only downside is when not all of the computers have the updates installed. I would also like to be more familiar with everything it does because it's obvious that I'm not using it to its full potential.
We are still testing this product, so I don't have issues at this time.

I find it was difficult to keep up with updating classes as I teach three classes of six, three classes of seventh, and three classes of eighth grade. It was a bit of a learning curve learning how to import and save the kids. Also sometimes it was a bit of a pain in the neck if another teacher in the middle school did not release the students in their class. Then I would have to call him or her and ask them to dismiss that class from their roster.

The selection of individual screens and releasing of remote control can be clunky and non-responsive sometimes crashing the interface.
Remote controlling and logging on as admin to multiple desktops from the Tech Console can be tricky if desktops screens are different size/resolution.

Some latency while watching my student's screens. I needed to refresh sometimes to see the current activity of my students. Sending message to individual students was not effective in my own experience.

I wish the keystroke alerts was better in tune with what students are actually looking up. I was getting hundreds of emails every day just because some words were within words for the list I had added on the Keystroke Alert.

We have had trouble with installation. On our laptops and with our network, we need to have our IT people cooperate, whic has been challenging. Lenovo's tech support is awesome, but we reached a point where they couldn't help us and our IT department was reluctant to help.

Honesty, the front-end work, loading the students into the site, this does take time for each class. However, once the kids are loaded for the year, it's done and you do not have to think of it again. Also, I would love a face to face training to really dig deep into ALL the benefits of Lanschool, there are so many and I feel like I only touch on a few.
It is often hard to navigate from one student to the next.  I often have to reload in order to do so.  Perhaps there is a better way to make the transition.

We're now using more Chromebooks than PC or Mac laptop.  Teachers have complained to us that LanSchool functions differently on a Chromebook than on a desktop and that they wish the features would be identical between devices.  Furthermore, they say LanSchool on Chromebooks is "glitchy" functioning on one Chromebook  but not on another.

Every time I go to use LanSchool at the beginning of the year I need to get assistance setting it up. I wish this was easier to navigate. Also when I switch between classes, my LanSchool often freezes. Maybe there is something I am missing or a reason that this happens, but this can get old when I can’t monitor my next class because my screen is frozen.

Assembling classes for multiple different teachers can be a bit of a pain. Set up and dumping fresh installs into  Channel 0 or Channel 1 and then having to differentiate what computer is what can be confusing for teachers the first time during setup. Also from a Tech perspective setup using the scripts is too technical. There should be an easy Quick Start Distribution tool that just allows me to say "Put These Next Computers Into This Room with these settings" as opposed to opening archaic config files. Also, website blocking on computers doesn't always work well even though we have computer updates turned off so compatibility issues shouldn't be happening. I ran a LanSchool software update and web blocking no longer works. Computers are blocked from updating browsers and OS so this is definitely a LanSchool issue.

Sometimes there is a lag in releasing control of machines. Also I have struggled with the installation & upgrading; if I do not remember or document the original installation location, then I cannot uninstall the software.

LanSchool seems desperately slow, even with computers in their most up to date state.
I wish in the right moment, a LanSchool staffer was with me in the lab.  Trying to monitor logging in, huge lag, Refresh tool does not work at a good pace.  Now that we've switched to Win 10, showing the teacher's screen to students results in a black screen, rather than the program.  The same can happen when remote controlling.   Videos can't be played from the lead computer to students' without it being downloaded ahead of time.
It's a lot of frustrations that can't all be blamed on the set up of our lab.

There's not a whole lot I don't like. Sometimes there are little things like the histories not being saved, but overall LanSchool is a positive experience. I'm not sure how class lists can be put to their best use... but that's not really a dislike.

We have more than 200 laptops (3 or 4 different models) in our school and it seems that lanschools do not work as good with wireless connections.  With wired computers Lanschools works great but not with wireless.  The teachers with laptops can only see few of those laptops in their Lanschools teachers console.

Set up is tedious.  We use Infinite campus and the importing, exporting, changing file names, renaming, etc. for each class (including when you switch at semester) takes extra time.  I have yet to figure out how to make the web site limiting work.  I have desktops, but when on Chromebooks, the capabilities are more limited.
It lags alot mostly when the students are displaying something for me on my laptop that is really bad because actually its the main point for its operations.

As the technology specialists in the building the process to roster the students into classes is cumbersome. We use and SIS called Option C and we have to manipulate the data a lot in order to get it ready to put into LanSchool. Not to mention that when students rotate classes at the trimester we are doing the same thing again. It would be beneficial to have bulk scheduling options, such as all of homeroom 8A roster, can be copied over to other classes, with a click of a couple buttons. Not having to repeat them over and over on a spreadsheet.

There are many upsides to LANschool, but if I had to point out a shortcoming, it would be the occasional freezing, especially when controlling multiple machines at once. When LANschool freezes, I find the only recourse to get it functioning again is to kill the process in the task manager. Beyond that, I suppose the UI has a fuzzy look that makes it feel like outdated software. When controlling a machine, the action is choppy, where other management software Ive used provide a fluid control experience.

I'm still not sure how to create class lists for different classes, but I know it is possible. FOrtunately, my lab has 35 desktop computers so I don't need to switch class lists, but our other teachers will need this. I'll be researching it soon!

Because of all the additional features offered (good thing), it can make it complex trying to figure out how to control everything that is able to be controlled. Sometimes the options can seem to be buried in the software and difficult to find. That said, the support staff is more than happy to answer questions that resolve this issue.

It was very slow to maneuver at first and the website would freeze. The application wouldn't work so I discontinued using it  until I contacted your support team.

I also think the instant messaging can be much more efficient. Perhaps a flashing symbol to show when students have messaged you.
Students can disconnect their Chromebooks.
Wishing it were easier to configure for teachers, functionality on chromeOS is limited (Google's fault not Lanschool's).

What I dislike is that the student you view are specific to you only. VE Teachers and Support Facilitators should have access to all students on their grade level or all ESE Students. When you are testing or completing practice test, you can see all students.

name: Lumio
pros_raw: My favorite thing about Lumio is that my students are able to have the presentation directly in front of them. They can zoom in on the part they want to focus on, interact with the lesson, and receive immediate feedback on their thoughts instead of waiting until the end.  I also love that all students can participate and are given a voice.
Lumio helps me as a teacher to structure my class and it makes my lesson interactive.

Ever since a teacher in my school board introduced me to Lumio - I have been in awe and amazed at all that it offers and how it engages my virtual students each day! I wish we had this tool during the pandemic, and I am so grateful for it now that I have been volunteering to present Lumio PD at TDSB and city wide education technology conferences (one is this Friday April 19, Unleashing Learning, downtown Toronto). I have to let other educators, administrators and school teams know how incredible and transformative Lumio is especially since our school board focus is UDL and fostering inclusion and accessibility - ensuring all students are met where they are at and facilitating belonging and capacity. I love I can use LUMIO to easily combine digital AT and UDL strategies, transforming my previously one sided virtual lessons into active, inclusive and collaborative learning experiences that engage students in real time, from multiple entry points, using a variety of presentation tools (ex. Google slides, PPT, SMART, PDF) on any device, from any location (they are at home). Using this SMART learning management system daily over the past year with my virtual DD ISP students, I'm thrilled to unveil the magic of LUMIO—a revolutionary digital learning tool that transcends boundaries.  LUMIO provides a way for students to seamlessly respond, share ideas and interact in person or virtually from any device, relevant to their specific IEP goals and starting points. I love to share with others that LUMIO enhances accessibility and ignites both independent and collaborative learning, and model ways to integrate LUMIO, UDL and AT to bridge the gaps for either in-person or virtual learning. I also incorporate AAC and Boardmaker 7 symbols into the Lumio slides and activities I make, which promotes essential communication skills for my students and empowers their voice and choice. I am so grateful for this tool daily, and even hope to pursue all 3 Lumio ambassador roles.

The best part of Lumio is how teacher friendly it is. I also like the pre-made templates. I feel like the templates have just the right amount of information. Not to much to be overwhelming, and yet open ended enough that I can make small changes to the templates and have a brand new lesson. I also like how I can switch from whole group to whiteboard to just me presenting in the middle of Lumio lessons.

Lumio makes it easy for teachers to quickly add their ideas to premade resources. Start with your own pdf or PowerPoint and with a click of a button your pages are now interactive for groups, individuals or a whole class discussion.  Once your activities are set students join with a code and enter your virtual classroom which is fully of interaction. I love that the code stays the same but you have the power to reset it as needed. As a teacher I appreciate the ability to provide my students real time feed back.Lumio also makes it easy guide my students during their lesson or release them for individual activities at their pace.    They also have a recording feature for each slide for recording audio for direction or even translation.  I enjoy using Lumio and their company is great at listening to their customers talk feedback on what features to bring it next. Happy to be a Lumio teacher and share it's with my colleagues frequently.

I love that I can upload documents from my computer, Google drive, Canvas, and other platforms to Lumio and edit to add games, assessments, and many other rich content to enhance my lessons and engage students throughout them. I also love that it has options to convert pages into individual pdfs and not having to make any more copies. Lastly, for now, I can use any of their presentations and resources, edit them or simply integrate them into my lessons, to adapt them to my students'  varied learning proficiencies. Thank you Lumio! #GoLumio

There are so many fun ways to involve students in lessons, through collaboration, individual worksheets and group work. There are also fantastic game based activities that leverage teacher's content to effectively stimulate learning and revision. Using the Response tool, teachers are able to create full assessments and get immediate results which can be downloaded as an Excel spreadsheet. This data can then be imported into an LMS and used for reporting, making tests and report writing a breeze! I am thrilled about the Immersive Reader, too, as it enables students with language or reading difficulty access the lessons. Lumio lessons are also very easy to create, and there is a vast library of ready made resources and lessons to use and personalise to fit the teacher and students.  Best of all, Lumio can be accessed on any device, meaning teachers can teach from remote venues, in class and connect with students remotely too. And all this can be accessed on a mobile device, laptop, desktop, Apple product and SMART Interactive display. BONUS: IT IS FREE! It is easy to use from a student and teacher perspective, and is siple to implement in any educational setting. With the continuous support available through local and international support teams and the embedded help videos, there is never a time that you are left to figure things out on your own. Personally I use it all the time, in every lesson I present. It integrates well with Google and Microsoft. It also works with any student device with a browser. It also has integration with Canva! Simply an AWESOME edtech tool NO teacher should be without!!

The most helpful thing so far is the ease of creating lessons that are fun and engaging. My district hosted a summer conference (DIGIN 24- Bibb County) where I learned so much more. The Lumio presenters were awesome. (Michelle and Michael were marvelous! )
I have already started planning games and graphic organizers to incorporate into my lessons. I love how Lumio allows me to collect data real time and help me with providing corrective feedback discreetly.

Lumio is very easy to implement, adapting resources and materials we already have, it just elevates our intruction providing fun activities for students. Very user friendly, webinars and training videos help put together quick and effective lessons.

As a LUMIO ambassador, what I like most about the app is its intuitive and easy-to-use interface, which allows users to organize their tasks and projects efficiently. Additionally, I highly value its real-time collaboration features, which facilitate teamwork.

Lumio provides opportunities to integrate ready made content, games, and other interactive elements to presentations in PPT and Google Slides.  It is very easy to use and implement.  It also offers opportunities to make PDF's not only editable, but individulized for students and professional learners alike.  While Lumio is an even more amazing tool when used in conjunction with a SMART Interactive Flat Panel, it is quite powerful even when used on its own.

I love that Lumio allows you to use so many digital resources in one platform. So many times I have to app smash to get the final product, with Lumio I am able to customize my lessons to meet the needs of all students. I have access to graphic organizers, formative assessments, manipulatives, and videos all in one space. My students love it and the teachers in my school are using it more than any resource we have! Great product and it just continues to get better and better.

Lumio is an incredible resource to use in the classroom! It is easy to create and implement in every day lessons. My students LOVE to do Lumio lessons and ask daily to do it. There are so many options including games, quizzes, immersive reader, manipulatives, and already made lessons.
I like the ease of setting up lessons and adding to lessons for my students. I also like to import the lessons onto my Googleclassroom.
Every time I use the game templates in Lumio, like Game Show, my students jump up and down with excitement.  I'm not kidding. They love it! Thanks, Lumio, for making learning so engaging.

In these days personalized learning is really important. LUMIO makes it possible allowing them to access the activities on their devices. Children can learn wherever they are,  whenever they want. Lessons are collaborative and enjoyable. Children really like learning and are the important part of teaching.

Lumio allows teachers to bring all their content together in one place (slides, pdfs, videos and more). Then teachers can share the presentation with students and have everyone interact with the content together. Not only is it amazing for engagement and collaboration, but the Lumio platform offers so many built in graphic organizers, manipulatives, and game based activities to take lessons to the next level.
What I like best about alumni is that it allows teachers to create interactive and immersive games for students.
Ready made resources are the most helpful.

I love that I can quickly upload my presentation, and combine them with PDFs, video, and interactive assessments to make a shareable activities that engage my learners. I also love that I can create libraries of content that make it easy to launch lessons on the go and pivot efficiently and effectively.
Templates, game-based activities, integration with Canva

There are so many things Lumio does well, but the interactive elements that can be combined with collecting formative assessment data are my favorite. With every student interacting with the lesson from their individual devices formative data is easy to collect. What's even better is that the students simply think they are playing a game.

Lumio shines as an indispensable tool for educators seeking seamless ease of use and enhanced student engagement in the classroom setting. With its intuitive interface, collaborative features, and interactive learning elements, this product is a valuable asset for fostering a dynamic and interactive learning environment for all ages.

Teacher / Student led resources can be created
User friendly software
Users can sync their work to Google Drive and save Lumio files
It integrates with our MX86 Smart TVs and Smart Notebook
Easy to use - web based - accounts are connected via
What I like best is the different tools that you can do to enhance any learning activity. I love how my students love the interactive games and activities.
Ready made instant lessons, ability to allow students to work together and engange in the lesson
The option for the teacher and student´s pace
Easiest and most engaging way to enhance lessons in the classroom!

I love Lumio because it's free for teachers!
#GoLumio!!
Lumio is super easy to create an engaging gaming experience that will draw in students to learn the content used.

I love the way it can support various planning tools including SMART Notebook, PowerPoint and PDFs, seamlessly bringing the tools together to create a unique learning experience. I also love the way it has the learner at the centre of all it has to offer, especially the interactive and engaging games that are perfect for retrieval and procedural practice.

The pupils work with great motivation thanks to the games and the playful environment. Lumio is easy for students to use.
Lumio is easy to use for teachers: games can be programmed in minutes. Students can connect to it even from home, great in these times of a pandemic. I couldn't do without Lumio!

Smart Learning Suite Online offers a great way to include both in-person and online learners at the same time.  Collaboration can even occur between the two!  I love the way that I can "see" what my virtual students are working on.  The games are a quick and easy way to add more active participation and can be made in minutes!  My students love them!
I love that the students can interact with the pages and I get results on what they are doing. I love that I can see in real time how they are doing especially using Shout it out!

Lumio is a fantastic cloud-based platform I use daily to keep my students engaged in math content. It truly is a one-stop shop. I love being able to import my own materials and add a little Lumio spice to make the lessons collaborative, fun and a tool I can use to gather valuable feedback.

It is interactive and has an incredible amount of variety.  You are able to bring in existing lesson plans and jazz them up, use readily made materials that have been vetted, or create an awesome lesson and activity from scratch.

I love that Lumio puts student engagement and learning first in what can be accomplished in the classroom! Hugely beneficial software and program in combination with Smart Technologies to really redefine what learning looks like in the classroom!
The ease in setting up & configuring classes; creating, modifying & sharing activities or lessons; following students learning; collaborating with others (teachers & students)

That I can upload documents and work along side my students. I like the fact that I don't loose any down time. Also, that I can add documents instantly. I do like microsoft edge that I can highlight, add text, and draw. What I prefer mostly is that it's less steps than that of Lumio.

Lumio is an amazing product.  It is so versatile and allows me to work with the many different subject areas that I teach.  I am able to adapt many of the templates in the library to really be able to improve engagement with my students.  It has been a joy to implement this product in my courses.

I like that you can upload all content that has already been created. I also like that you can add it all together in one presentation. Lumio provides game-based learning and quick formative assessments that allow teachers to adjust their lesson on the fly!
LUmio is a great tool for building interactive lessons, resources, daily charts, and managing routines. I love how  Lumio is available everywhere on any device.

During a whole group lesson, I can present information to students on my interactive board and students can see it on their laptops too so they are more engaged. I can walk around the classroom and see which students are understanding the work and which need more help quickly. I can also share student work with the rest of the class to demonstrate a concept or idea.
The interactivity and variety of tools available for teachers

Lumio is interactive; you can use existing lessons or incorporate your slides. It is very engaging for students and has many different options for interaction. I love how you can incorporate Youtube videos to enhance learning

I like the fact that I can create a presentation that contains several different pieces of content. I can use a slideshow, videos, interactive activities, graphic organizers, and I can add a whiteboard on the fly if needed. I also like that I don't have to import the entire slideshow or Lumio lesson. I can just choose the slides/activities I want to use.

As a classroom teacher I am always in search of creative ways to keep my students engaged and excited about learning. Lumio/SMART has brought such joy into our classrooms. Imagine 17 students vigorously reaching for their devices in anticipation of joining the math review game for the day. This is the reality of my classroom after utilizing the many resources provided. Students love the option to work independently or in groups.
The ability to quickly create materials to use live with students and the ability to add a diverse set of interactive elements that keep students engaged

I love that you can find lessons that are already complete and that you can use your slideshow to make your digital interactive presentations to use in your classroom. The students find the games fun and engaging, and I can gear them directly towards my standards.
The many options for interactivity. You can do it as a large group, small group, or individually. I can also collect data on my student's work and progress.
cons_raw: Lumio runs slow on our network at times, but the good far out weighs the bad.
It would benefit with AI-integration to get full potential of the product.

The only features (and I have given feedback to ask if these can be developed) I wish Lumio had was a crop button, the ability to work on/open more than one lesson at a time, being able to save a photo as a background to a slide, how long it takes to download or upload a lesson, access to shared libraries (not a cost my school will pay), the ability to download student answers and responses afterwards instead of having to screen shot each page or it may get erased, and I wish the hard work I spend animating my google slides and powerpoints would remain after uploading them to Lumio (please add a feature that allows gifs and animations it keeps my students much more focused especially during reading (ex. blending segmenting words with an animated hand moving from letter to letter). Other than that I love how interactive Lumio is!

My only struggle is that there is no way to save student work. If we are working on a Lumio together than the students work is not saved. It is only available while the Lumio is open. I am not able to review their work at a later time or even use Lumio to show growth over time.
The only thing that I believe would make Lumio the perfect tool would be the ability to see the student thumbnails of work instead of having to click on each students name.
There is nothing to dislike about it, for it enriches my lessons delivery while actively engaging  my students in their learning processes. Nothing to dislike at all! #Go Lumio
A big downside is that it requires internet access, which means teachers in remote areas with spotty or poor internet connectivity might struggle with accessing lessons.
I honestly have to say I haven't come arcross anything I dislike yet.
A way to connect with our gradebook and the option to have classes for departamentilized teachers.
As for what I like least, I could mention that some advanced functions may require a learning curve to fully utilize, but overall, LUMIO is an excellent project management tool.
The least helpful part of Lumio is that it is occasionally unreliable.  With that being said, the support is always very responsive and quick to work on solutions.
At times the new Editable PPT feature does not work, but I understand it's in Beta so I know it will only get better with time!
I would love for the games to have the ability to have immersive reader or to record your voice.
I really don't have anything to add here.
When my students don't have devices with them, we have to skip those student-led activities until a later time.
Nothing. It is perfect and improving day by day
It's easy to use, but there is a learning curve as the interface is unique.
I do not dislike anything about Lumio.  It is a great tool for the classroom.
The downsides of Lumio are trouble downloading.
More options for activities where I can export the data for formative assessment
Slow upload sometimes and not a lot of space for free account
Some of the interactive games do not allow easy access to the student results
Lumio attempts to bridge platforms for students and educators, but is not quite there yet.  Limitations on what can be imported and tools to use during presentation can be frustrating for users.
Not much - maybe some resources or games that can be aimed at 16+

I usually don't have issues with Lumio. the onlu thing that I could consider a downside is that it doesnt allow you to be a teacher of multiple course at the same time. You have to keep changing the Class ID.
Not being able to be logged into more than one screen as a teacher
It depends so much on the connecction. It would be perfect to have offline option.
Nothing!  We love Lumio and the continued enhancements.
I think Lumio is perfect, and there is nothing to say about it.
would love to have a spinner, rolling dice, etc

There are some aspects of MSART Notebook that are brilliant including various fonts, screen shaed, timers that can be added to individual pages. Having this accessible on Lumio will be a game changer!

I would like Lumio and Notebook to be at the same level of use. But I know that SMART is working on it and we see new things appearing very often. I look forward to being able to join sounds for words or pictures.

There is nothing I dislike.  You do need to spend some time learning the system.  I do like how I can use my Google Slides presentations that I already have.  You can simply "pop" in the games and update easily.
There aren't enough widgets to use on theboard when creating, and when you bring in a notebook file it doesn't work the same way it does when you are using Notebook without Lumio
The only current downside is that the math text feature is functional for the teacher only. I would prefer that my students have access to the equation editor also.

I really do not see a downside.  When getting others started on Lumio I feel like the most significant challenge I have faced is helping them not to feel overwhelmed.  There are so many useful features and some teachers do not know where to start.  I like to start them off with Flip Out and once they are as hooked as I am I show them the vast array of innovative teaching activities.

With the exception of some internet issues or drops at rare times, Lumio is forever growing and making changes at the request of teachers and educators around the globe. There is much to be improved just like any piece of tech, but they are well on their way!
Well... er... Do I have to write anything? It is challenging to find something that I dislike of Lumio...

That there are several steps to get everything uploaded. Perhaps, look at other similar programs that educators use. Less steps.  I would like to see how I can make the assignments only where students can answer in the space provided and not able to just scribble all over the documents.

Since my division does not allow us to use the Google integration feature, I am only allowed to upload already made PowerPoint via PDF.  Since they are imported as images, it is challenging when I find an error.  I have to re-upload that page or the entire presentation.  It is really frustrating when that happens.  I wish there were an easier way to import presentations.
The downsides of Lumio are some of its editing features. It is sometimes best to create slides in another platform and then upload them into Lumio than to start from scratch in the Lumio platform.
I cannot wait for Lumio to have more of the SMART Notebook features. It's growing very quickly and I have no doubt it will be as powerful as Notebook is in the future.
I wish I could change the background color when I create an activity. It would be nice if there were a set of simple black and white icons to use in designing activities that I could color.
There are a lot of shortcomings with the software.  The latest update opens the keyboard in the upper left corner every time I try to type something.
The downside is having colleagues use Lumio; they seem overwhelmed with all the features. Another challenge is getting it to work correctly on Chromebooks.
I dislike that I can only add videos from YouTube. I'd like to be able to add vides from MyVRSpot as YouTube is blocked in my district.
I would love more ready-made resources aligned with our new assessments
Lumio needs to increase it's co-teacher abilities so teachers can collaborative create, present, and collect/review instructional data as a partnership.
I wish the library was a little larger. I know this is a fairly new process, but looking forward to finding more engaging activities for each grade level to enrich and support our students learning.

There is not a lot in the library that can be used with higher education. I also would like to see some of the old smart activities updated to match the Lumio look. Like when you look in the library you can tell which items are old school smart and which ones are Lumio.

name: Brainscape
pros_raw:
There is so much to include here.

Customer service is best I have ever experienced in any platform by far. Quick replys, very helpful, very respectful, 10/10 all around.

The thoughout system of this study guide exceeds what I thought possible.

Flash cards that will show back up based of your rank of undersatdning you pick after answering. It's geniuse.

So easy to set up and implement your content or even find other content that is already organized in what you may be studying.

I found Brainscape during my course. From someone who makes content on youtube that gives tips on how to pass course. Now use it more than the course itself for studying.

The youtube content creator made sections for each of the 4 textbook / 4 module. Broke that down into flashcard decks for each chapter, multiple exam practice question decks, math / calculation only decks, overview of textbook / module deck. It is a better source of information that my actual course. Easy integration.

I use Brainscape everyday and the majority of my studying goes to it.

I cannot express enough how fortunate and blessed I am to have found this platform. Trust me it is worth your time.

The "real world" application of learning flashcards is replicated with the added benefit of AI for intelligent learning. Whenever I am using physical flashcards, I always have a few nailed, but they keep coming around giving me a false sense of mastery through each round in the deck. I like how I am able to rate my knowledge of a card when it pops up and if I am not learning it it appears more frequently.

Also, this makes studying with friends much easier as we have the same deck of cards but not the SAME deck of cards.

I'm a first year medicine student, so learning biology and anatomy for me is really complicated. I love that brainscape offers fast and easy access to cards, i can make my own cards/decks and easily repeat my knowledge. The best thing is that you can rate any of your answers based on knowledge from 1-5 and it will repeat the card to you again in some time if you don't know it that well.

Spaced repetition and active recall in one place. Two most effective study options I believe. Also, vast array of curated flashcards so I don't have to worry about making them on my own. Also - night mode is ery helpful during long night study hours. Very easy to use and navigate, also great customer support - they repsond quickly in case of any issues/requests.

As a foreign language teacher, I first chose using Brainscape over Quizlet because of two important features: First, Brainscape allows you to include audio recordings, so I add my recorded voice to each vocab word. I also add images to help students who are more visual learners. Second, Brainscape allows me to observe how much my students are using the flashcards, so I can assign them and give the students credit for doing them. I also like the features of creating reverse cards and duplicating decks.

I’ve been using Brainscape since I started med school, after a professor recommended it to us. It’s been about two years now, and Brainscape has become my go-to study tool. The 5-Step spaced repetition system is highly effective for both short- and long-term test prep. Plus, creating flashcards makes it much easier to memorize and grasp concepts, as it forces you to condense and process the information.

One feature I love is the offline mode, which lets you download your classes and decks. It’s a lifesaver because it allows you to study anywhere, anytime—even from your phone!

Brainscape is super user friendly. Even the free version is good.

My favorite thing is that you can look up for classes and set up reminders to study.

Study sessions are not too long so it's so easy to just do one when you have a 5 minute to spare. Also, I looooove the phone app. I think it makes it so easy.

Brainscapes is amazing because...
- Creating flashcards is so easy, you can type them in or use ai to use pre created flashcards
- You can download decks and study on the go
- You learnt the material quickly and it stays in your long term memory as you come back to it often (I do about 5 min a day)
- It has a platform in which you can ask the developers for features, they will respond and hopefully will put that feature on their checklist, or if not will ask for more specifics, or explain why they can't.
- You can share your flashcards with friends easily and split the workload of creating the flashcards up.

What I like best about Brainscape ist that -unlike other classic, or simple flashcard apps - it has a 5 category rating system or spaced repetition, which ensures that cards you know least will be tested more frequently. This process allows you to slowly transition content from your short-term to long-term memory.

It further allows you to find complete courses on any subject. I am using it both for my Spanish language learning and WSET level 2 exam study and am thrilled to not have to laboriously create the cards myself.

Last but not least, I like that you can sync the cards to all your devices and even export /download flashcards into spreadsheets.

I used Brainscape a lot while studying for my WSET Level 2 exam. The flashcards were super helpful for learning and remembering all the tricky wine terms, regions, and concepts. What I loved most was the spaced repetition—it really kept me focused on the stuff It was easy to use and perfect for studying anywhere, and I honestly don’t think I would have been as prepared without it.

I love the spaced learning that Brainscape utilizes. Student confidence level in their flashcards will determine how often a flashcard reappears in their rotation. So far, I have built one flashcard deck for the unit my AP students are working on. It was very easy to set up on the website and very easy to have my students access the classroom I created on Brainscape. Students all joined and many of them have downloaded the app on their phone and they can access these decks whenever they have some time available to study. I have recommended this program to multiple AP teachers at my school already.

Easy to create an accont and easy to use the learning material provided. I also love that is free of ads.
I use their products on a daily basis. Their customer support is excellent.
Learning material is up to date with the vendor material. Has so many features you can set to make the learning experience unique. I will continue to use the service to learn more about IT Certifications. I'm in love with Brainscape I'm so happy I found them! Thank you Brainscape!

I was completely panicked about the WSET III and huge amount of information to consume until I found Brainscape. It makes it so easy to build a habit of daily practice and repetition. I love the progressive feature and easy user experience. I will 100% recommend it to future WSET III students.

Brainscape is one of the best flashcard applications on the market, from using it I have achieved further success than any other app. The flashcards make it easy to learn from the different topics within my area of study and this has helped develop me even more in order to pass the exams needed to advance in my career. In total I have been using Brainscape for an average of 30-60 mins a day and this has helped dramatically.

I personally like the features of where I can say how confident I am on certain areas and certain cards, these then get revisted therefore helping me with my memory of certain topics.

Overall amazing revision tool!

I enjoy the flashcard feature where you select 1 (not at all) - 5 (perfectly) based on your confidence in the answer. This allows me to see my mastery in the course and move at my pace! Plus 10 cards at a time keeps me engaged and can freely pass free time.

I primarily use Brainscape to study for my WSET Level 2 exam, and the spaced repetition flashcard system has been very effective. I like how the topics are neatly separated by WSET levels, making it easy to focus on specific areas.

Brainscape is a fantastic memory training tool with both free and paid components that suit any level of investment. On the free side: flash cards are easy to create, there's no limit on # of decks (subject areas) you can create, and the UI is clean and easy to move through. I like how it defaults to 10 cards a session and allows you to self assess your knowledge with a simple 5-point scale for future follow up. I have yet to use the lesson sharing tools but I can see distinct value as an F&B manager creating lessons for my bar staff on our signature cocktails that can be shared across the staff.
On the paid side: I love that there are created decks in a huge variety of subjects from certified education providers. It helps me fill out the decks I create for myself being able to search for cocktails, wine sommelier subject matter etc.

I am a student of WSET 4 Diploma course and I found the support offered by Brainscape team pretty good! It helped me to focus on the necessary notions to remember for each different chapter/wine style and guided me through the correct way to understand questions, organize answers and being precise in the set of info to focus on.

I really like studying with brainscape for my WSET 4 diploma  :
- to review random topics from several chapters. It helps not forget the first chapters compared to a linery study
- to study on the go / commuting/ everywhere you want with your phone
- show you cards again that you rated lower in term of knowledge

My 10 year old daughter is in 5th grade. I use Brainscape pro to help him maximize his study time. It works really well! Brainscape is a simple and intuitive app that I recommend to parents who want to help their children learn to learn :)

Brainscape flashcards are a game changer for studying. I found them to be an amazing resource for my WSET 2 wine exam and I am now using them for WSET 3 as well! It's so helpful to understand how my confidence level increases after I study the flash cards.

I love using Brainscape for studying. It's very user-friendly, and I appreciate the rating system that focuses on missed answers while filtering out the correct ones, making studying more efficient. I strongly recommend this app for everyone!
Brainscape has helped me stay sharp and prepared for my securities exam.  I like that I can access it via my tablet, which allows me study anytime I have down time.

The flashcard content is very current to latest edition of the WSET Level 3 text.  The site is easy to access and get right into studying.  The self-rating system for each flash card is very helpful so that you can see exactly how well you know a given section.  The dashboard tools really helped me plan my studying to make sure I had time to get through everything before the exam.
The interface is wonderful, as is the way they update according to scientific evidence on learning methods. The iOS app is also very good, fast, and practical.

Brainscape is a wonderful flashcard app that allows you to search for resources ranging from a variety of topics such as the topics you have A-level subjects, all the way to courses you have in universities or college including medicine and engineering. You review a round of flashcards that alongside tells your confidence level which is great for understanding where you stand. You are also timed when rating the flashcards, which can tell you how quickly you can recall information. The customer support is quick and efficient. I found myself getting replies in emails as soon as possible, and they came with the help I needed. It is overall, a save for energy and time!
Everything you need to do study questions preparing for your exam. The amount of content to study on is incredible. I highly recommend using brainscape.
I love the app. I decided to splurge and get the pro version for a month, and the AI flash card maker saves me actual hours worth out typing.

Easy way to take your flashcards on the go.
Rating how well you know each helps review more what you are weaker on.

I have started using Brainscape for the WSET level 3 study materials, and I'm impressed. I just recently started studying, found Brainscape and downloaded the app and I've started using the flashcards. The flashcards are designed in such a way that totally makes sense to my visual learning style. I like the color-coded buttons where I rate how well I know the material and I like the flow of how to use the cards . I appreciate the pictures, as it brings concepts and terms to life, and the bullet pointed/numbered answers helps chunk the information in my brain. Also, the key bolded words within the answers is SUPER helpful, is that is how I make things stand out when I take notes on my own. These flashcards are going to save me a lot of time as they are designed in a way that makes sense to me and how I learn, so I won't have to create my own. I am excited to have found this product.

I use several different flashcard apps to study and teach Japanese, but I keep coming back to Brainscape.

Ease of Use - It's extremely easy to create new flashcard decks. You can easily create cards by hand, or import CSV and TXT files to instantly create decks. Other apps are not as user-friendly, so I keep coming back to Brainscape.

During class, I just take my notes directly into Brainscape which ensures I will come back to review and retain the material. Seriously, how often do you go back and read that Google Doc full of notes?

Algorithm helps you learn faster - You can choose between "random"d "progressive" mode. Both can be useful to give you a good balance of repetition and challenge. I am learning characters so much quicker thanks to their algorithm.

Better Habits - Stop doomscrolling, start Brainscaping! I feel much happier and more productive since I decided to study my Brainscape flashcards when the urge to scroll hits.

Awesome community - There are tons of classes/decks made by the Brainscape community available for free. I've learned about wine, emergency management, health, street smarts, and technology because of free Brainscape classes.

Teacher's pet - I've shared my Brainscape decks with my professor and classmates. It's a great resource to share with your learning community!
Extensive content, easy to access everywhere, allows to see progress which helps keep me motivated for my studies. Has been a great partner while studying for my WSET exams!

Effective and productive model to memorize information
Easy to create flashcards with unique and simple formatting options
Aesthetically pleasing interface
Responsive customer support

Using Brainscape significantly boosted my confidence and knowledge going into the WSET Level 2 exam. The combination of spaced repetition and high-quality content made my study sessions more productive and efficient. I felt well-prepared and ultimately performed excellently on the exam passing with distinction.

The most helpful thing about Brainscape is its effective algorithm for optimizing card exposure times based on my responses and its confidence rating feature. I also like that most of the app’s content is free. In addition, I found the pre-made flashcards very helpful.
What I like best about Brainscape is how its certified courses directly enhance my professional skills. The platform's structured learning approach allows me to quickly master job-relevant topic.

Brainscape has been critical in my studies for a certification exam coming up. I wish I had known about this program during my earlier years in high school and college because It's an excellent source, much better than Quizlet, in my opinion.  It's easy to use and implement into my study routine, and I love the way the training is set up. I also really appreciate that, even in Free mode, we can create as many cards as needed for study. It's been a huge help in helping me prepare for this big exam and I'll continue to use it for other certifications in the future!

Really great app to learn. Learning through flash cards, its a great idea. It will help the professionals for time management, to do work and study together. I used to study during my break time at work. Highly recommended..

Brainscape is very versatile and easy to use. It is regularly updated and they seem to listen to customer feedback. For example. the option of being able to reverse questions and answers was added following feedback. As well as pre-made decks, there is the also the possibility of producing your own and this is what I use it mainly for, although i use a mixture of both.  I like the way you can now record directly onto a card using your microphone. Before, you had to upload an MP3. This is an example of how it is always evolving and improving. The customer services is also great. Whenever I have had a query or a problem, someone gets back to me quickly to resolve it.

I discovered this app when I was studying for my AWS Certification and it really helped a lot. The best things about are that you can use it for free for your own flashcards and you can use it on multiple devices, I primarily used it on my desktop but also on my Iphone to keep studying on breaks during my day. Besides this I also discovered that they have user created decks which I'm exploring and they are really helpful.

I've recently began studying for my WSET1 for a career move and the flashcards have proven invaluable. I genuinely love how they have you rank how well you know the answers so the harder questions come up more often, allowing you to better retain the information!

I especially love that they offer a lifetime membership option for premium features.

It's nothing to brag about, but I had never seen the textbook three days before my WSET Level 2 exam. Then I saw a recommendation for Brainscape, and similarly in the past, I studied with a flash card and passed many exams, so I paid and used it right away. I will also use this app for the next level. They offer a lot of sets of study materials not only WSET, but even tarot cards! Haha. So many useful resources that I can use, so I am thinking to pay for lifetime usage too.

I have used a number of Flash Card apps but Brainscape is the one that I love the most! For me,  a flashcard app needs to be clean with easy controls, meaning I am less likely to get annoyed using the interface and that the information is clear and easy to read.

I study for a lot of industry exams and Brainscape allows me to create clear and concise cards for each subject. The algorithm they use for spaced repetition is the best, bar non. Its like it can read my mind! The grading system for cards is great as it really allows me to focus on the right cards at the right time.

The platform is great as it allows me to study wherever I am, even if I only have a few more ns to spare. I have consciously made an effort to use Brainscape to study rather than mindlessly scrolling and it has helped wonders! Don't hesitate, give it a try.

As someone who values efficiency and effectiveness in learning, Brainscape’s use of spaced repetition has helped me a lot!

Brainscape has made studying more efficient and enjoyable for me. The combination of spaced repetition, customizable content, and robust analytics makes it an indispensable tool for anyone serious about mastering their subject matter. As both a student and educator I am using Brainscape Pro’s features and couldn't think of not using them now.
Both the web app and mobile app work great, and the learning experience is seamless.
The ability to have cognitive learning, bringing what you are least profficent at to the front.

It is great for studying, learning and memorizing, I think it's better than Anki because it is a lot more user-friendly. The possibility to upload excel sheets directly into flashcards is SO SO helpful, what a great feature! I am very satisfied with Brainscape, would highly recommend it to whoever asked.

I started studying wine as a sommelier in Korea. I was recommended the “brainscape” app, which proved to be helpful for my wine studies. Exams like WSET Level 4 were too vast and challenging to study from books alone, but thanks to this app, I was able to save a tremendous amount of time. Additionally, being able to review unfamiliar questions multiple times was incredibly helpful for memorization.
