Using the app greatly enhanced my wine knowledge and instilled confidence in my wine studies.

The “brainscape” app is an excellent tool for studying specialized knowledge and memorization. It efficiently helps to learn the extensive amount of information required for exams like WSET Level 4. It is particularly recommended for those struggling with memorization. Personally, I received a lot of help in building my wine knowledge through this app. I highly recommend it to anyone interested in studying specialized knowledge or improving their memorization skills.

Brainscape's adaptive learning program helps me feel confident going into exams or related areas knowing I have practiced my weak spots multiple times. I also highly value that the program providers I am studying have approved decks, knowing that the content I am studying is legitimate. As an educator, I have recommended Brainscape to my students after having used it as a student myself. The mobile application (iPhone, iPad) versions are very user friendly. When studying for an exam, I use Brainscape multiple times a week, so to me it is a good value for the membership level I use (6 - 12 month.)

Brainscape has been very accessible and easy to use for a number of reasons. I really enjoy the fact that I can add pictures and edit the text. I have reached out to customer support one time while having this app for ~1 year and they were extremely responsive and helpful. I frequently use Brainscape, nearly every day, as well as those who also use my cards for studying.
cons_raw:
Honestly, it is hard to put my finger on anything I dislike.

If I had to pick something I wish they would incorporate is some way to buy certain content of flash card deck sets made to buy it once and have full access of it always.

Only way to do that is lifetime access, but for someone who is just looking for one set of content is a bit steep. But that is a very unique request and is just because I am in a course and only need full access to that material.

Very minimal issues and is the only improvment I could see for my situation.

I wish there was a way to reverse the cards, instead of having to make the same card twice (e.g. Q/A, A/Q or Term/Def, Def/Term). I also wish there were features for importing cards into a specific deck.
Can't wait for more AI tools. And the only thing that i'm missing right now is some kind of bookmark to mark any card i like or the option to print them out. At least i have not found it yet.

More advanced statistics model would be nice, for example how long did I study specific class/set. Also, ability to mix/add flashcards within a class between decks. You can't edit someone elses flashcards so if you spot something you would like to change, there is no way other than notifying that person - ability to duplicate and edit these for your own purpose would be nice.

But this is really just nitpicking.

Unfortunately, Brainscape has changed their policies. Now, for me to view my students' use of the flashcards, I would have to pay for an account for each of my students. That is not possible given limited school budgets. And now, quizlet offers automatically generated audio, so my recorded audio is not necessary. Ultimately, I continue to use Brainscape because I have created all these pre-made flashcards, but if I were to start all over again, I would use Quizlet.
I’m not a fan of how long it sometimes takes for the app to sync or download my classes. Also, you need to upgrade to Pro if you want to add images or reset your cards to start over.

Would be nice to be able to bold or underline words when creating cards.

When creating cards, you only have text, image or record option, but it would be great to have options like ; select the right answer, etc.

I also would love to schedule multiple reminders a day.

My only wish is that I could print of my cards which unfortunatly is not a feature at this time. It is currently on their backlog and hopefully will be created soon. Phones are not allowed at my school so not being able to print of my flashcards easily is quite a disadvantage.
They do have some work arounds like screenshotting and such but with the amount of flashcards I have this would take me years to complete.
As with most products, I find the pricing to be on the expensive side and think it's a pity you would have to pay the yearly cost (cheapest option) in one installment.
I honestly can’t think of anything negative to say—Brainscape was exactly what I needed for my studies
Some of the features are for pro users only such as viewing students' data or adding images to flashcards.
There is not one thing I can think of, would like to know if they have the service in Spanish?
There is nothing I dislike about Brainscape. It has been extremely useful to me.
Nothing it is persfect! I am really enjoying using it.
I honestly haven't found a dislike for this app. You get quality education at a fair price.
I find the subscription model a bit too expensive, especially for the monthly plan compared to the yearly option

The cost is a bit aggressive at first until you internalize the value in accessing decks from certified education providers. I wish there was a bit more functionality in formatting when creating cards so they can be set up to be a bit more visually appealing. Additionally, I wish there was better layout functions in the cards to address different ways to set up cards (definitions vs. question/answer, multiple choice etc.). But all in all, with a little more input I can get 90% of the way there in how I structure my decks.

It will be great to have some maps and diagrams.
A one to one virtual assistant to guide you through your doubts.
Could be useful to type answer to check if you answer it correctly. Not only in your head
I don't see any negative points in Brainscape :)
I don't like getting too many emails from Brainscape
I have not found anything negative about the app

If there is a downside its that its most effective if you're already familiar with the work. I would recommend one to become familiar with course content, and then use Brainscape to stay sharp and keep the information top of mind.
All of the flashcards are static, meaning no video or animation.  I wish they had more maps of the key wine regions.
The price ($) for Brazil is still a bit high for students.
The one thing I would suggest is if they can implement a reminder to review your flashcards so that you can keep your study streak, as someone like can often forget to use it everyday.
I didn't find any drawbacks or dislikes using brainscape.
Pro pricing. That's the only downside. Other than that, great.

Only being able to ask open-ended questions makes it hard to study for multiple choice at times.
If you pay monthly, it feels too expensive. Value for yearly is good.

I have just started using it so I haven't really found anything that I dislike, yet. What I can say is I really like the use of pictures to demonstrate the concepts/terms, as it brings the words to life for things I'm not ever going to get to see in person. I can't really say they should have more pictures because I've only been using the app a couple of weeks, but I can say that the pictures used this far have very useful. If Brainscape could consider the use of pictures to demonstrate the concept/term wherever possible/appropriate, it would be great! I can picture a picture long after the words have faded

I'm not sure if it's a "dislike," but perhaps adding some AI features in the future would be cool.
Quizlet has some cool AI features like conversational drills etc.

But I also like the current balance of features on Brainscape as it is--it's flexible enough for a wide range of topics and learning goals, but it doesn't have so many bells and whistles that you get distracted.
I would say I haven't found any downside in Brainscape, I would 100% recommend it to everyone.

Difficult to navigate within the site to find your sets vs other people's sets
Site sometimes moves slowly and has lags when creating flashcards
Would like to be able to star certain flashcards and only study those rather than the whole set

Some flashcards could benefit from more detailed explanations or additional context to help deepen understanding. Additionally, incorporating multimedia elements such as images or videos could enhance the learning experience further.
I have had a very positive experience with Brainscape so far. I highly recommend it as I cannot think of anything I dislike.

I've had a generally very good experience with Brainscape, so there isn’t much to dislike. If anything, my main issue with Brainscape is the frequent emails. I'd appreciate more control over the frequency and types of communications I receive.

I can't complain too much because I am enjoying it for my studies, however, I do have one recommendation to improve on an already great product: I'd like to get more out of the training. The current training is great, but it'd be neat to have other ways of learning. I used MindTap in college, and I always thought that was such a neat tool! The way they set up their training was super helpful in retaining the information and little quizzes or ways to test ourselves would be awesome! But outside of the recommendation, there is simply nothing I dislike, I love Brainscape and will continue to use it!
I dont have any dislikes about this app.
I find occasionally when I am using it on two different devices it doesn't up date the other as quickly as it could.
I had no problem with the app during my utilization.
Honestly nothing. Their premium model is great as-is.

I wish they could just open up a free trial period, so that many users would be able to know how usefule this method is. I already knew the effects so did not mind paying right away but trial was too short.
Currently at the moment the shared decks don't really cover my areas of study but I can already see this improving as people are picking it up.
There is a learning curve for new users, especially those not familiar with flashcard-based learning, to effectively utilize all of Brainscape’s features. But once got the hang of it it is worth it.
Nothing really to mention on dislikes. For now everything is positive.
The fact that there are small glitches with the software, namely in private flashcards.

Some formatting and spacing errors do happen in the text during the transition from xlsx to Brainscape, but there are not that many. Also, it is kinda hard to sort the cards instead of them always coming in the same order, would be nice to sort everything. But maybe that's me not knowing how to work it.

It would be great if there were a feature to directly select and translate text. I'm a foreigner, so I'm using English translation. Especially on iPhones, not being able to select text makes it inconvenient to go into the dictionary every time and copy and paste.
I find the web-based program, while detailed, to be a bit cubersome and more difficult to navigate than the web system.

I love the 'bookmark' feature that Brainscape offers. However, I wish I could access the cards I have bookmarked from my iPad or phone on my Mac. This would make it easier to revise cards or review them more quickly.

name: Quizlet
pros_raw: Test study questions available for most college courses.  I have test anxiety so the practice tests and flash cards help lower my anxiety because Quizlet has up to date information, not outdated like some websites.  It's been a game changer for me.
All the topics and flash cards you have access to.

My go-to tool before exams. Quizlet offers a brief summary of all concepts and topics that could be tested in an exam and exam-type questions. It has a great UI/UX design making it easy for beginners to navigate through exam subjects and topics. The best part though is that it tracks my progress, so I know where I am falling short
I love that you have options of self study activities
I love that there are so many ways to utilize this platform. I have made Quizlet sets for my students and have also allowed them to create their own. Sometimes I create assessments from Quizlet

One of the best features of Quizlet is its ability to study any topic using flashcards. It has support for adding courses and classes, which teachers can use to organize different units for their curriculum, as well as limiting the course/class access to those in it. There seems to be no limit on how many cards you can add to a study set, and that is immensely useful for students and teachers looking to use the service.

I find Quizlet very helpful when making review games for my students, such as Blooket, Kahoot, and Gimkit. Quizlet allows me to create a master list of terms and definitions and quickly export them to various review games.
The help for questions for so very beneficial for everyone and is a good great app, and quizlet is very helping, so I am reccomend that quizlet so cool y am happy for app

The most helpful thing about Quizlet is that I can create my own flashcard sets and then share them with my students. That way, I know they are studying the correct information that I have given them. Also, it is a great exercise to instruct my students to create their own flashcard sets and use them to study for tests and quizzes. My students always seem to enjoy this activity and it is much more efficient than having them create paper flashcards that often get misplaced or messed up.

The thing I like the most about Quizlet is that it is best for test preparation. The app has a feature to create flashcards which is an amazing thing to build vocabulary. The best thing I like is the animation feature in Quizlet because it helps us to memorize vocabulary words firmly in our brain, which is difficult to memorize without it.
I love how easy Quizlet is to use as a way to learn something new or study for certification or an exam. There are also tons of premade study guides that help a lot too
Learn and Test Modes were some of the best tools on Quizlet.
I loved Quizlet, have used it for a decade. Love the spaced repetition, loved all the options students used to have for different modes of learning
The ease of creating a study set from Google sheets or Excel

There is a wide variety of ways to use Quizlet.  Two of my favorites are Quizlet learn and using diagrams.  Quizlet Learn has students face and answer correctly, each question twice in a row in order to "Learn" the set.  Diagrams allow teachers to create "hot spots" on images to help students label a diagram or image correctly.

It is hard to choose what I like best about Quizlet. First of all, creating my own sets of vocabulary words is an incredibly helpful tool! Adding images and details to each definition truly helps my student learn the material in many different ways. When I am in a pinch, I truly value the fact that I can use previously published sets by searching for key terms.

It keeps people engaged without the dull feeling of being "tested." The uptime is great, it is user-friendly, and it is easy for our Training team to track how people are doing. Often times it is an ice breaker in our remote training classes. People become a bit competitive when they see the scores of others.

easy to put together- you can create your own flashcard sets or you can use others that have already been created by other users
interactive- gives students instant feedback and allows them to work at their own pace

My favorite thing about Quizlet is that it is effective as an individual study tool. Programs like Kahoot and Quizizz are great, but they require a group to play with you. Quizlet, on the other hand, is great for when you need to study on your own. While working as an AVID tutor at UNC Asheville, I used Quizlets to brush up on the AVID process and occasional topics that students might have questions on.

Quizlet is a fun-to-use and handy website and service suite. Our office uses Quizlet Plus for flashcards, study guides, and practice sets for a broad range of subjects. The sheer amount of content on the site makes it a fabulous bargain!

The most helpful thing about Quizlet is definitely the study tools it has. When trying to memorize definitions or ideas, practice is key. Quizlet offers many different ways to study, including flashcards, tests, and study games.

I like how easy it is to input my data and use many formats to quiz myself and better learn the material. Some examples of ways Quizlet presents your data to you is through flashcards, a quiz format, or with fill in the blank questions.

I have always been a fan of flashcards and microlearning in general. Nowadays, no one wants to study by reading long pages of information that can be shorten in a small bite. Once you try learning by flashcards, you will never go back to any other method :)

The thing I like the most about Quizlet is that it is fabulous for test preparation. The app lets you create flashcards and you can organize them however would best fit your needs. For me, I organize them by class and then by module within that class.
Nora was really helpful, and I'm appreciative for the Quizlet billing team in times of confusion.

I love the layout and how intuitive is it to use and access the material. The imagens available to be attached even in the free version are enough in many cases. The mobile version is great and easy to use.
Quizlet has features like spell, test, and other effective ways to learn, like flashcards and match the following. These techniques have proven to be one of the best ways to improve one's vocabulary
Quizlet is the best tool out there in the market to learn vocabulary by methods like flashcards and matching the following and even gives out the correct pronunciation of the word.

Quizlet allows you to create flashcards to view on your computer, phone, tablet, etc., so you can access study material with ease wherever you go! Quizlet is all about helping you succeed with remembering important content :)
I love the ease of use, and the number of different options available for studying. I like that it can seperate out terms you know well from ones you need more practice with.
The platform by which it makes learning more fun, interactive and hands-on. It has several options for how to manage and create certain games to make terms and learning fun in any subject.
It is very easy to create new study sets or to use preexisting ones for students to study. There are a multitude of sets to use.
Easy to use and full of learning resources
I love how easy it is to make study sets of new product I need to learn

I am able to organize out units and lessons separately and then provide links in Google Classroom in a very orderly manner so the students always have an easy way to access the current vocabulary in Science.

I enjoy the 'Learn' subsection. This section allows you to continuously study your set of cards until the material is burned into your memory. The key to learning terms is to utilize repetitive tasks by whatever means necessary. Quizlet also makes it very feasible to add notecards and to add images to your notecards which can be very helpful at times.
Ability to customize, add pictures, choose types of questions in quizzes that will most likely mirror the training you are getting.
I like that you can make a virtual flash cards to help study for exams.

I love Quizlet because it makes it super easy to study for tests and quizzes by creating custom flashcards and offering many ways to memorize them including standard flashcards, tests, and games. You can also search Quizlet for premade study sets created by other students in order to help learn anything from foreign language, math, biology, and much more. Quizlet also allows images to be added to the study sets, which can help a lot with different subjects such as science and biology. The Quizlet app for mobile devices is also a great way to study on the go.
I LOVE LOVE LOVE THE OLD QUIZLET THAT WAS ACCESSIBLE, USER FRIENDLY, AND HIGHLY EFFECTIVE AND IMPACTFUL.  I loved that it was free for years and my students used it all the  time.

I have the ability to create fun and engaging study material for my students which I implement when they need to study for an upcoming test. It is very easy to access all of the features of Quizlet and use them in the most effective way possible. I can quickly make create flash cards for students to use at anytime they please. Quizlet also contains a gamify feature which allows students to have fun while learning all the material needed to pass the tests. It also has many pictures that you can choose from to add to your flash cards to make them more interesting and to let students be able to visualize the words so they can better remember the information.
The ability to create games and quizzes as well as the flashcards
It's helpful to memorize facts and figures

It's a quiz I can type in in my test app, instead of bringing and losing paper cards. More than anything else, it helps with storage, particularly the different methods of testing it offers, and can even generate free practice tests in different formats for you. The fact that people can exchange vocabulary lists. Students that are ten times fed up with writing vocabulary lists are very useful for learning, they are able to play and practice vocabulary. Simple to print and make cards, which are useful for memory training, too. That's ideal for storing your assignments or test results.
I love the quizlet live feature! It helps to gamify my classroom.

As of December 2022, Quizlet has removed free access to what is considered their most useful features:

- "Learn." Learn mode allows the user to gauge their knowledge of the set they're studying and then build from answering multiple-choice questions to writing out the answers. Currently, the feature is now limited to their "Quizlet Plus" service, which is hidden behind a paywall. -
- This also goes for their "Test" mode, which mixes different types of questions for the user to answer, another one that is a very useful study tool.
- This has happend to their "Match" mode, as well, which allows you to match terms to other terms and then compare your speed of matching to others, creating a competitive and fun way to learn.

All these modes are now behind a paywall, leaving only "Flashcards" as free. In comparison to what Quizlet was to a student, it is now a shadow of its former self, and I, unfortunately, believe this will lead to its loss in popularity.

I dislike that Quizlet recently started charging for some of its features. I work in a small school district that doesn't have the funding for every education program, and it is somewhat frustrating to lose access to some aspects of Quizlet.
you need paid for anwers is so bad and very necessary for help so very benefit for everyone and is good grat app and quizlet very helping so i am reccomend that

I have been using Quizlet for several years in the field of education and I cannot think of any downsides to Quizlet. It is a great way for my students to study and review. I will continue using Quizlet for many years to come.

CUSTOMER SERVICE IS THE WOOOOOOORRRRRRSSSSTTT.....All around.  This week i created a Quizlet for a test review.  The updated quizlet layout is rediculous and makes absolutely no  sense.  There is nothing logical or intuitive about it and I was  compelled to  look all over the site for simple support (like how to copy a Quizlet).   Ultimately I was compelled to email Quizlet and wait for their response, which was (Look at hte directions in our  website) that never worked in the first place.
After FINALLY succeeding in  this task I made my  quizlet and was discouraged that we can no longer make multiple choice questions witout a membership. So I p urchaed my menbership and proceeded with the review I was creating.  Again, the user interface is 100% ambiguous and makes no sense.  I  wrote the questions and  stems.  There is no instruction that  the ANSWER is considered among the stems,  so each of my stems included the answer again, and this ruined the review for my students.
When I went to correct my Quizlet this morning, AGAIN I struggled to update the stems. I spent 25 minutes clicking  around my 'edit' optinos and say nothing beyond DELETE this question.  Considering the HOURS I spent crafting the questions and stems...this made NO sense at all.  Again, I negotiated the 'Cusstomer Support'" and was met with a similar experience. This rep  informed me that I can always 'delete and start over'.  What he needed to do was instruct me on  how to  edit stems.  By chance after wasiting another 25 minutes, I discovered that clicking on the ANSWER drops down the stems.  Again, this is rediculous and NOT logical or intuitive.
When I replied that this was really unhelpful,  Quizlet CANCELED my paid account without my consent.  I have already requested reimbursement from my school  and couldn't be more disgusted with my quizlet experience here.
PLEEEASE Bring back the Quzlet we all love ....What has happened to Quizlet???
