name: Quizizz
pros_raw: They continuously upgrade their features. I love how they provide multiple quiz assessment types. The multiple attempts and pool of questions gives an edge to other competitors because I can easily integrate to LMS.

Quizizz is so much fun to play and allows educators like myself to assess for the understanding of content when students are fully engaged and fully invested in getting the correct answers. I love that I can take a grade on each assessment and put it in the grade book or use the results of a quiz to reteach material!
Ability to design own quiz and play with different people. Nice site design.
It is engaging, especially when it comes to learning, as I have a very short attention span. I also like that it utilizes RPG aspects to make it more fun for the users

Quizizz allows me to create multiple learning moments during sessions and homework activities.

The use of the platform helps to enrigh my session and the learners become more involved and engaged.

A must have in any learning journey.

assessment, knowledge check or fun quizzes for all moments of the class.

The LIVE quizzes are the best!!!

The most helpful thing about Quizzizz is that everything is in one place. I can assess student learning as well as gain instant feedback on how they are doing. My students have much more fun taking assessments on Quizziz than normal pencil and paper quizzes.

I love the features of how you can print or use it online. I love the data you collect on each player. The kids love how easy it is. I use it as my primary source of testing this year. Students take it once for practice and then again in class for a grade. They love the power ups and surprisingly they like to learn the answers if they get it wrong. It has replaced re-takes in my class and is much more engaging. I love Quizizz and recommend it to my colleagues often!

Being able to connect with Google Classroom classes & individual students, and quickly get reports back on student progress. I like redemptions. I like the ability to set how many times a student can play
Nothing. It's just yet another Kahoot ripoff that does not do the job better.

Quizizz allows for in-class or individual content review in a fun and engaging environment. The ability to assign quizzes for adaptive and spaced learning is on par for optimum learning psychology. My students enjoy the opportunity to gauge their learning in a gamified testing environment, allowing for power-ups and redemption questions, whereas the teacher is provided with robust data from the learner interactions. I also enjoy the fact that the cost structure is an annual minimal single cost to the instructor. The platform also allows for the generation of an embed code to directly place the quiz into an LMS page.

Quizizz provides a more advanced or challenging platform that some of its competitors. The incorporation of fill in the blank questions alone was worth the wait. The ability to store large numbers of question sets gives you some variety in future years.  The easy editing means that you can update quiz sets here in the face of a new textbook. ***One unsolicited tip from the Old Guy: use chapter numbers --including 01 to 09 for the early chapters, at the front of the titles for your quiz sets. That way when you sort alphabetically, everything for Chapter/Unit/Module 3 will be together in your list,(03 Homesteaders,  O3 Cattle Ranchers,  03 Buffalo Soldiers,  03...etc) and save you search time.

One of the best features of QUIZIZZ is how easy it is for me to share the QUIZIZZ that I create with my colleagues. It really is as simple as a click of a button. Another feature that I truly love is how I can EMAIL the results to the students' parents! AND how I can easily create a spreadsheet of the results as well!!!

I love that I have full control over designing the games. In other resources, I have to deal with character limits and can't change the fonts. Because I can write as much as I want in Quizizz and change the fonts or add special characters, my students are able to work on everything from grammar questions to paragraphs to math problems.

I love that I can search any topic/subject and allow my students to simply practice or use it as an assessment!  I also like that I can access and view reports to see how my students did on their quizizz! It tells me how long they spent on each question, which questions they missed, and which questions they got correct! Not even a paper test can tell you how long each student spent on each question!

I have been using quizizz for several years now.  They have done a real great job of keeping it fresh and updated.  My students love to compete solo and in teams.  They love the funny names they can receive. As a teacher it is an amazing resource to use as a quick formative check of my students knowledge.  I use google classroom and it integrates with quizizz seamlessly. Making it easy and fast for my students to log into a quiz. The homework feature is a great way for my students to practice over and over again.

I have been using quizizz for several years now.  They have done a real great job of keeping it fresh and updated.  My students love to compete solo and in teams.  They love the funny names they can receive. As a teacher it is an amazing resource to use as a quick formative check of my students knowledge.  I use google classroom and it integrates with quizizz seamlessly. Making it easy and fast for my students to log into a quiz. The homework feature is a great way for my students to practice over and over again.

Self-paced whole group quizizz works great for a warm-up. Kids come into the room, sit down, and are eager to compete.
Instructor-paced is better than Kahoot because questions and answer choices can be longer, up to five answer choices can be used, and pictures can be used more easily.
Being able to assign activities as homework is an added bonus!
These tools also worked really well from the virtual setting. My warm-up procedure didn't have to change.

I love how students can go at their own pace, and I can set questions and answer choices to randomize. My students love the power ups to earn more points and get lifelines to answer questions. There’s a leaderboard that fosters competition, but when students want to see what they missed, they can see their missed questions at the end. As a teacher who doesn’t like to reinvent the wheel, you can also transport questions from other users so you’re not spending as much time writing questions.

My favorite thing about Quizzez is that there is an option to create your own quiz for students or to use one of the thousands of teacher-created quizzes already in the app. I have been impressed with the quality of the quizzes already available, which makes for a great last-minute engaging activity for my students.

I love that it allows me to give the students more time than other game sites.  In math, especially at the older grades, problems become more complex and I want my students taking their time to work out each step.  Quizzizz allows me to do that and the students do not feel rushed to just pick an answer.
I love the easy to understand layout, and the teleport question feature is AWESOME!
It has instant feedback and is connected to google classroom. I can easily assign math problems to students.
Quizizz is super easy to create quick, formative assessments and the students love "playing" the quiz, which is actually teaching them content.

I use Quizizz in my class as a quiz/test review tool. My students treat it as a game and enjoy it just as much, if not more, than Kahoot. Because of this, they enjoy Quizizz and engage with it for the entirety of the game, rather than losing interest halfway through like they would in a regular test review session. I also like that after a student gives an incorrect answer, Quizizz provides the student with the correct answer and gives them a wait time before their next question, allowing them to read the correct answer choice.

In Quizizz, I like that I can check for understanding in my math classes.  I use the create a lesson option and make daily, short warm-ups.  I can see how many in the class understand a concept and if I need to review that topic again.   I also use Quizizz as a weekly quiz.  It grades it automatically and inputs it into my Google Classroom.  I use Quizizz as daily homework assignments sometimes too.  The students can play Quizizzes during the week as many times as they want to get proficient in a topic area.
Smooth Interface and ease of usage this website

Quizizz has more modern graphics than other programs and a better free version. The basic  (free) version for teachers is limited to 100 participants, which is usually enough. The other program is limited to 10. They also have great music and features like "eliminate two wrong answers."

Quizizz is incredibly useful, as students have the questions on their own devices.  You can post the leaderboard from your own device when playing a review game.  Quizizz can also be assigned for homework or as extra review for students to complete on their own.  Lastly, their new feature of Lessons has allowed me to teach concepts to the students and have them answer questions in the middle of the lesson.  This is incredible helpful with distance/hybrid learning.  It keeps all of the students engaged.

(1)  Since I am a math teacher, I like using the equation editor (math templates) to make up my own quizzes, usually as fill in the blank, where students will submit an answer and need to match my answer.   This is very useful because most of the students that I teach will go on to college.  Prior to that, most will take an entry exam where text matching is very important.
(2) Quizzes can be assigned either as homework or done live in the classroom (either instructor paced or student paced).  For me, the instructor paced gives immediate feedback to students.  I can explain common errors and re-explain a certain topic.  I given student paced quizzes not only for homework but also in class live.  Students are into the technology (either use their cell phones or a chromebook)
(3) Record keeping allows me to go back and see student mistakes (if they want to go over a particular question) or double check a grade associated with an assignment.

I love the “edit” feature.  It allows users to borrow another user’s quiz and edit it to fit your needs.  It saves time and provides rigor.

I also love love the ability to import my rosters from google classroom.

It is very engaging. I use it to leverage the Testing Effect. This is a body of research out of educational psychology that shows that one of the best ways to learn anything is to take a test. the research is very robust it even shows that you learn when you take a test on a subject you know nothing about.

Is application is a must during this time of pandemic, since face to face is not allowed, QuiziZz gives fun and excitement to us most specially to our clients. Games are the mose awaited part of webinars.

I like the instant feedback I get on where my students are struggling after each game. It allows me to reteach those concepts most missed. The different question types and formats (lesson, quiz etc.) give me lots of choice.

I love being able to schedule activities in Google Classroom.  The variety of question types that are now available make the platform more versatile for homework, formative assessments and (of course) quizzes.  Being able to choose whether or not students can re-do assignments makes students more likely  to practice in the pursuit of better grades, or you can only allow one attempt.

As a tutor, I find Quizizz to be an effective and engaging review tool. I is more game-like than many interactive review games and keeps student attention we'll because of this. I also like that I can allow students to progress at their own pace as well as all at once.

Quizizz is an excellent platform to provide both in-person and virtual assessments. There are a variety of pre-made questions and quizzes to upload right into an assessment, as well as the option to create your own. Quizizz is connected to the Google suite, so it is extremely easy to attach an assessment to a Google Classroom as an assignment. The analytics are also fantastic, with the data showing strengths and weaknesses as a whole class, as well as individual.

Quizizz is a fun, flexible, engaging platform for review or practice. I appreciate the opportunity to allow students to use this independently or to have them complete the quizzes at the teacher’s pace.

Quizizz is simply amazing. As a substitute teacher, there are many times when I have to introduce new topics and ideas to my students. Often, I need some online assistance to make sure I am teaching them the right thing or showing them how to do something correctly. During times like this, I immediately turn to Quizizz. The lessons and quizzes on Quizizz are always so helpful! I can even quickly make my own!

I have been using Quizizz for 5 years now and I have to say that it is one of my top 5 apps to use in the classroom because it is effortless to use.  It saves me a lot of time as a teacher, that I can import or teleport questions without having to create one from scratch.

Quizizz is a fun way to review and reteach concepts. Students are more likely to participate with a game-like atmosphere. I also like that there are so many features that let you customize it to meet the needs of your class. For example, you can set the pacing so the teacher controls it when you really need to reteach. You can also open the pacing so everyone can work through as fast or as slow as they need.
I love to use quizizz because of the engaged learning and the results from the games that I use as a formative assessment.  This helps guide my instruction.
I love the transport feature that makes it so easy for me to find a plethora of problems on the specific topic I am assessing. It is great for skill practice. SO MUCH LESS PREP TIME!

Quizizz has an abundance of ready made quizzes for every topic and its easy to teleport new questions or delete questions you don't like.  Differentation is a snap as well...you can level out tests to meet all the needs of your students easily.   I'm talking like less than a minute.
It's very easy to create quizzes, and students love it!
Quizizz is extremely user friendly and it's invaluable that it's integrated with Google Classroom
You can use and edit previous made quizzes that match what you want to assess
Teleport option has a multitude of excellent content questions that can easily be edited if needed. I use it to make quizzes.
Self-paced and engaging for students to have fun while learning.
I like the live game play and interactive environment
Quizizz is the best platform to review past lessons while engaging students during virtual learning

Quizizz is my go to platform for engaging my students. My students love the competition of the live game. I love the ease of creating sets and borrowing from sets that were created by other educators. The detailed reports give me valuable information in real time. Quizizz ease of use, student engagement, and detailed reports make 5 out of 5 stars!
cons_raw: It gets lagged when there are so many active users during live stream. Maybe, improved the users capacity when doing live stream assessments so that they will be included in our records.

It is difficult to think of anything I dislike about this product. I will say that incorporating more diversity in the game experience concerning the music and the available visuals would be great! Also, adding characters to make it more of a traditional video game feel would make for a more interactive experience.
Many premium features; sometimes sharing link with kids didn't work for me, not clear enough how to use some features.
So Far I haven't been able to pinpoint anything that I dislike about Quizizz
NOthing negative to report as my 2years experience proved to be fantastic.
There is nothing I dislike about Quizzizz. My favorite part of the software platform is that there are so many premade quizzes that I can use in the classroom and elsewhere.
Nothing because I can use the data for so many purposes as well as engage the students.

New features (just started this fall) that are merely another way of doing the same thing that  other programs already provide. If Google Suite of services already provides us with Sheets, Docs, Drawings, Slides, etc., then it doesn't make sense for me to take the time to learn a new feature that does the same thing. "If it ain't broke, don't fix it."
I like that my students can't see what a correct answer is if they miss a question, but I would like for the conclusion of a game to include an obvious "here's what you missed, summary", so they can study their wrong answers before they make a second attempt. Perhaps such a summary could be added and include a button that the student has confirmed that they have reviewed what questions they missed.

It's too distracting. There are bad memes after a lot of the questions which slow you down and there are powerups which seem to be the focus but really it degrades the quality of the platform. You can't even go back and change your answers.

The only item that I see as an issue is the LMS integration policy. In order to have integration, the company only allows for the integration of an enterprise (University or School System) license. This means that the instructor must manually transfer grades into the LMS if they are being tracked.

Something needs to be done to make it easier for TEACHERS to edit student names. I still recall the trouble I had during the 'Year of the Seven Brittany"s. Seven of 140+ with the same first name, most unwilling to use a last name. If I could edited(added) last names, I could have saved a fortune on Tums.....

The only thing that I've disliked so far is that we have to pay to record our voices. A lot of my students required for the questions and answer choices to be read to them. It is a shame that this feature isn't free.

I wish that there was more that could be done with the coins students earn, it would be great if they could buy new account icons or backgrounds with the coins they earn or buy new memes for the games.
To be honest, I haven’t found one thing I dislike about quizizz.com.
I would like to see the lesson module to include a way to stop a video and ask question such as you would find in nearpod or edpuzzle.

The "Classic" "Live Quiz" feature requires a student log in before I can start the quiz. That means I have to hover by my computer and press go as soon as the first student logs in. I want to see the live progress and leader board right away.
I also don't like that the Live quiz automatically stops
I know that "Instructor paced" is still in beta. It works well, but the timer length cannot currently be changed.

The only issue I have with Quizizz is that I tell students to use their last name as their player ID so I can alphabetize for easy grade input. As a Google school, we have a lot of students who just use their Google log in instead. But Quizizz doesn’t recognize their last name with their Google account, so that’s a bit annoying.

Something I dislike about Quizzez is that it can be difficult for my younger students to get a hang of the platform. Some students catch on quite quicky, while others require a but of support on my end to help them navigate and access their materials that I assign to them.
Nothing, it has been fantastic for me during our hybrid instruction and for teaching in person.
If the reports could be in a gradebook-style layout, that'd be great.
That I haven’t had time to play with the non multiple choice options
The ONLY thing I dislike is the fact that you have to buy the paid version to reopen a certain quiz.   A VERY MINOR DETAIL - I just assign the quiz again to reopen it.
The only thing that I dislike about Quizizz is that students can get competitive to the point that they become angry when forced to wait, such as when Quizizz shows them memes after a correct answer.
I don't like that I have to pay for an upgrade to add some of the cool extras.  Luckily, my school is going to pay for the upgrade!
More customization option for B2B clients and secured way to conduct competition online.

At this point, I dislike that they keep changing their policies, and they keep changing their documentation. But I understand that that is a sign of growth and progress. I just hope they don't get greedy and try to monetize the program beyond what most teachers can afford. Currently, most teachers can afford to use this fantastic tool.

I wish that Quizizz had a matching or whiteboard feature that the teacher could grade.  Normally I like the instant feedback Quizizz provides, but sometimes I wish I could see student thought-process on some questions.

When a quiz is given, the results are still on student's phones after taking the quiz.  This means that students could show students from later classes the questions and answers.  So I need to make sure that the quizzes are different.
Drag and drop does not always work.  But that is so minute in comparison to all the perks.

Students who aren't winning don't have as much fun. I worry about those students who always end up on the low end of the scale. The competitive aspect is what makes it fun and engaging but gosh what about those other students I really don't have an answer.
User interface, personally, I don't like choosing a photo before typing my name, or maybe they can udpate a version with funnier faces for more excitement
I dislike the powerup system. I know I can turn it off, but students really love it, and it can at times become an argument when I tell them no because I have to collect data.

I cannot select the Google Classroom topics when I assign the activities.  Just a minor drawback but it would be nice.

Perhaps someday you will add in a math-based entry where students can easily type in their work.  Again, just a nice perk that this chemistry teacher would like!


Oh, and one student switched sections and has trouble accessing Quizizz from Google Classroom.  No idea why...

There is nothing that I really dislike about Quizizz. There was a small learning curve for me as the host of the games, as it was different than any other review game that I had hosted, but my students had no trouble at all learning how the game worked.

The only thing I would say I dislike is that some of the data is more difficult to understand where it came from. Most of the data (time spent on questions, percentage correct, etc) is quite easy to read.
Honestly, there aren’t many negatives. You can’t please all students, so not every kid likes it, but most of them enjoy the opportunity to turn learning into a game.
Quizizz has everything a learning platform should. Lessons, quizzes, feedback reports, and a way to search the general pool to find premade lessons and quizzes. It's so great!

The only thing I don't like is how the format for the questions when the quiz is ready to be printed is not flexible. I want to find different views that suit my needs in class and not just the standard.

Sometimes it encourages students to rush through the questions rather than slowing down to think. I have to set the pacing or get rid of the timer so that students can slow down and not rush. I also would like it to show explanations for the correct answers so that students can see why they are getting problems wrong. I also would like an ease of use for uploading mathematics. Sometimes it is difficult to edit math formatting like square roots and fractions.
Nothing!  My fifth grade students love playing quizizz review games.  They are engaged and excited.
It is hard to assess conceptual understanding of some topics. Quizizz doesn't lend itself to that very well, so I don't really use it for that.
I dislike when I want to just select certain students for specific tests when sending to Google Classroom and it doesn't allow me to automatically select the students before sending out.

I have yet to automatize passing the results of the quizzes to the spreadsheet I use. Since I only take into account the number of correct answers (and not the points that Quizzizz gives), I have to calculate manually the grade that each student obtains.
I do wish I could leave quizzes open for longer
It needs to be easier to assign to specific students.  I have a problem with assigning to students if they haven't accepted an invite.
No dislikes...I started using Quizizz during the Covid online learning and will continue  using it when we return.
Less customization with free version, likely much better if my school could afford the premium version.
the interface is not as clean and simple as I would prefer
I couldn't think of any negative remarks, so far. I am loving it!
Creating a class has proven difficult. But I can easily share link on Google Classroom.

name: SchoolStatus
pros_raw: SchoolStatus allows me to communicate easily with parents or guardians. I am able to send pictures or pdfs of things that students can work on at home. It allows the user to unsubscribe, for example, if a parent gets a new phone number and does not update it in the system. I also really enjoy the fact that I can call or text parents/guardians without having to give my personal number out. I am able to easily use and implement the fact that I can contact all parents as many times as I want. It also stores all of the messages between me and the parents.

My staff says it is the one piece of software we have introduced that has been a game-changer! It is so quick to send a message and a great way for us to track communication with families. Our families love it because they have each teachers contact and can communicate at anytime via text.

I have worked in other schools that do not have school status. What I like about this program is that you can still have parent communication without needing to be at a school landline. This program also codes your personal number, so parents do not have access to it. Another plus is that you can message parents by texting. It seems more and more parents prefer this method of communication is straight and to the point.
I love that School Status tracks the communication between parents and myself so records are easily kept. I also love the ease in which that communication takes place.

I love the ease of sending out broadcast messages in my classes and I also love the personal messaging by email, text and/or phone calls --- and the accountability of recording for my personal liability coverage. SchoolStatus is the best teacher tool in my repertoire and its ease of use and implementation makes it the best to integrate in every day use.
The parent receives instant contact. Many parents respond quickly.
The text feature has greatly increased our communication with families, in this day and age, adults are able to see and respond to a text and we can send out quick updates without a disruption.
SchoolStatus takes the guesswork out of translating to other languages.  I love that I can send out an email or text and it's automatically translated for me! All parents get the information.

As a Physical therapist working in a public school setting, it has always been a professional development goal of mine to keep in touch with parents more than annually at parent-teacher conferences or IEP meetings. School Status has made this a reality, translating my messages into the preferred language of the family and allowing for this connection so I can share the vicotries that their child has had in their gross motor skills at school!
I love how it documents every conversation. No paper trails needed. Makes life MUCH easier!

SchoolStatus makes it very easy to communicate with families. I like that I am able to communicate with families but still keep my number private. I also appreciate the recording feature when calling families.
School status is a teacher's  help.  It makes it easy to send messages ( in different languages).  A mobile function
School Status provides an easy way to keep communication between teachers and parents open. It keeps me from having to share my personal cell number but still allows easy 2-way communication.
The best thing I love about SchoolStatus is communication, scores, and contact information is in one place.

My favorite part of school status is the text message parent contact and the broadcast feature. It has been extremely helpful in parental contact. I especially like how parents can call you back and leave a message and how School Status will notify via email when you have a text or voice message. I also like how I have one central location to go for attendance, test scores, grades, contact info, etc.

What I like most about SchoolStatus is the ability to change the messages into different languages. Our school has a large Spanish population and communicating with parents is a challenge. I am able to send messages in Spanish to these parents.
Convenience of the app to use broadcast message.   Also to call parents and not revealing my personal number.

I love that school status allows me to message or call parents on a device without giving out my personal information, or having to find a phone to use in the building. I can contact them directly through my laptop! You can also makes groups (works awesome for homeroom teachers! or club leaders! or coaches!), send mass messages (broadcasts) to your student population and every contact with that parent is documented! There are so many great things about SchoolStatus!

I like the ease with which I can contact parents using my own personal phone without giving out my personal number. I also like that the student's contacts are readily available. I do not have to look up and type in any info.

This platform works well for parents to contact us, too. We can truly partner in the student's education and work towards a goal that works for all.

School Status has been a game changer. It is easy to use and has everything you need to communicate with parents right at your finger tips. Names, phone numbers and email addresses are all in one place.

School Status keeps me organized too. On busy days I would forget to log all of my communications with parents. With School Status, I don't have to worry. School Status records your conversations during phone calls and saves text messages and emails.

I also love that parents can contact me without giving out my personal phone number. The mobile app is great as well. I highly recommend School Status.

I love the fact I can communcicate with parents efficiently anytime I need to. It takes the hassle out of contacting parents over the phone. I can message individual students or whole classes at a time. It has helped me to keep parents invovled with what's happening in my classroom.
SchoolStatus keeps a record of the contacts that are made to scholars guardians.  Also, information on scholars is only a click in front of you.
The fact that everything gets saved, in case you need to go back and need evidence.

I love SchoolStatus. It allows me to quickly and easily communicate with the families of my students. I like that I can send broadcasts to multiple families at once as well as individual messages. Communication with families is such an important part of a child's education, and I have received the most feedback from parents when I use this app versus some other means of communication. It is great for sending short reminders, and it is easy for families to check and respond to.
School Status makes it easier to contact parents for meetings and information.

The most valuable asset using SchoolStatus is that most parents don't answer numbers they don't know. But we all know most people have their phones close. And being able to text is a game changer! It has helped me reach out to parents so they know how their child is doing and how we can work together to fix it.

I believe SchoolStatus has helped our staff better connect with families across the district! I love seeing school status to help gauge upcoming birthdays, contacting parents, and noting concerning absences or providing announcements for whole groups!
I appreciate that school status records phone calls as well as keeping a log of text messages sent. As a special education teacher, this is extremely helpful for documentation!

What I like mostly is that all the student information and their families are in one place and the data of the communications is saved for future reference. Teachers can also see the general data of each student and have an idea of them.

SchoolStatus provides a user-friendly vehicle for documented detailed communication between educators and families. The options to text, email or call helps those with diverse communication which creates a  multi-tiered method of maintaining clear communication which ultimately adds layers of insulation that protect the integrity of student support or reveal areas for teacher growth.

Before I learned about school status, my parent contact logs were lost in the wasteland of good intentions.  I never got around to them.  To get to a school phone I only had access by going down the hall to a phone shared by a dozen or more teachers.  Whenever I did make calls I would jot down notes that often were lost before getting them into a log I could locate when asked to submit them.  Such contact was a nightmare for me.  School Status has changed that for me.  I love it because it gives me access to everything I need to make effective parent contacts with a record I can't lose.  I can send emails, texts and make calls directly from my computer wherever I might be.  If parent contacts mess with your sleep or otherwise causes anxiety, School Status is your answer.  Sweet Dreams.

The most helpful element of SchoolStatus is the ability to document behavior or celebrate a student with a parent immediately.  The ability to shoot a text makes all the difference in the world! In this fast-paced world, teachers often forget to make phone calls at the end of the day; however, with the ability to send a text immediately, that mistake can be avoided. Sending a text also relieves the pressure on teachers and parents to find time to make contact about a student. Working parents can respond whenever they get a chance and teachers have a running documentation of contact. The Broadcast message helps me send updates, reminders, and instructions to parents and students regularly.
It takes very little time out of my schedule to make contact with parents. The texting feature is amazing!!

School Status allows you to confidentially call or text guardians, documents all contact history, records phone calls, and so much more! It's accessible from the app or on your desktop. You can also leave voicemails if a guardian does not answer your call.

The most helpful feature of Svool Status is the easy "one click" ability to communicate with parents individually or as a group. I love being able to type a message to parents that they can receive immediately as a text message. It allows for fast responses. I also like the digital record that it creates for both text messages & audio recordings. This has proved to be helpful when proving that communication with a parent occurred.
I love SchoolStatus because it is efficient, effective and protective. I can quickly correspond with students and parents. Plus, there is a record of all correspondence.

As a principal, I have to keep up with alot of data. School status has made my life so much easier. I love this program. It keeps me updated with attendance, I can check on my reachers' communication log, and so much more. If your school doesn't have it, I encourage you to get it
School Status is the most efficient way to communicate with parents.  I like that I can either use the School Status app on my phone or the School Status website.
Its very convenient, it updates quickly and works efficiently.  Also can use multiple languages

What I like best about S.S. is that I can text, along with send photos, to parents efficiently and timely. Most parents work and cannot answer the phone, but they can check text messages.  SchoolStatus also documents phone calls, emails and texts that the teachers send to parents.  As an administrator, I no longer have to interrupt the classroom to find out what the teacher said to the parent and then wonder what is true and what is not true.  It is all documented.
The ability to send text messages from either the web interface, or the app, in a userfriendly platform, makes it easy for users and parents alike to stay in communications.
I really love the ease of sending messages to parents on SchoolStatus! It records on my computer so I don't have to make a different document for every email!

I love that I can communicate with all the parents at my school with no language barrier, and they can feel more comfortable talking to us too. It has made communication as easy as it can be. If school status goes down, I go down with it! Love it!!
School status makes it effortlesss to communicate in guardians proffered language and modality!

School Status is great, because at the touch of your keyboard you can send a message or email to parents and let them know how good their student is doing or let them know they are having issues. It is a BIG time saver!
After using SchoolStatus for the last 5 years, the use has grown and it is the #1 login requested by teachers. They love the ability to stay connected with family.
I love that all my students are there and I can easily message the whole class or an individual student's family.
SS is so easy to use. I am able to communicate with parents in multiple ways. I love that it gives me a record of all my communication.
School status is very user friendly. We enjoy using it on a daily basis.
cons_raw: The least helpful thing about SchoolStatus is that you cannot unsend a message. If you accidentally send a message to the wrong parent or grab the wrong picture, you have made a bit of a snafu.
I wish that we could find a way to add student numbers to the options. App not having all the information-especially addresses

Parents being able to communicate with you at any point of the day can also become tiresome. It is possible that this program may have a setting to set "Office hours", but I have not discovered it yet.

The new platform was difficult to learn. Many of my colleagues were unhappy with the change. When I was finally getting the hang of the new platform, our district switched back to the old one temporarily. Maybe with a little more training up front on how to maneuver the program people wouldn't have found it so difficult.
I only wish that it kept old messages since I teach siblings in different years. It would be nice to be able to keep those message threads going.
I have not found anything I don't like yet.

Having to select primary contacts and maintaining those have made some communication errors with guardians that didn't recieve a message due to that not being selected. Also, as a groups creator, not being able to see other groups that other building administrators have created make it difficult to manage the groups feature so you have to ask who created which group to update those.
It is a little cumbersome when you want to send multiple single messages.  Other than that, it's amazing.

The challenges I face are that the translation feature is often off- the reply sent by the parent translated to English can be difficult to decipher at times. I also feel restricted by the kinds of files I can attach- I would love to share short video clips or files in other formats.
The app could be more user friendly. Sometimes it doesn't load properly.
Sometimes I do not get messages on my phone and do not realize that a family has tried to contact me until I check my computer.
Actually,   I can't  think of a downside
One limitation is character limits, however broadcasts allow longer messages to be sent.
There isn't anything that I don't like on SchoolStatus.

The only downside is many parents will not answer a number they do not know. Until it is saved in their cell phone, they will reject the call.I would recommend telling them to save it to their phone for future reference. I also do not like how the grades are not always up to date with our grading system. I am not sure if it has to do with teacher posting grades, or not.
I have not found any features that I dislike.
Sometimes I would get my notifications late.
The only thing I dislike is how hard it is to create groups. I cannot seem to figure out how to separate by grade or homeroom teacher, but that is probably user error.

There is nothing particular. I want the software company to create a signature line for the one sending the information out. Sometimes, I forget to remind parents of my name. I have learned from parents that they may get multiple messages, and knowing from whom each note comes is essential.
I love School Status. It is hard to think of an area where improvement is needed. The School Status mobile app could be improved upon. It is not as easy to navigate as the website.

When looking up students, you can only search by student name, not by parent name. It would be convenient if you could look up parents by their name. This would help me to send out messages quicker and more efficiently.
SchoolStatus is a great platform that is extremely useful to me in preforming my duties and expectations at work.
Sometimes the app, is not as convenient as using on a desktop.
I haven't found many downsides to SchoolStatus. The app continues to be improved. Occasionally, it takes a bit to load a message, but that could just be a connection issue rather than the app itself.
Sometimes the parents do not answer because of the odd phone number.
There really isn't anything that I dislike about Schoolstatus. It gives you all the information you need to know about your students and that helps in communication with parents.
So far, I have no criticism of SchoolStatus. I am still new to all of its components, but every section has been user friendly, easy to access, and easy to use.

There are times when I do not get my messages from parents in a timely manner. I will get messages on Fridays that say they were actually sent on Tuesday. I do not know what the cause of that could be.
When families want to communicate back with us, School Status changes teachers' phone numbers each time so parents have a hard time knowing how to communicate back.

This is truly a tough question to answer. If I could add anything to SchoolStatus it would be the option for Google Meetings at the push of a button. Sometimes that face to face or screen-to-screen communication can truly add to the efficacy of the communication. That video recording absolutely would be essential to ensure integrity on both sides of the conversation.

I honestly have found no downside to School Status.  I love all of its features.  My only problem, if you could call it that is that I fell I have no good excuse for failure to make contact with all of the parents of my students.  It actually pushes me ito action to do that.  This year I am hoping my classes are populated in time to reach out wilts an early welcome letter to my students.

I haven't found an element that I don't like about SchoolStatus. I love that I send Broadcasts to classes, groups that I make, and all started individuals. The only thing that I would like to see is a compiled list of parents or users who reply STOP to end correspondence/opt out of messages. This would help with school documentation.
I haven't found any down side to using SchoolStatus.
There is a word count on text messages. So my longer messages have to be divided into multiple messages. Which the guardian still receives everything, just in chunks instead of one long message.
I wish it would automatically include a digital signature from the sender.
I honestly haven't found one thing that I don't like about SchoolStatus. I love that there's a record of every conversation, email and text. I love the contact card!
I don't dislike anything about the program. I am so glad my school has this program. I am hoping we add the s'more portion this upcoming school year, I want to use it to send out newsletters
I have found nothing I dislike about School Status.  It meets all of my needs as far as parent communication is concerned.  The system provides you with a record of parent communication.
I have not found anything that is a negative or con at this point and I use it everyday.
I want to be fair and give a dislike, but I really cannot think of one.  It is one of the most used programs in our building.  Well worth the cost.
At the time of this review, I cannot find anything at all that I do not like about this product.
It was a little challenging figuring out the phone calls but once I did, it was easy to use!
It is rare when it's down, but when it is, I feel like I am missing my phone.

I do wish that the specific student name was attached to communication. Parents gave many students in school, in the same school, and even same grade. It would be great for parents to immediately know which student the message is about (especially useful when sending out broadcasts)

The only problem I have is with phone calls. I have not had very many successful calls this year, because they seem to drop as soon as they are answered. This is no way makes the it any less helpful though.
Having to manually activate teachers. I need it to be automated!
It's not as user-friendly as some platforms. You will need to take time to watch online tutorials to figure out how to use most of the features. It's worth it, though.
No problems with the service. I can easily find my contacts and communicate.
Nothing, I have had a good experience. No Complaints.

name: TalkingPoints
pros_raw: The best part about taking points is how it offers teachers, like me, the ability to communicate with parents of all languages. It makes the process super seamless!
I like that I can create several different groups to send ot announcements to those that need the information or I can message parents individually.

What I find most helpful about Talking Points is that your communication goes out to parents with a fluent, professional translation that sounds like it was written in their language! You don't have to simplify your communication because you're not sure about using google translate or other online translation apps.  Talking points has always been accurate for me and I've been using it for three years. It's wonderful that so many languages are available! I've used Russian, Spanish, Swahili and others. It saves time because it translates in all those languages. Your communications are saved and you can even save templates. It makes it easy to communicate with my multi language families.
Talking Points is quick and easy to use.  Ease of implementation, integration, and features are all excellent.

This app is a spectacular resource to use in connecting with families who speak languages I am unable to communicate in. It's so nice to know families are still able to know what's happening in our class without the use of constant translators.
I get to share announcements with parents! It's so helpful & the program also does the translations for me so I don't have to! An easy to use program

I have loved using Talking Points this year because I was able to reach EVERYONE in my classroom, every time! Whenever I sent out a message I had parents with different home languages messaging me back and this was so helpful from years past!

TalkingPoints is an efficient platform that makes teacher-parent communication easier than ever. It keeps track of how many messages you send and who is reading the messages. It is also great to be able to write in English and the parent can easily change the messages to their native language. The templates provided are also super helpful.
I love that parents can receive messages whether they have the app or not.
I think it is an easy way to send out information that will go straight to a parent's phone. This way I can communicate effectively and in a timely fashion.

Compared to other communication apps I have used, TalkingPoints is simple and efficient. Many parents don't want to sign up and go through all the difficulties that others require. So far, I have had zero complaints! TalkingPoints has made this piece of teaching so much easier!

Talking Points allows me to respect parents by communicating with them in their language - quickly. I don't have to use another source to paste my writing into, translate, then copy and paste. It was soooo easy to get started, and it is really simple to use!

I am an ESL Specialist and Coordinator.  I do a lot of professional learning for teachers across many school districts.  Talking Points is the very first platform we promote for connecting and communicating with families as a way to make them feel welcome, valued and members of our school and greater community!

Talking Points is easy and convenient when communicating with parents.  It takes away barriers to communication by translating messages for me.  It is the best app I've used in terms of integration, ease of use, and effectiveness.
That it communicates with parents in their native language. I no longer have to find someone to make contact with my Spanish speaking parents.
The ability to talk to parents seamlessly and effectively. The application makes the notification easy and fast when emails accounts can be overloaded.
Talking Points is hands down one of the best apps I have used when communicating to parents.  This wonderful app completely eliminates the language barrier we as educators sometimes face.
I don't know how they did it but this resource is remarkable! I am grateful be able to contact families who speak another language without an interpreter every time!

I love that TalkingPoints automatically translates what's written into the language the parents/guardians indicated. Makes communicating SO much easier! And parents/guardians ACTUALLY reply back! (Versus email, which always seems to be hit or miss. This goes too their text message hub.)

It's so easy to communicate securely with all parents in a class or one parent individually. They have every feature a teacher could want, and it's all so thoughtfully packaged in a highly-intuitive, highly-functional design. It's the best technology tool I've ever used as a teacher! Absolute game changer.
It is very easy to use as a teacher and for my students
I love that TalkingPoints automatically translates my messages to the preferred language of each of my students' families. It's such a great tool!
That is translates things for me when I need to talk to parents

Talking Points is the easiest way for teachers to connect with parents. It's an app that alerts your parents to new messages for quick communication. Additionally, parents can have the messages translated into their home language.
TalkingPoints helps me bridge the language gap  I face with the families I work with every day. I have yet to use a platform that is so user friendly in its servicing.
The ease of using the tool for teachers and parents.
Build-in translator feature facilitates communication and significantly lowers language barriers.

- convenient and user friendly
- solid translation software
- easy to reach parents without them needing to first download an app
- keep parents up do date about student progress

It's so wonderful to be able to talk to all of my students' families. It's really been a useful tool for me this year for positive and negative communications. Other platforms don't translate into the family home language and that's been so helpful.

We have a very diverse student population at our school and we appreciate being able to send messages home that can be translated into the students' home languages. We really appreciate being able to reach all of the families simply and for them to respond back.
It's great! Easy to learn use and reach all players at once
I appreciate that this app connects to our school app that easily allows us to send group messages and translates it into the native language.
TP gets messages quickly to parents as well as the students.

TalkingPoints is a great communication tool for connecting with my students' families who have a first language other than English. It is easy for both parties to use and has helped me securely and accurately send important information.

I love how I can easily communicate with my parents through text and they can easily reply back through their smartphones. It's also great to communicate with my parents that speak other languages besides English.
I love that it is extremely user friendly to set up and it is a great resource to communicate with families who speak another language at home

The upsides are ease of use, documentation, communication between platforms like PowerSchool, and the ability to switch between admin and teacher accounts if you have both. I love using this app to easily communicate with parents and be able to provide documentation of these interactions.
You can send multiple text messages to parents at the same time.

Translation Translates to languages that I would normally not have the capacity to access and make a connection to parents. There is really no comparison to the school connect that we use at the moment because it does not translate.
I'm a teacher and Talking Points has been an excellent platform for my parents and I. I also love the fact that it can set to translate in Spanish to make it easier for some of my parents as well!
I am able to quickly post a message to all parents quickly and efficiently.

I absolutely love TalkingPoints for many different reasons. We have very high population of learners at our school who are ESOL. TalkingPoints always for me to communicate with all my parents with little to no language barriers because both teachers and parents can select the language in which they would like to send and receive messages and TalkingPoints does a wonderful job of translating with no delay in receiving them on either end. The flexibility of adding families is seamless also and they offer multiple methods to do so. There is both a teacher and a parent app and there is no limit to the text or messages that you send to your families. Getting start is easy also. I could go on and on about how amazing this tool is but you will never know until you try it for yourself! So ahead try it! I promise you won't regret it at all!
Direct message to parents cell phone, and class announcement.
The feature I love the most is that it translates the messages from English to different languages spoken by our students' parents.
I like that ENails are HIGHLY LIKELY to be read by student caregivers and it's ability to translate from English to best understood home language!
Allows parents to connect with teachers especially in regards to providing updates.
Parents are very receptive to the messages I send. It makes it easier to organize data for families.
Talking Points is a great way to easily connect with parents and stay in communication with them.
The most helpful is the communication I am able to do with non English speaking people.

I love being able to communicate with my students and with Talking Points the students can receive the message in their own languages! It's a great and outstanding to talk and connect with my students.  I highly recommend this app.
cons_raw: I dislike how the program functions far better with parents who download the app -- I wish it would work just as well for parents who are just using it thought texting.
Would love it if I caould drag and drop or copy and paste graphics

What I dislike is that because my district doesn't allow google docs, I wasn't able to copy/paste my students' profiles. I had to do it individually. However, it was well worth the time and effort at the beginning of the year. I have now used Talking Points for three years and find this initial work at the beginning well worth it.
There is nothing that is not good about Talking Points.

Sometimes the translations can be inaccurate/confusing which is likely true for any automated translation service.
My biggest problem with this resource is the APP. I was made an admin in my district three years ago and never noticed any differences in my app other than being able to connect with more students. Then, all of the sudden at the beginning of this school year I (and others) can no longer send messages from the app, they just disappear. After being in contact with support for a month, they closed my case because I just kept telling them it wasn't working??? And when I inquired about this they told me it was because I was an admin so it wasn't supported but 1. I've been an admin for 3 years and it hasn't been a problem and 2. There's nothing that looks physically different about my app since becoming an admin. There was also no mention of if they were working to solve this problem or any other solution. Just continually closing my help ticket. We're coming up on 6 months of this being an issue.

This is a great resource, but without use of a phone it is nearly impossible to use in addition to being extremely impractical. It's frustrating that I can't use it to the full extent given that I know my district pays a lot of money for the resource.
I honestly don't have any dislikes about TP but I would recommend maybe adding more languages so that we are able to speak to different families!
I think the only things I didn't like were the glitches, like how it loads the names. That was really the only thing. I think these kinds of issues can be easily solved with experience and feedback.
A few times it seems to glitch or be slow or out of service.
I have not found anything yet that I dislike and I have been using Talking Points for five years.
I have not found anything I dislike about TalkingPoints thus far, and that is saying a lot because us teachers always seem to complain!
There isn't anything to dislike! TalkingPoints is simply amazing!
I wish the communications could be forwarded to my actual phone number so I didn't have to log into another place to communicate and check messages.
Based on my use and needs, I have not encounter any situation where I thought to myself, "I wish the platform had this feature," but if I ever do, I'll be happy to share my thoughts and ideas.
It can be a bit confusing seeing new messages.  Sorting and filing could be better.
I haven't found anything yet. Everything is great.
Honestly, there isn't any thing that I haven't enjoyed about using it. My connection with families is much better.

name: ParentSquare
pros_raw: I love everything about ParentSquare! It has been a total game changer in our school district, opening up the doors of communication between families and staff. Their customer service is top-notch as well. The ParentSquare team is always looking for ways to innovate, making improvements that advance school communication and family collaboration, and they truly listen to our requests. As a district administrator, it was a seamless rollout and our families and staff absolutely love it!
Easiest way for the office staff and teachers to communicate with parents efficiently.
The ease to message multiple people at once without having to type in their email or deal with difficult email settings to make groups.
It was a super quick way to communicate with parents. I could check replies on my phone at home or wait until school to reply. More reliable than a student bringing a note in from home.

ParentSquare is a good way to keep a collection of communication.  Built in translations can be helpful to reach multiple populations.  Linking to an SIS to automatically populate groups to message is more helpful than inputting each email individually.

My whole site uses Parentsquare to communicate with our families. Each student and family member must sign up for Parentsquare at the beginning of the year. This ensures that teacher messages will be recieved. It is also easy to use, and I love that I can schedule posts in advance.

I like how ParentSquare handles mass communication and SIS integration.  They've made a lot of improvements to the interfaces as well as remediation of issues with students transferring between multiple districts using ParentSquare.  A lot of work has gone into making it more useable and user-friendly from the end user standpoint.
It is very user-friendly along with translation if parents need it.
I like how easy it is to access the contact information of students and all their family members, as well as how easy it is to send mass communication to families and classes.
cons_raw: That we didn't jump ship to ParentSquare sooner.
Sometimes the feed gets mixed up and takes me to look at older posts.

The translation feature needs more options for users that are bi or multilingual.  It is difficult to understand a message that was originally written in Spanish when translated into English.  I don't have the option of receiving my messages in the language it is written in because it forces me to choose one language only, and it would need to be English because we live in the US.  I'd like to receive my messages in the original language that is either English or Spanish rather than be locked to choosing English only.  The translation will never be great, no matter the program.  Many nuances in language change the meaning of messages, especially in education when parents are trying to communicate about their children.

My school now uses this as their primary form of communication. I hate that I can't save info for later use, like with emails. Sometimes when information is sent, I don't always have the time to read it right away. I missed out on things because they got lost in my "newsfeed" of communication.

ParentSquare doesn't save progress in drafts, so if you accidentally close or navigate away from the page, all work is lost.  Picture and video attachments are not streamlined and are difficult to ensure they are sent properly.  The amount of notifications can feel overwhelming to parents, which actually has them checking messages less frequently.  You aren't able to send messages to only a select group of parents.  You can send individual messages, but not groups, so email is still necessary in some cases.
I currently do not have any issues with Parentsquare. Before our site adopted it, I had to send emails through our grading system, which not everyone recieved.

Simple things, such as remediating student issues with the student registration emails, continues to be a little frustrating.  I have asked for an enhancement to put all the student management info and remediation issues in one spot, but it seems to still be all over the place.  For instance, I can edit student user info under each individual school, but can't work out of the district level from that standpoint.  Instead of going to the Admin tab and utilizing the Users section to remediate student email verifications in which the emails were rejected, I have to go to the full directory for the individual school, search for the exact name of the student and remediate registration issues from there...one at a time.  When there are only a few items, this isn't so bad...however, when there are a lot of new students, this is very time-consuming as there is also no way to do mass remediations of this type.
a couple downsides are that it may be confusing to differentiate posts with messages along with grouping.

I dislike how it looks to the recipient when it groups message chains together in an email. Not all parents only log in through parentsquare and the formatting through which the emails come through is out of order chronologically, making it easy to miss key information.
